| | Hey Reader, I got you a gift for you... Something to help protect you and your family while you’re on the road. And you’re going to want this, especially if you travel with your CCW. Because you don’t want to go to court with nothing but a story... you need solid, indisputable evidence...
**[Which is why I’m giving away 500 of my new Dash Eagle cameras at below wholesale cost](https://links.gunowner-news.com/a/1537/click/21647/6374453/97c4368830da34bbac169510c1447908ae46398f/f778767225339b880939fe6e37514092e415de91)** Every time you hit the road, you’ll have an HD dashboard camera to record everything that happens on the road, and inside your vehicle... So if you get in an accident, an altercation, or if you get pulled over by the police... You’ll be able to **record every second** with crystal clear video and audio... And you’ll never have to worry about not having any proof to back your case in court. It installs in just seconds and you can charge it in your car while simultaneously recording... But like I said... [We’re only giving away 500 Dash Eagles for 80% OFF...](https://links.gunowner-news.com/a/1537/click/21647/6374453/97c4368830da34bbac169510c1447908ae46398f/f778767225339b880939fe6e37514092e415de91) And it’s first come, first served... Oh yeah, did I mention that I’m giving you free shipping? I’m positive these won’t last long, so claim yours before they run out...

name: Michigan COVA
subject: Protect Michigan’s Elections — Read This Important Op-Ed
body:
The Citizen-Only Voting Movement Is Gaining Momentum

| | | | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/2dd96558f9b6/breaking-news-citizens-only-voting-amendment-submitted-to-board-of-canvassers-8370911?e=ecf60db114)
---
[|
---](https://vote.us6.list-manage.com/track/click?u=12ae025e5876fdceb812e1ab0&id=018372ca43&e=ecf60db114)
| | | | | [DONATE TODAY](https://vote.us6.list-manage.com/track/click?u=12ae025e5876fdceb812e1ab0&id=868fb49e4e&e=ecf60db114)
---
|
---
| 🔒 A Big Step Forward for Election Integrity in MichiganMichigan is at a turning point — and the fight to secure our elections just took a major step forward.
Former Secretary of State Ruth Johnson has penned a powerful op-ed in _The Detroit News_ outlining exactly why **Michigan needs a Citizen-Only Voting Amendment**. This is a must-read for every concerned voter who wants to protect the integrity of our elections and ensure that **only U.S. citizens are voting** in Michigan’s state and local contests.
👉 [**READ: “Michigan Needs a Citizen-Only Voting Amendment” by Ruth Johnson »**](https://vote.us6.list-manage.com/track/click?u=12ae025e5876fdceb812e1ab0&id=d453d7c1b4&e=ecf60db114)
As Senator Johnson outlines, while the law says only citizens can vote, **Michigan does not currently verify citizenship when people register.** That has to change.
The Citizen-Only Voting Amendment will:
* Require **proof of U.S. citizenship** to register
* Prevent non-citizens from voting in local elections
* Restore trust in the fairness and legitimacy of our elections

🚨 Momentum is building — and **you can be a part of it.** Share this op-ed, talk to your friends and neighbors, and stay connected with us as we push this common-sense amendment to the ballot in 2026.
**Let’s protect Michigan’s elections, together.**
---
|
---
| We are going to need our entire grassroots nation to get out and support this effort. Go to our website to sign up for updates, like and follow us on social media and help us spread the word. This issue will be critical to 2026 and we need you!
---

name: Team Cassidy
subject: We saved you a spot
body:
Right next to President Trump & Sen. Cassidy

| | | | [](https://e.firstlook.news/rd/9z2z3mj2f64j44gr7j58rj8vg0835a06tvmlbl15n2g_rp22sh2s8i6epb2c3hmcob1cdh2bs)
---
| | | | Friend, President Trump just signed Senator Cassidy's bill into law – and even thanked Senator Cassidy by name:
---
| | [](https://e.firstlook.news/rd/9z2zv4u8t65pdoqf08nf6k56hgfngalifqo506beu50_rp22sh2s8i6epb2c3hmcob1cdh2bs)
---
| | | [CLICK HERE TO WATCH THE VIDEO](https://e.firstlook.news/rd/9z2zjcrbn5ur42onoofqts0c8lbibv4tdm11gt6121o_rp22sh2s8i6epb2c3hmcob1cdh2bs)
---
| | Thanks to Senator Cassidy and President Trump, the HALT Fentanyl Act is now law – and America is one step closer to being FENTANYL-FREE! We’re sure you wanted to get an opportunity to thank Sen. Cassidy, too. So we saved you a spot on his thank you card, right next to President Trump’s name.
---
| | | [>> SIGN THE THANK YOU CARD >>](https://e.firstlook.news/rd/9z2zigr8ng6avm1gb6d1vto6um0bse78jg74frfui38_rp22sh2s8i6epb2c3hmcob1cdh2bs)
---
| | Together, we will make America FENTANYL-FREE! Team Cassidy
---
---
| | |
* * *
---
| | [](https://e.firstlook.news/rd/9z2zni9ce6g3dcrqarh9hufd8h1618rb6r42bv6r8c8_rp22sh2s8i6epb2c3hmcob1cdh2bs)
---
| | _Bill grew up in Baton Rouge, Louisiana, and is a proud LSU graduate. He worked in Louisiana’s charity hospital system for over 25 years, ensuring that the less fortunate in our state were able to receive quality health care. Later, Bill co-founded the Greater Baton Rouge Community Clinic, providing free dental and health care to working families without insurance. In the wake of Hurricane Katrina, Bill led a group of volunteers to convert an abandoned K-Mart building into an emergency healthcare facility._ _As a conservative in the Senate, he focuses on the issues Louisiana families care about the most. Bill is working to lower the cost of health care, make flood insurance affordable, and save Social Security from a 24% cut if Washington fails to act._ _Bill, and his wife, Laura, live in Baton Rouge and attend church at the Chapel on the Campus. They are blessed with three children and one grandchild._
---
| | | [LEARN MORE ABOUT BILL](https://e.firstlook.news/rd/9z2zb37dlu4stk8ia63rmrijec21upkiau0bgkpt7co_rp22sh2s8i6epb2c3hmcob1cdh2bs)

name: McKay for Senate
subject: Ronald Reagan warned us. It’s happening now.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by McKay for Senate Inc 19 Bakers Creek Rd, Warwick, RI, 02886
body:
I didn’t serve in the Cold War to sit back and watch THIS:

| | | [](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zk081bu2j1lod9gntmlae009dmq21v242um4k0ho_rp22sh2s8i6epb2c3hmcob1clh2bs)
---
_**Patriot**_ ,

**[🚨 I need you to chip in RIGHT NOW if you believe America is under attack from within. 🚨](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zhegh98565vtuo1b95nvg7b4d513ls6niqculer0_rp22sh2s8i6epb2c3hmcob1clh2bs)**
---
[ CHIP IN TODAY!
<CLICK HERE>](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zntqsovmi9jb2ineo0dbbc4n6el2ng9b7qgubuq8_rp22sh2s8i6epb2c3hmcob1clh2bs)
---
**I served during the Cold War.**

_**I stood face-to-face with the threat of communism.**_

Back then, it was in Moscow.

Now? It’s here. _**In our schools.** **In our government.** **At our border.**_

**The red threat is REAL** — _**and I’m running for the United States Senate to stop it.**_

This isn’t hyperbole. It’s history repeating itself.

They called it the Soviet Union in the '80s.

Now they hide it behind buzzwords like “ _**equity**_ ” “ _**climate justice**_ ” and “ _**reparations**_.”

It’s not progress.

It’s _**SOCIALISM**_ with a fresh coat of lipstick—and they’re ramming it down America’s throat.

But I’ve seen this story before. It ends in RUIN..

_**That’s why I’m launching the most unapologetically anti-communist campaign in the country—and I need you standing with me.**_
---

name: Chris Deluzio
subject: the largest single cut to public broadcasting in our country’s history
disclaimer_text: paid for by a politician or corporate overlords
body:
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111700916/558110127/1895761198?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjU0MyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3ZWM2NDAwMC1lNjYzLWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=HlQLrI95xS_501_WYQwMocwDKlKw0yRyIyrlWtxkU2Q=&emci=70c64000-e663-f011-8dc9-6045bdfe8e9c&emdi=7ec64000-e663-f011-8dc9-6045bdfe8e9c&ceid=13185894) _**Reuters: US House Republicans pass Trump plan to cut foreign aid, public broadcasting**_ The House just approved Trump’s cuts to public broadcasting. If this becomes law, that would mean devastating cuts to NPR, PBS, and thousands of public radio and television stations across the country. We’re talking about Sesame Street and Daniel Tiger, for crying out loud. _It’s the single largest cut to public broadcasting in U.S. history._ Why? Because Trump doesn’t like what they report. He’s called NPR and PBS “radical left monsters” and claimed they’re biased—so now he’s trying to silence them. **Derek, our democracy depends on a free press—not one bought and paid for by a politician or corporate overlords.** That’s why Trump’s attacks on our public media are so dangerous, and why I’m fighting back. [**Will you stand with me to protect our public media? Pitch in now to help me stand up to Trump and Republicans—and keep fighting for Western Pennsylvanians, the public media we rely on, and our democracy →**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111700917/558110128/-898016266?refcode=email_071825_media&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500,1000&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjU0MyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3ZWM2NDAwMC1lNjYzLWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=HlQLrI95xS_501_WYQwMocwDKlKw0yRyIyrlWtxkU2Q=) | | [Donate →](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111700918/558110129/-898016266?refcode=email_071825_media&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500,1000&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjU0MyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3ZWM2NDAwMC1lNjYzLWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=HlQLrI95xS_501_WYQwMocwDKlKw0yRyIyrlWtxkU2Q=)
---
If this bill becomes law, it would be a major blow for public media that millions of Americans rely on for news and information.
Here in Pennsylvania, at least 20 stations could lose funding—devastating especially our rural communities.
Derek, I won’t stand by while Trump tries to gut funding for critical programs that millions of Americans depend on for information.

clean_body: Reuters: US House Republicans pass Trump plan to cut foreign aid, public broadcasting The House just approved Trump’s cuts to public broadcasting. If this becomes law, that would mean devastating cuts to NPR, PBS, and thousands of public radio and television stations across the country. We’re talking about Sesame Street and Daniel Tiger, for crying out loud. It’s the single largest cut to public broadcasting in U.S. history. Why? Because Trump doesn’t like what they report. He’s called NPR and PBS “radical left monsters” and claimed they’re biased—so now he’s trying to silence them. Derek, our democracy depends on a free press—not one bought and paid for by a politician or corporate overlords. That’s why Trump’s attacks on our public media are so dangerous, and why I’m fighting back. = | | = If this bill becomes law, it would be a major blow for public media that millions of Americans rely on for news and information. Here in Pennsylvania, at least 20 stations could lose funding—devastating especially our rural communities. name: Wes Moore
subject: MBOA
body:
We fixed it.
# [](https://act.wesmoore.com/go/8?t=1&akid=325%2E1086043%2Elum1VL)
Peter, this is a story about a website.
It’s a little long, so if you don’t have the time to read on – but want to support our campaign today – **[you can donate right here.](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/wes-em-20250718-1?refcode=em-20250718-1&refcode2=325_1086043_lum1VL&akid=325%2E1086043%2Elum1VL)**
For so many Marylanders out there, the status quo is simply not cutting it. They have found themselves working and existing in a system that was never built for their success, or for their families to get the support they needed.
To get help, they've had to run all across our state. Filling out paperwork, for agency after agency, reminding us about their situation again and again. I mean, it’s almost like the system was designed the way it is because they didn’t want people actually getting benefits.
At a time when the President of the United States chose to take a sledgehammer to basic human services and Congress passed a bill that severs the relationship between the federal government, the states, and the people — **in Maryland, we are here to say: No more.**
So many families are working hard and playing by the rules just trying to get by. And it is time to make the system work for them.
That is why this week I announced Marylanders can go to a website online and enroll in Medicaid, SNAP, WIC, TANF, energy assistance and emergency assistance, all at the same time and the same place.
It’s called the **Maryland Benefits One Application,** and it’s a smarter, people-centered approach for the people of Maryland.
We cut the average application time down by nearly a full hour. It means fewer forms, fewer trips, faster service, and more support for families when they need it most.
It ensures that all Marylanders can access the supports they are entitled to.
That’s what I mean when I say ours is a state where we Leave No One Behind. Yes, these times are difficult. But we’ve seen difficult before.
We are promise keepers, and we are meeting this moment.
So this story is about a website, but it’s also about keeping a promise to everyone who believes in our movement and our ability to make change.
Peter, it’s thanks to folks like you who believe in our mission that we are able to make progress like this for Maryland families.
**[If you’re with me in the fight to leave no one behind, will you support our campaign with a donation of $5 or any amount today?](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/wes-em-20250718-1?refcode=em-20250718-1&refcode2=325_1086043_lum1VL&akid=325%2E1086043%2Elum1VL)**
**[Donate $5 ››](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/wes-em-20250718-1?amount=5&refcode=em-20250718-1&refcode2=325_1086043_lum1VL&akid=325%2E1086043%2Elum1VL)**
Thanks for everything you do.
Elevate,
Wes
[](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/wes-em-20250718-1?refcode=em-20250718-1&refcode2=325_1086043_lum1VL&akid=325%2E1086043%2Elum1VL)
* * *
**[Donate »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/wes-em-footer?refcode=em-20250718-1&t=2&refcode2=325_1086043_lum1VL&akid=325%2E1086043%2Elum1VL)**
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with grassroots supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.wesmoore.com/signup/receive-fewer-emails/?t=3&akid=325%2E1086043%2Elum1VL)
* [Update Your Information](https://act.wesmoore.com/signup/update-your-information/)
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.wesmoore.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=4&akid=325%2E1086043%2Elum1VL)

Email us: [info@wesmoore.com](mailto:info@wesmoore.com)
BY AUTHORITY: WES MOORE FOR MARYLAND, MARY TYDINGS, TREASURER

|

**SOUTH DAKOTA** RINO US Senator Mike Rounds of South Dakota was one of a few Republicans to publicly oppose President Trump's $9 billion rescissions package that would defund NPR and PBS. The **South Dakota Freedom Caucus** was having none of that, so they publicly told Rounds to listen to the voters who are demanding that he CUT THE SPENDING.Not long after their statement went out, Rounds reversed course and stated that he would support the bill. Since then, the Senate and the House have passed the legislation. It is now headed to President Trump's desk for his signature!

**GEORGIA** The **Georgia Freedom Caucus** is providing no oxygen for statewide "Republican" officials when they defend woke policies. This time it's RINO Attorney General Chris Carr, who has been talking out of both sides of his mouth.
---
[](http://give.statefreedomcaucus.org/ls/click?upn=u001.SSCAglCEkhp55PDWKhWTjja-2FX-2BHiJMkQeHavsUeprX-2Bmb6rZOv5CYPNdPI-2Bv5JW87iOLozOplhzOF3kWNgckLzIakZc-2FTV055VMZ8rf74z0-3D9G_W_9XnTPLnaEjN9OHWhKYUffDeECIVzR9M-2B5LaO5RH8NVBZyx1SQGGYhOdwcjZYtHerpyvhhcBiVRxj4knFrjrdM5tA-2BREn6cGQlsjnI4rN6kGxnRpFWa3VDdvgh1UKpAn3OlqaVTvR-2BaK62gh4Z9XQvtkQ3YLh3nxAy0Yb3BcW7MQJaYe1XPnkFjK9jnHIHzfWFy1uGZsdo4W-2F6VIf60Xzxx9Cv2hSMKV4yDXjfvGO0mIjfYExq5GCn5l-2FK7I0uuvuKMt7sOE39fAdTwWLwPumRy-2B-2B7T63uSQ-2BImJPYEC0jtk-3D)
---
|

**CONGRESS** This past week was Crypto Week on Capitol Hill. The **House Freedom Caucus** was supportive of the pro-growth crypto bills that were being considered, but with one caveat - the legislation didn't explicitly ban, in all forms, a Central Bank Digital Currency (CBDC). CBDCs are a form of financial slavery. If one were enacted, the government could use digital money to monitor your transactions and even censor them. Want to buy a gun? The government could prevent that with a CBDC. Want to buy a subscription to The Federalist? Nope. Radical leftists like Senator Elizabeth Warren desperately want a CBDC, so it's imperative that we codify into law a complete ban on CBDCs. So the HFC negotiated a deal with the White House to ban CBDCs in the upcoming NDAA bill that will be considered later this fall.We are mentioning this, not just to applaud our brothers and sisters in the House Freedom Caucus, but to remind you that our State Freedom Caucuses have led the way on this fight as well. In previous years, there have been several attempts in multiple states to enact a framework that would enable CBDCs at the state-level. Our **South Dakota Freedom Caucus** was the first to sound the alarm when they convinced then-Governor Kristi Noem to veto the legislation. Our **Arizona Freedom Caucus** and our **Montana Freedom Caucus** , just to name a couple - killed the legislation as well. Because CBDCs are so insidious and dangerous, it requires a concerted federal-state joint event to stop them. And our freedom caucuses have led the way. This is a perfect example that proves the point made by President Calvin Coolidge who said, "It is much more important to kill bad bills than to pass good ones."
---
[](http://give.statefreedomcaucus.org/ls/click?upn=u001.SSCAglCEkhp55PDWKhWTjkHmdqxONWixpsSVbyA8x2Glo0a2Wgv14GZGilHJouToejror87f-2BQNiOewiZbop6mitr8oP-2BCoh9tNFfbvAFMQ-3DII5O_9XnTPLnaEjN9OHWhKYUffDeECIVzR9M-2B5LaO5RH8NVBZyx1SQGGYhOdwcjZYtHerpyvhhcBiVRxj4knFrjrdM5tA-2BREn6cGQlsjnI4rN6kGxnRpFWa3VDdvgh1UKpAn3OlqaVTvR-2BaK62gh4Z9XQvpVJr5aC-2Bb8uNVdpCBRO4Xxy4gIlTRkOK5oj2uVT-2BTuffQ7TMlxiSm3HdnypnH0NVLQfyAMGIziZQoyVNPSfVCvOe5gQj27Pd5bGjIWFLFOudkO3LTkNLeJq4OAlPuIKXyKvVHa36RY-2Bk2looO4uAsE-3D)
---
|
---
**WYOMING** A leftist judge has issued an injunction against the school choice bill sponsored and passed by the **Wyoming Freedom Caucus** earlier this year. Thankfully, the state's attorney general has appealed the decision, which means the case will now go before the Wyoming Supreme Court. The Wyoming Freedom Caucus's statement is a must-read:

[](http://give.statefreedomcaucus.org/ls/click?upn=u001.SSCAglCEkhp55PDWKhWTjppHen015Gx6AaFY6rk-2BtVm1EbG9EFRY83Mo-2Fm5ndzB3iuvnzPMoXCm59cdjh-2FVkqnLWEmnaAfiELj1bAgl6Al8-3DbFiX_9XnTPLnaEjN9OHWhKYUffDeECIVzR9M-2B5LaO5RH8NVBZyx1SQGGYhOdwcjZYtHerpyvhhcBiVRxj4knFrjrdM5tA-2BREn6cGQlsjnI4rN6kGxnRpFWa3VDdvgh1UKpAn3OlqaVTvR-2BaK62gh4Z9XQvgHIdhLkqzYMb1zaB7kqPU6RzuiS-2Fy5feOrs1abUc6b-2BUHxY5vaqsQQf42cKD6zz8-2BUHaFlL3yFX4IWjvA9s-2BgceC52hVOV-2BPlpu-2B7giW9rXwWqTwe-2FxpCRHiNDpPjfVq9QFxY5ms4l7BAi2QZR1FXc-3D)

**HELP US GROW** This summer is critical for laying the groundwork for our expansion into new states and keeping our current states strong.

But we can’t do it without you.

Grassroots donors like you keep us going strong. Every dollar counts—especially now.

Can we count on your support today?

Together, we’ll turn up the heat—and the momentum.

name: Lizzie Fletcher
subject: The GOP plan to redraw Texas’ Congressional map
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY ELIZABETH PANNILL FLETCHER FOR CONGRESS
body:
ELIZABETH PANNILL FLETCHER FOR CONGRESS [https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP10/1/82676/images/Non-Re-Elect%20Logo.png]
As you’ve no doubt heard, Governor Greg Abbott is calling the Texas Legislature back in session, and he has prioritized redrawing Texas’ congressional map.
At a time when Texans are in the middle of an ongoing tragedy in the Texas Hill Country, Governor Abbott and Texas Republicans are preparing to save their own power when they should be preparing to save Texans from the next natural disaster.
Governor Abbott and Texas Republicans have utterly collapsed to pressure from President Trump, and they have surrendered Texas to political operatives from Washington, trading and dividing their constituents like commodities. They are prioritizing helping out-of-state political operatives abuse the voting rights and distort the political will of Texans to eliminate seats now held by Democratic representatives so that national Republicans can try to hold on to the majority of seats in the U.S. House of Representatives.
This effort is not about any one member or any one district—it is an attack on the citizens of Texas, the voters of Texas and their right and their ability to elect people to represent them in Washington. This effort threatens the very principle that people deserve to have their say in our democratic system. In Texas, Republican partisan gerrymandering is racial gerrymandering. And Texans already suffer from one of the most racially gerrymandered congressional maps in the country.
Texas is a cautionary tale of what’s to come when an out-of-control President and administration demand loyalty to them rather than to the people and places that Republicans have been elected to represent.
The House of Representatives hangs in the balance. In 2022, the House majority was decided by five seats. Last year, it was just three seats. When the election results are this close, it’s easy to see how Republicans stealing three or four seats in Texas could tip control of the Congressional majority.
Together, we will fight this redistricting effort. We will fight it in the halls of Congress. We will fight it in the courts. And we will fight it in our communities across Texas.
And we will have friends in the fight with us—from now to next November and beyond. I am working closely with partners to make sure that we bring this fight to ballot boxes across Texas. But this fight is about more than the midterms, more than the map, it is about making real the founding promise of this country—that we, the people, all the people, not just the privileged few—have the right and the ability to have a say in our government.
FIGHT BACK [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/lpfe250718?refcode=lpfe250718]
You are a critical part of this effort. We will share more opportunities to make your voice heard on this issue in the coming days. If you want to help today, please consider making a contribution that we can use to fight back. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/lpfe250718?refcode=lpfe250718]
Together, we can do anything,

clean_body: ELIZABETH PANNILL FLETCHER FOR CONGRESS [] As you’ve no doubt heard, Governor Greg Abbott is calling the Texas Legislature back in session, and he has prioritized redrawing Texas’ congressional map. At a time when Texans are in the middle of an ongoing tragedy in the Texas Hill Country, Governor Abbott and Texas Republicans are preparing to save their own power when they should be preparing to save Texans from the next natural disaster. Governor Abbott and Texas Republicans have utterly collapsed to pressure from President Trump, and they have surrendered Texas to political operatives from Washington, trading and dividing their constituents like commodities. They are prioritizing helping out-of-state political operatives abuse the voting rights and distort the political will of Texans to eliminate seats now held by Democratic representatives so that national Republicans can try to hold on to the majority of seats in the U.S. House of Representatives. This effort is not about any one member or any one district—it is an attack on the citizens of Texas, the voters of Texas and their right and their ability to elect people to represent them in Washington. This effort threatens the very principle that people deserve to have their say in our democratic system. In Texas, Republican partisan gerrymandering is racial gerrymandering. And Texans already suffer from one of the most racially gerrymandered congressional maps in the country. Texas is a cautionary tale of what’s to come when an out-of-control President and administration demand loyalty to them rather than to the people and places that Republicans have been elected to represent. The House of Representatives hangs in the balance. In 2022, the House majority was decided by five seats. Last year, it was just three seats. When the election results are this close, it’s easy to see how Republicans stealing three or four seats in Texas could tip control of the Congressional majority. Together, we will fight this redistricting effort. We will fight it in the halls of Congress. We will fight it in the courts. And we will fight it in our communities across Texas. body:
https://alexkelloff.com/ [https://alexkelloff.com/]
Hi everyone,
Newsweek just dropped a list of “Republicans vulnerable to losing their seats after voting yes on Trump bill” and guess who is on it?
Jeff Hurd.
We can’t say we’re shocked. Especially when you consider that CO-03 has the highest rate of Medicaid recipients in the state—a clear sign that healthcare matters here. That didn’t stop Hurd from voting for Trump’s reckless budget that will kick our families off their health care plans.
And for what? To further line the pockets of billionaire donors and massive corporations.
Thankfully, we only have to put up with Hurd until November 2026, because after that, we’re flipping this district Blue and kicking Hurd to the curb. If you’re all in on flipping the House next November, rush a donation right now >> [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_agk_email-25q3?refcode=X_250718_redtoblue_act]

Hurd’s bad for business, bad for our health, and operates in bad faith—it’s time we flip the odds back to fair.
We deserve someone who gives a damn instead of hurting our families. Join us if you’re ready to take back Colorado’s Third. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_agk_email-25q3?refcode=X_250718_redtoblue_act]

Alex Kelloff is an accomplished entrepreneur, financial executive, and committed community advocate whose family’s Colorado heritage dates back to 1897.
His family has operated community-oriented businesses across Colorado—including Kelloff’s Food Stores and Kelloff’s Movie Manor—for over a century. He took that entrepreneurial spirit and became a seasoned financial executive at Jefferies and SBC Capital, focused on expanding IT infrastructure and high-speed internet to rural America, and a dedicated community advocate. Then he co-founded Armada Skis, one of the most innovative brands in the industry and on the slopes.
Now, Alex is running in one of the most competitive seats in the country.
Inspired by his father, Dr. Gary Kelloff, who dedicated decades to cancer research at the National Institutes of Health, he brings that same deep sense of purpose to this campaign.
In Congress, he’ll fight to create jobs, lower costs, preserve our public lands and water rights, protect our seniors, and defend our democratic institutions. He’ll listen to the people back home—and make sure they come first.

[] If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: DONATE $5 [] DONATE $10 [] DONATE $25 [] DONATE $50 [] DONATE $100 [] OTHER [] – Kelloff HQ [] [] Alex Kelloff is an accomplished entrepreneur, financial executive, and committed community advocate whose family’s Colorado heritage dates back to 1897. His family has operated community-oriented businesses across Colorado—including Kelloff’s Food Stores and Kelloff’s Movie Manor—for over a century. He took that entrepreneurial spirit and became a seasoned financial executive at Jefferies and SBC Capital, focused on expanding IT infrastructure and high-speed internet to rural America, and a dedicated community advocate. Then he co-founded Armada Skis, one of the most innovative brands in the industry and on the slopes. Now, Alex is running in one of the most competitive seats in the country. Inspired by his father, Dr. Gary Kelloff, who dedicated decades to cancer research at the National Institutes of Health, he brings that same deep sense of purpose to this campaign. In Congress, he’ll fight to create jobs, lower costs, preserve our public lands and water rights, protect our seniors, and defend our democratic institutions. He’ll listen to the people back home—and make sure they come first. Help him flip our Colorado 3rd by chipping in now. [] DONATE TODAY [] DONATE MONTHLY [] [] [] [] [] Alex Kelloff for Colorado P.O. body:

| Dear Friend, Let’s be clear: the crisis at our southern border didn’t happen overnight. Joe Biden’s open-border agenda — from halting wall construction to ending Remain in Mexico — unleashed chaos we’re still dealing with today. Millions of illegal crossings. Deadly fentanyl in our communities. Cartels emboldened. Families living in fear. President Trump had it right all along: [**Secure the border. Enforce the law. Put America First.**](http://link.finstadforcongress.com/api/LinkHandler/getaction2?%3d) That’s why I’ve proudly stood with President Trump in Congress — pushing to finish the wall, give Border Patrol the tools they need, and block taxpayer-funded benefits for illegal immigrants. But Democrats haven’t learned their lesson and now they’re targeting conservatives like me to try and regain power. Let’s not let that happen.

name: Andy Kim
subject: Republicans voted to cut NPR and PBS
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Andy Kim for New Jersey
body:
From PBS NewsHour to Sesame Street to Mr. Rogers, these programs aren’t partisan.
| | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111702630/558115597/41798106?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84Nzk3MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyYTM0MmRmMy1lNzYzLWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=n7MIBpB3p9Z1GmilTCGJ0U1edTcxfByXK5y2F2jEqlg=&emci=a962fb7f-d363-f011-8dc9-6045bdfe8e9c&emdi=2a342df3-e763-f011-8dc9-6045bdfe8e9c&ceid=26261389)
---

| | Yesterday, Republicans rammed through a bill that claws back billions in _already-approved_ federal funding – including devastating cuts to public broadcasting and foreign aid. This bill is a direct attack on NPR, PBS, their member stations, and the public journalism that millions of Americans rely on every day for fact-based news, emergency weather alerts, local reporting, and free, accessible educational programming for all ages. Public broadcasting has enriched generations of Americans. From PBS NewsHour to Sesame Street to Mr. Rogers, **these programs aren't partisan.** Donald Trump demanded these cuts. Now, the funding Congress already agreed on – funding for trusted news, for global democracy, for humanitarian assistance – has been ripped away. This is Trump's playbook in action: dismantle the institutions that inform and connect us, silence independent voices, and further divide Americans. I won't stop fighting to restore this critical funding. We've got a lot to defend, and we need a Congress, and a country – that values truth, transparency, and the public good. More soon, _Andy_ | Express Donate: If you've stored your info with ActBlue Express, we'll process your contribution to Andy Kim instantly:

name: Team KTR
subject: Pinetop-Lakeside Mayor Stephanie Irwin Endorses KTR for Governor
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY KARRIN FOR ARIZONA
body:
Growing coalition of local leaders are backing Karrin Taylor Robson for Governor

| | | | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/karrinforarizona/irwin-endorsement?e=ebfb0fda52)
---
[|
---](https://karrinforarizona.us22.list-manage.com/track/click?u=473c144f5f100c2eff990c077&id=b8a257838b&e=ebfb0fda52)
| | | | | **FOR IMMEDIATE RELEASE** July 18th, 2025
**Pinetop-Lakeside Mayor Stephanie Irwin Endorses Karrin Taylor Robson for Governor** _Growing coalition of local leaders are backing Karrin Taylor Robson for Governor_
**PHOENIX, AZ** — Karrin Taylor Robson, Trump-endorsed conservative candidate for Governor of Arizona, today announced an endorsement from Pinetop-Lakeside Mayor Stephanie Irwin. Mayor Irwin joins Yuma Mayor Doug Nicholls, Buckeye Mayor Eric Orsborn, and Queen Creek Mayor Julia Wheatley in endorsing Karrin Taylor Robson for Governor of Arizona.
“Karrin Taylor Robson is a proven leader who delivers results,” said **Mayor Stephanie Irwin**. “Her background as a successful business executive gives her the experience and discipline to Make Arizona Strong Again. Karrin understands the needs of our small towns, supports rural economies, and will bring common sense back to our state government. I’m proud to support her for Governor.”
Mayor Irwin has lived in Pinetop-Lakeside since 1982 and runs a successful CPA practice in town. She played a key role in the town’s incorporation and has remained active in community affairs, from serving on the Planning and Zoning Commission to leading local nonprofit and faith-based initiatives.
**Karrin Taylor Robson** welcomed the endorsement, saying:
“I’m honored to have Mayor Irwin's support and look forward to working with local leaders like her to strengthen our economy, fix our housing affordability crisis, and stand up for rural Arizona.”
This endorsement adds to the growing coalition of mayors, sheriffs, and law enforcement organizations backing Taylor Robson’s campaign for Governor of Arizona.
###
---
|
---
|
---

clean_body: Growing coalition of local leaders are backing Karrin Taylor Robson for Governor | | | | | | | | | | | FOR IMMEDIATE RELEASE July 18th, 2025 Pinetop-Lakeside Mayor Stephanie Irwin Endorses Karrin Taylor Robson for Governor Growing coalition of local leaders are backing Karrin Taylor Robson for Governor PHOENIX, AZ — Karrin Taylor Robson, Trump-endorsed conservative candidate for Governor of Arizona, today announced an endorsement from Pinetop-Lakeside Mayor Stephanie Irwin. Mayor Irwin joins Yuma Mayor Doug Nicholls, Buckeye Mayor Eric Orsborn, and Queen Creek Mayor Julia Wheatley in endorsing Karrin Taylor Robson for Governor of Arizona. “Karrin Taylor Robson is a proven leader who delivers results,” said Mayor Stephanie Irwin. “Her background as a successful business executive gives her the experience and discipline to Make Arizona Strong Again. Karrin understands the needs of our small towns, supports rural economies, and will bring common sense back to our state government. I’m proud to support her for Governor.” Mayor Irwin has lived in Pinetop-Lakeside since 1982 and runs a successful CPA practice in town. She played a key role in the town’s incorporation and has remained active in community affairs, from serving on the Planning and Zoning Commission to leading local nonprofit and faith-based initiatives. Karrin Taylor Robson welcomed the endorsement, saying: “I’m honored to have Mayor Irwin's support and look forward to working with local leaders like her to strengthen our economy, fix our housing affordability crisis, and stand up for rural Arizona.” This endorsement adds to the growing coalition of mayors, sheriffs, and law enforcement organizations backing Taylor Robson’s campaign for Governor of Arizona. | | | PAID FOR BY KARRIN FOR ARIZONA. AUTHORIZED BY KARRIN TAYLOR ROBSON. | | | | | | | | | | | | | Want to change how you receive these emails? You can or |

Peter, it’s officially been more than six months since I was sworn into office, and there’s been no shortage of challenges along the way.

Throughout the highs and the lows, hearing directly from New Yorkers on what matters most to them is what renews my commitment and energy to fight for our community and continue working to build a better future.

That’s why I wanted to reach out today. I want to know what’s on your mind and how I can best serve you. name: Senator Lashrecse Aird
subject: 2025 Summer Newsletter
disclaimer_text: Paid for and authorized by Friends of Lashrecse Aird
body:
logo [https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP21/1/89018/images/Newsletter/Screen%20Shot%202023-11-12%20at%207.15.37%20PM.png]
2025 Summer Newsletter
July 1, of each year marks the culmination of work completed by the General Assembly with all new laws going into effect. Our 2025 legislative agenda and now official laws reflect health and maternal care supports, housing protections, public education investments and economic development incentives. Further, I am proud to have partnered with my colleagues on legislation that also focused on social media protections for minors ( SB854 [https://lis.virginia.gov/bill-details/20251/SB854] ), establishing penalties for the use of drones over defense facilities ( SB757 [https://lis.virginia.gov/bill-details/20251/SB757] ) and requiring the development of guidelines on policies relating to extreme heat safety and protection for student-athletes ( SB1104 [https://lis.virginia.gov/bill-details/20251/SB1104] ).
https://lis.virginia.gov/bill-search/?q=eyJzZWxlY3RlZFBhdHJvbiI6NTA5LCJzZWxlY3RlZFNlc3Npb24iOiIyMDI1MSIsImNoYW1iZXJDb2RlIjoiUyIsInNlbGVjdGVkUGF0cm9uVHlwZSI6WzFdfQ== [https://lis.virginia.gov/bill-search/?q=eyJzZWxlY3RlZFBhdHJvbiI6NTA5LCJzZWxlY3RlZFNlc3Npb24iOiIyMDI1MSIsImNoYW1iZXJDb2RlIjoiUyIsInNlbGVjdGVkUGF0cm9uVHlwZSI6WzFdfQ==]
There are hundreds of other bills that also are now in effect. The Division of Legislative Services offers an in-depth annual publication, In Due Course, [https://dls.virginia.gov/pubs_idc.html] that is designed to provide a brief summary of legislation passed by the General Assembly and likely to affect your daily life. Please review it here. [https://dls.virginia.gov/pubs_idc.html]
https://www.facebook.com/airdcampaign [https://www.facebook.com/airdcampaign] https://twitter.com/lashrecseaird [https://twitter.com/lashrecseaird] https://www.instagram.com/lashrecseaird/ [https://www.instagram.com/lashrecseaird/]
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP21/1/89018/images/Newsletter/Untitled%20design%20%2830%29.png] 2025 Legislative Recap
As your Senator, I’ve continued to prioritize legislation that delivers tangible results for working families, protects the vulnerable, and positions our region for sustainable growth. Here are a few highlights from our legislative agenda:
👩⚕️ Health & Maternal Care
With maternal health outcomes in crisis, especially for Black women, I introduced and passed legislation to support safer, more equitable care:
SB1384 [https://lis.virginia.gov/bill-details/20251/SB1384] ensures that doulas — who play a critical role in lowering maternal mortality — are allowed to remain with mothers during labor and delivery, without barriers or unnecessary restrictions.
SB1418 [https://lis.virginia.gov/bill-details/20251/SB1418] expands Medicaid coverage to include two additional postpartum doula visits, providing much-needed emotional, physical, and educational support to new moms during the most vulnerable weeks after childbirth.
SB1383 [https://lis.virginia.gov/bill-details/20251/SB1383] empowers the Health Commissioner with stronger tools to hold long-term care and nursing home facilities accountable, ensuring families can trust that their loved ones are receiving the care they deserve.
🏠 Housing Protections
Housing stability is foundational, and I remain focused on protecting tenants and improving living conditions across our region:
SB1267 [https://lis.virginia.gov/bill-details/20251/SB1267] increases civil penalties for landlords who repeatedly violate local zoning codes in multifamily properties — helping deter unsafe or exploitative housing practices.
SB1221 [https://lis.virginia.gov/bill-details/20251/SB1221] strengthens tenant protections in public housing, addressing issues like rent miscalculations, eviction process transparency, and improper maintenance charges.
📚 Public Education Investment
Our children and educators deserve a well-supported learning environment:
SB1230 [https://lis.virginia.gov/bill-details/20251/SB1230] extends the time long-term substitute teachers may serve from 90 to 180 days. This prevents disruption and allows schools to maintain consistency when facing staffing shortages.
SB1236 [https://lis.virginia.gov/bill-details/20251/SB1236] sought to remove the outdated cap on state funding for school support staff, and the policy was incorporated into the final budget — delivering $223 million statewide , including more than $16 million for school divisions in SD-13 to hire reading specialists, nurses, counselors, and other essential positions.
💼 Economic Development
To strengthen Virginia’s economy, SB1231 [https://lis.virginia.gov/bill-details/20251/SB1231] expands the Virginia Investment Performance Grant Program to better support advanced manufacturing and research-based industries. These targeted investments help create high-quality jobs and build long-term opportunity, especially in historically underserved areas.
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP21/1/89018/images/Newsletter/Untitled%20design%20%2830%29.png] Anything but...
One Big Beautiful Bill Act
Passed by Congressional Republicans and signed by President Donanld Trump is the massive spending bill, One Big Beautiful Bill Act [https://www.congress.gov/bill/119th-congress/house-bill/1/text] Here are some key facts:
According to the Congressional Budget Office and the staff of the Joint Committee on Taxation estimate that enacting H.R. 1, as passed by the Senate on July 1, 2025, would increase the deficit by $3.4 trillion.
While the legislation would make most of the 2017 tax cuts for the wealthy permanent, increasing spending for border security, defense and energy production; it does so through cuts to health care coverage, food programs like SNAP benefits, decreasing the child tax credit and much more.
Specifically throughout Senate District 13, with our median household income ranging around $66,000 and a 13.4% poverty rate, Medicaid cuts as proposed will cause a spike in our uninsured rates and increase demands on health clinics. As a district with significant rural communities, those depending on rural hospitals are at risk of fewer healthcare options as those hospitals now face the threat of closure.
Further, the legislation reverses SNAP administrative exemptions for veterans, people unhoused and youth aging out of foster care [https://www.americanprogress.org/article/10-egregious-things-you-may-not-know-about-the-one-big-beautiful-bill-act/] , which will lead to more families being disqualified for food assistance and becoming unable to put food on their table.
In Virginia we have been preparing for the possibility of this bill’s passage and passed changes that allow us the ability to reconvene when necessary to take up serious matters to our budget such as the passage of the reconciliation budget. This will allow us to assess the Commonwealth’s resources and put in place supports and resources that are critically needed by individuals and families. Find out more about this legislation here. [https://www.progressivecaucuscenter.org/unrig-the-rules/2025-07-01]
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP21/1/89018/images/Newsletter/Untitled%20design%20%2831%29.png] Our 2025 Democratic Nominees
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP21/1/89018/images/2025%20Democratic%20Nominees%20Graphic.jpg]
With the primary season well behind us, I am excited to support our Democratic statewide ticket, Abigail Spanberger [https://abigailspanberger.com/] for Governor, Ghazala Hashmi [https://ghazalaforvirginia.com/] for Lieutenant Governor and Jay Jones [https://jayjones.com/] for Attorney General. The decisions made by our statewide leaders are more important than ever before. Visit their websites linked above to learn more about their policy plans and vision for our Commonwealth. As a reminder:
* The General Election is November 4, 2025
* The deadline to register to vote is October 24, 2025
* Mailed ballot request may be made until October 24
* Early in person voting occurs September 19 to November 1, 2025.
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP21/1/89018/images/Newsletter/Untitled%20design%20%2830%29.png] Joint Boards & Commissions
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP21/1/89018/images/IMG_0971.JPG]
Meeting of the Joint Commission on Technology and Science
When the General Assembly ends its legislative session, the work of our interim boards and commissions begins. I’ll spend my time this summer and fall serving on the following commissions listed below. Each commission's meetings are open to the public with the schedule and agendas accessible online. [https://studies.virginiageneralassembly.gov/studies]
* Tobacco Region Revitalization Commission (Vice Chair)
* Joint Commission on Technology and Science
* Behavior Health Commission
* Fentanyl and Heroin Task Force
* Newly Appointed, Commission on Women’s Health [https://studies.virginiageneralassembly.gov/studies/775]
* Newly Appointed, Joint Commission to Oversee the Transition of the Commonwealth into a Cannabis Retail Market [https://studies.virginiageneralassembly.gov/studies/774]
* Newly Appointed, Blockchain Advisory Committee [https://jcots.notion.site/blockchain-advisory-committee-members]
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP21/1/89018/images/LA%20Graphics%20_20250422_192425_0000.png]
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP21/1/89018/images/Newsletter/Untitled%20design%20%2831%29.png] Around the District
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP21/1/89018/images/2025/2026%20July%20Newsletter/Around%20the%20District%20-%20July%20Newsletter%20%281%29.png]
* Official Welcome of Bleachtech in Petersburg - It was exciting to officially welcome the ANSA McAL Group to Petersburg as a result of their acquisition of BLEACHTECH LLC. Not only is this the largest acquisition in the Caribbean conglomerate’s history but it represents another strong manufacturing partner for the City. It is clear that this family owned company is not only committed to their industry but to investing in the people in our community. A special thank you to Mr. A. Norman Sabga, Mr. Anthony Sabga III and the entire executive team for their vision and leadership shown through this project.

* National Guard Aviation Support Facility Groundbreaking (Sandston) - We broke ground this summer on a new Virginia National Guard Aviation Support Facility in Sandston — a long-awaited milestone made possible through General Assembly investments beginning in 2020. This state-of-the-art facility will serve as a critical hub for our citizen soldiers, ensuring they are prepared to respond quickly and effectively when called to serve, both here in the Commonwealth and beyond.

* Tindall Corporation Tour (Dinwiddie County) - It was a privilege to tour Tindall Corporation, a family-owned concrete manufacturing company that has operated in Dinwiddie since 1988. Their commitment to hiring and training workers at every skill level stood out — turning entry-level jobs into careers with dignity and growth potential. Seeing their process up close was a reminder of the skilled labor powering our local economy and the need for continued state support of industrial workforce development.

* Presenting Commending Resolution to First Baptist Church of Petersburg - First Baptist Church in Petersburg — one of the oldest Black churches in America — has stood as a pillar of faith and justice since 1756 . This year, I was proud to carry Senate Resolution 284 commemorating their 250th anniversary , officially enshrining their legacy into Virginia’s historical record. Presenting that resolution in person was a deeply meaningful moment and a celebration of centuries of resilience and leadership.
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP21/1/89018/images/IMG_1606%20%281%29.jpg]
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP21/1/89018/images/Newsletter/Untitled%20design%20%2833%29.png] 2026 Senate Page Program Applications Open!
Since 1850, 40 young leaders were selected to serve as Senate Pages in the upper chamber of the legislature during the General Assembly Session.
The mission of the Senate Page Program is to facilitate a structured environment in which young Virginians accept responsibility and accountability, develop professional characteristics through strict standards of conduct, and engage in the legislative process through work, observation, and discussion.
In keeping with tradition, the application process is now open to apply for this tremendous honor. Applicants seeking admission must be 13 or 14 years of age on the first day of session and all students interested must submit their completed application by 5:00 pm on Tuesday, October 1st. Those interested in applying can find more information here. [https://capclass.virginiageneralassembly.gov/PagePrograms/PagePrograms.html?emci=31215aec-bd6b-ef11-991a-6045bdd9e096&emdi=ea000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001&ceid=29045629]
This past session I had the opportunity to recommend a middle school student from district applicants to serve as a page. Hannah from Prince George was both an amazing representative of our community and was well respected among her peers. Her mock legislation SB14 regarding IEP (Individualized Education Program) violations passed successfully and she was elected floor leader! I thank Hannah for her service and wish her all the best!
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP21/1/89018/images/Newsletter/may/Screenshot%202025-05-08%20at%201.25.06%E2%80%AFPM.png]
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP21/1/89018/images/Newsletter/Untitled%20design%20%2832%29.png] To the Past, Present and Future
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP21/1/89018/images/IMG_0090%20%281%29.JPG]
Last month, surrounded by friends and supporters we kicked off our year-long toast to the past, present and future. June marked ten years since winning my very first election and the beginning of my public service. My commitment since then has only grown stronger and those who stood with me then continue to uplift and stand with me now. For that I am grateful and even more energized to serve with integrity, transparency and accountability.
As always, my team and I are here to support you. During the legislative interim, we’re continuing district outreach, preparing for the next session, and advocating for your needs every step of the way. Reach us anytime at:
📞 (804) 452-7076
📧 senatoraird@senate.virginia.gov
🌐 www.lashrecseaird.com [http://www.lashrecseaird.com]
DONATE [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/la_website_direct]
https://www.facebook.com/airdcampaign [https://www.facebook.com/airdcampaign] https://twitter.com/lashrecseaird [https://twitter.com/lashrecseaird] https://www.instagram.com/lashrecseaird/ [https://www.instagram.com/lashrecseaird/]
Paid for and authorized by Friends of Lashrecse Aird
Friends of Lashrecse Aird
Post Office Box 3943
Petersburg, VA 23805
Aird for Delegate
PO Box 3943
Petersburg, VA 23805
United States

Specifically throughout Senate District 13, with our median household income ranging around $66,000 and a 13.4% poverty rate, Medicaid cuts as proposed will cause a spike in our uninsured rates and increase demands on health clinics. As a district with significant rural communities, those depending on rural hospitals are at risk of fewer healthcare options as those hospitals now face the threat of closure. Further, the legislation reverses SNAP administrative exemptions for veterans, people unhoused and youth aging out of foster care [] , which will lead to more families being disqualified for food assistance and becoming unable to put food on their table. In Virginia we have been preparing for the possibility of this bill’s passage and passed changes that allow us the ability to reconvene when necessary to take up serious matters to our budget such as the passage of the reconciliation budget. This will allow us to assess the Commonwealth’s resources and put in place supports and resources that are critically needed by individuals and families. Find out more about this legislation here. [] [] Our 2025 Democratic Nominees [] With the primary season well behind us, I am excited to support our Democratic statewide ticket, Abigail Spanberger [] for Governor, Ghazala Hashmi [] for Lieutenant Governor and Jay Jones [] for Attorney General. The decisions made by our statewide leaders are more important than ever before. Visit their websites linked above to learn more about their policy plans and vision for our Commonwealth. As a reminder: The General Election is November 4, 2025 The deadline to register to vote is October 24, 2025 Mailed ballot request may be made until October 24 Early in person voting occurs September 19 to November 1, 2025. [] Joint Boards & Commissions [] Meeting of the Joint Commission on Technology and Science When the General Assembly ends its legislative session, the work of our interim boards and commissions begins. I’ll spend my time this summer and fall serving on the following commissions listed below. Each commission's meetings are open to the public with the schedule and agendas accessible online. [] Tobacco Region Revitalization Commission (Vice Chair) Joint Commission on Technology and Science Behavior Health Commission Fentanyl and Heroin Task Force Newly Appointed, Commission on Women’s Health [] Newly Appointed, Joint Commission to Oversee the Transition of the Commonwealth into a Cannabis Retail Market [] Newly Appointed, Blockchain Advisory Committee [] [] [] Around the District [] Official Welcome of Bleachtech in Petersburg - It was exciting to officially welcome the ANSA McAL Group to Petersburg as a result of their acquisition of BLEACHTECH LLC. Not only is this the largest acquisition in the Caribbean conglomerate’s history but it represents another strong manufacturing partner for the City. It is clear that this family owned company is not only committed to their industry but to investing in the people in our community. A special thank you to Mr. A. Norman Sabga, Mr. Anthony Sabga III and the entire executive team for their vision and leadership shown through this project. National Guard Aviation Support Facility Groundbreaking (Sandston) - We broke ground this summer on a new Virginia National Guard Aviation Support Facility in Sandston — a long-awaited milestone made possible through General Assembly investments beginning in 2020. This state-of-the-art facility will serve as a critical hub for our citizen soldiers, ensuring they are prepared to respond quickly and effectively when called to serve, both here in the Commonwealth and beyond. Tindall Corporation Tour (Dinwiddie County) - It was a privilege to tour Tindall Corporation, a family-owned concrete manufacturing company that has operated in Dinwiddie since 1988. Their commitment to hiring and training workers at every skill level stood out — turning entry-level jobs into careers with dignity and growth potential. Seeing their process up close was a reminder of the skilled labor powering our local economy and the need for continued state support of industrial workforce development. * Presenting Commending Resolution to First Baptist Church of Petersburg - First Baptist Church in Petersburg — one of the oldest Black churches in America — has stood as a pillar of faith and justice since 1756 . This year, I was proud to carry Senate Resolution 284 commemorating their 250th anniversary , officially enshrining their legacy into Virginia’s historical record. Presenting that resolution in person was a deeply meaningful moment and a celebration of centuries of resilience and leadership. [] [] 2026 Senate Page Program Applications Open! Since 1850, 40 young leaders were selected to serve as Senate Pages in the upper chamber of the legislature during the General Assembly Session. The mission of the Senate Page Program is to facilitate a structured environment in which young Virginians accept responsibility and accountability, develop professional characteristics through strict standards of conduct, and engage in the legislative process through work, observation, and discussion. In keeping with tradition, the application process is now open to apply for this tremendous honor. Applicants seeking admission must be 13 or 14 years of age on the first day of session and all students interested must submit their completed application by 5:00 pm on Tuesday, October 1st. Those interested in applying can find more information here. [] This past session I had the opportunity to recommend a middle school student from district applicants to serve as a page. Hannah from Prince George was both an amazing representative of our community and was well respected among her peers. Her mock legislation SB14 regarding IEP (Individualized Education Program) violations passed successfully and she was elected floor leader! I thank Hannah for her service and wish her all the best! [] [] To the Past, Present and Future [] Last month, surrounded by friends and supporters we kicked off our year-long toast to the past, present and future. June marked ten years since winning my very first election and the beginning of my public service. My commitment since then has only grown stronger and those who stood with me then continue to uplift and stand with me now. For that I am grateful and even more energized to serve with integrity, transparency and accountability. As always, my team and I are here to support you. During the legislative interim, we’re continuing district outreach, preparing for the next session, and advocating for your needs every step of the way. Reach us anytime at: 📞 (804) 452-7076 📧 senatoraird@senate.virginia.gov 🌐 www.lashrecseaird.com [] DONATE [] [] [] [] Paid for and authorized by Friends of Lashrecse Aird Friends of Lashrecse Aird Post Office Box 3943 Petersburg, VA 23805 Aird for Delegate PO Box 3943 Petersburg, VA 23805 United States If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please unsubscribe: .

Our members are the driving force behind our movement to elect state Democrats. Our membership team helps inform our strategy, fund our investments, and lay the groundwork for Democratic powerbuilding.
**This year, we’ve already won 24 special elections, flipped two seats that Trump won by double digits, and invested over $750,000 into Virginia to protect our majority in the House of Delegates this fall. None of that would have been possible without the dedication of our 16,000 members.**
But with a team of _20,000+_ members, we can accomplish even more. With a well-funded movement, we can work in _more_ competitive races, reach _more_ voters, run _more_ targeted ads in close districts, and so much _more_. **That’s why we’re counting on you to own a piece of this fight and activate your membership – before it’s too late.**

Our membership team helps inform our strategy, fund our investments, and lay the groundwork for Democratic powerbuilding. This year, we’ve already won 24 special elections, flipped two seats that Trump won by double digits, and invested over $750,000 into Virginia to protect our majority in the House of Delegates this fall. None of that would have been possible without the dedication of our 16,000 members. But with a team of 20,000+ members, we can accomplish even more. With a well-funded movement, we can work in more competitive races, reach more voters, run more targeted ads in close districts, and so much more. That’s why we’re counting on you to own a piece of this fight and activate your membership – before it’s too late. name: Adam Schwarze, former Navy SEAL
subject: I’m starting with nothing
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Schwarze for Senate
body:
As a former Navy SEAL, I thrive in tough fights. I’ve faced worse odds overseas and am ready to bring that same grit to Washington |

---

Fellow Conservative,

My name is Adam Schwarze, and I’m a former Navy SEAL, Marine Corps veteran, and proud Minnesotan **running for U.S. Senate against Tim Walz’s handpicked lieutenant governor—a politician even more radical than her boss.** I’m requesting your support in this critical battle to restore common-sense conservative values to Minnesota.

I hold a Master’s from Harvard, a Master’s Certificate from Johns Hopkins in Security and Nuclear Deterrence, and an MBA. But my proudest achievement is serving our country to make my parents, especially my mom, proud. I want to serve you in the U.S. Senate, fighting for the values that have made Minnesota and America great for generations.

[](http://links.justandfreegop.com/u/click?_t=8ca5ef3d31cc43bcb1fbe6968d953911&_m=11358a3f8c0747fdb1947f8d65b0a160&_e=u_AX_uH7NNKkjpvBYrRiXrhqtc6iTwdzhrxBVsQ_Lq3QYi5Psq7HqKUtVVZqmr0B4DsMiuPY-jUQZKGfJZKe7wcvQLJi7oHfVhjwh40qDaeNAC88R9VYpNl79k2OUXUPn1FV3UxDT6Jwp9oNQ4l8MuF7hwJTozDB30mQj9Mduo5pX5qjEZb-BDdZ9CUhkK6RTEtZzx9bLSN9xREsfS7L39gaL-aRquR0JLEDmcRNg2XVy6XfgHuRmjFnZkbp7t5Qhf1GvkAplxQZTdfqPQ_brdh_G4sgCJUUkdA83hndOfcL9rvs9SDCKKzUqno8Wk277ATMljCcOVq60L2rSQOQYcArXVDykXbwz4cBJMrS544zmWhkuup901dP5EoJ3t9qqB6pCcXqgXGGlB9nVb-MngOWXnxqo71MLwcV6pNU1PpFKI5x9wflGlwZ9Ng26PzepDwNp5aY8mPYZPrpTzT9W5cwhIeY91iILYDj4otsCVSGC93BBVf3sx93QwyxB1w9vpItzVDY9xErDIll_2IAsrKT4syTamufJU7BNy-QPdqM9mi6ZZ-9xAvgh6MRuMrI-DePZSgmWEYBS7HUQuZZRgp_wBliDGScgScTzeIPJAmr-3nAn30D1JeqBK8PtJBTDWbu-uvs9KdH3FcOADO3bkkUt-cw73YNK6zi-qXjD3htfYeU5m4JI3IWA-r9GlJzmNsIGvcYHye7Fwv7_OxqXXkYNKEgrz-uPvwR91f-Bu1flsEWUasn2n4sxLwIlUCbqRIb2xtUx4YU3AwtHWoxjg%3D%3D)

**I’m starting this race with absolutely nothing** , but because of the enormity of the race's outcome, I figured there is no harm in asking for your help. At the end of the day, my request of you will end in one of two ways:
* 1.) **[you contribute to help elect a former Navy SEAL to the United States Senate](http://links.justandfreegop.com/u/click?_t=8ca5ef3d31cc43bcb1fbe6968d953911&_m=11358a3f8c0747fdb1947f8d65b0a160&_e=u_AX_uH7NNKkjpvBYrRiXrhqtc6iTwdzhrxBVsQ_Lq3QYi5Psq7HqKUtVVZqmr0B4DsMiuPY-jUQZKGfJZKe7wcvQLJi7oHfVhjwh40qDaeNAC88R9VYpNl79k2OUXUPn1FV3UxDT6Jwp9oNQ4l8MuF7hwJTozDB30mQj9Mduo5pX5qjEZb-BDdZ9CUhkK6RTEtZzx9bLSN9xREsfS7L39gaL-aRquR0JLEDmcRNg2XVy6XfgHuRmjFnZkbp7t5Qhf1GvkAplxQZTdfqPQ_brT9kPY-m-Jw_DWRHjz6EB5dQhudC_rXS_tSrron3uFM9VvohxNPaLf4oE4eDJ95OluS8zWLQPNMyZSMnAmlmvum2MvBjjMQrWq73jLJgFLEZn5_bXu4sXfofZLPmdMbVyzgyZckN62p_HNzmIUWTFW6bOYhsbHw54VX5b584TJKOIPihbZsFM7XIfiAzb0pekeIZ4AqqduT0ICyL5Pt0lfP00YGdfltFdyzGZoo4tklu0YdcZfM0UwlcFaGW0XVSt_xQm7mTCHFQSCLEz6Xp-yqahiGGuwRLNbSFCZEyANTUZSvm8fgEBy6e2amCauLLBWDgf92lgMQZYLJvX3q87KDP0Yv6PzFdWa2jom2KkyNJ1Wqc010gL8ML8X2MXLUiEpJSUI_a9EcqogpyFRMCpUPVI8wpECcThAtap3w_dHUAmusPAsLULt2l2dmn6Ap4eaHAy0tOM5zQRe0gEF0IyyVNzChF_04LWz8qewbwYYwYW0okmeFUMck9RgypS-nU1A%3D%3D)**.
* 2.) you read this email without contributing, but you at least now know who I am.

[**HELP ME GET MY CAMPAIGN OFF THE GROUND
CONTRIBUTE $2 OR MORE **](http://links.justandfreegop.com/u/click?_t=8ca5ef3d31cc43bcb1fbe6968d953911&_m=11358a3f8c0747fdb1947f8d65b0a160&_e=u_AX_uH7NNKkjpvBYrRiXrhqtc6iTwdzhrxBVsQ_Lq3QYi5Psq7HqKUtVVZqmr0B4DsMiuPY-jUQZKGfJZKe7wcvQLJi7oHfVhjwh40qDaeNAC88R9VYpNl79k2OUXUPn1FV3UxDT6Jwp9oNQ4l8MuF7hwJTozDB30mQj9Mduo5pX5qjEZb-BDdZ9CUhkK6RTEtZzx9bLSN9xREsfS7L39gaL-aRquR0JLEDmcRNg2XVy6XfgHuRmjFnZkbp7t5Qhf1GvkAplxQZTdfqPQ_brT5NYKrkrwCX0MpH0Ibu0rAFSnNiXyOfO2P2X66qLzRwsmIgM_TIZZkzQCqB3gu9hneTyK4Wa4OqViRAm3-tR7rFejNbQuJVdTh5DC1vNpCEPzYiuc6fb-As4VDcz0BNtIKlxF0WOjTG4YbZ84Cn5E9tzgXTq_pAlwuuDYAMNM7gooXhEiihYUyoRd-uMgB0siaOICEWSJBFr5Lv1avp_Ja5Rg1Gk_5n56j9Nbcq6SGOgrScalNALTi_0EXjG6QveDdUZqjsS6mseSPnRovChFxsTGBwsTtT5QlzKWSh69DKPVtkVvIPpvn72GApH5yAf9ywrrKyPMtTEjgQFiKTE8_AOqjLV0qa6iuPlB7x2jdNNnMrkXwVaYNZQFEKBncdvb2Cm1F6ANqBlWbJdxdognBOLZPib2fK4Ua8vKM5wkDliZ0yXkUeYu7DiLmq0JpwGQcaJsqbxo3U-ogmNTQm6c0kOTgHdjXwdwyF8-BbvyoZQreHMkfJMUzvZD3Ka-if0w%3D%3D)
**I’m not a career politician. I’m a regular guy from a working-class family who grew up playing hockey and answered our nation’s call on my 18th birthday in October 2001, just weeks after the 9/11 attacks.** I joined the Marine Corps to defend our country, and as a Navy SEAL, I deployed nine times to over 70 countries, risking my life to protect our freedoms and way of life. Unlike Tim Walz, who retired from the National Guard before his unit deployed to Iraq, I never backed down from serving our nation during war.

Minnesota is at a crossroads. Under Governor Walz and his lieutenant governor, our state has been dragged left by radical policies that fueled the violent unrest in Minneapolis in 2020, **defunded our police** , and burdened hardworking families with higher taxes and inflation. **While our streets burned, the lieutenant governor pushed lawsuits against law enforcement, undermining the officers keeping us safe.** This isn’t the Minnesota I grew up in—or the one any Minnesotan deserves.

What I’m about to do is going to put me in the crosshairs. **_But it must be done._** I’m going to force every Member of Congress to go ON RECORD about Jeffrey Epstein. And if you support what I’m doing here, _**_[I hope you’ll consider having my back by making a donation of just a few bucks.](https://e.youramerica.news/rd/9z2zvhovhr7fos3ioja0tcqfnnntcuoqouauvflh0bo_rp22sh2s8i6epb2c3hmeo34c9h2bs)_**_ I am about to file something called a “discharge petition.” **_In simple terms, I am using a congressional procedure to attempt to FORCE a vote on releasing the Epstein files in their entirety._** \- The client lists \- The blackmail networks \- The foreign intelligence interests ALL must be exposed. **If this vote succeeds, EVERY Member of Congress will be forced to vote simply “YES” or “NO” on whether or not the government should release the Epstein files.** **And at the end of the day, this decision, this action right here and right now, could be the end of a 20-year-long saga of CORRUPTION, LIES, and CRIMINAL ACTIVITY.** I’m about to make a lot of enemies – Republicans, Democrats, Independents, and everyone in between. I don’t care who I upset – all I care about is the TRUTH. Thank you for taking a moment out of your time to read my message. Sincerely, Congressman Thomas Massie P.S. Whether or not you donate today, I am moving forward with this. I’m not pay-to-play like so many other politicians in Washington. **I won’t be bought, bribed, or blackmailed.**
But the tidal wave of pressure from the Uniparty is about to get 10X worse when I force them to go on record over the Epstein files... _**_[_**_and when it does, I truly need patriots like you in my corner. _**_](https://e.youramerica.news/rd/9z2zknnfeepg4sbo9oectocb0m2nlgfib7iedoqbiso_rp22sh2s8i6epb2c3hmeo34c9h2bs) _**_

name: KAREN CRNKOVICH FOR JOHNSON COUNTY COMMISSION
subject: We’ve Built the Spark—Now Let’s Light the Fire
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY KAREN CRNKOVICH FOR JOHNSON COUNTY COMMISSION * 6709 West 119th Street * Ste
body:
| | | | | [|
---](https://kcforjoco.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=d540da3225ec5b2797f3018d2&id=4949b59dee&e=7320325a61)
| | | | [|
---](https://kcforjoco.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=d540da3225ec5b2797f3018d2&id=876038f7d9&e=7320325a61)
| Dear Peter,
Over the past month, I’ve been deeply moved by the outpouring of support for my campaign for Johnson County Commission Chair. Every kind word, every donation, every person who has stepped up to volunteer—it’s all reminded me why I’m in this race. Your encouragement doesn’t just lift my spirit—it drives this movement forward and strengthens our shared belief that Johnson County can do better.
But while we’re building momentum, the challenges we face are growing too. Mike Kelly and county leaders have proposed a **$1.9 billion budget for 2026,** the largest in Johnson County history. That’s not a sign of responsible governance. That’s a symptom of **unchecked spending, bloated bureaucracy, and misplaced priorities**.
As a business owner and taxpayer, I know how to live within a budget and make tough decisions. I don’t believe in raising taxes every time the government wants more. I believe in **respecting your hard-earned dollars** , eliminating waste, and restoring **common-sense leadership** to our county government.
**Together, we can shape a county that reflects our shared values—fiscal responsibility, transparency, and a true commitment to the people who call Johnson County home.**
**But I can’t do it without you.**
To stay on track and competitive, we need to raise $20,000 by the end of the month. If you believe we deserve better than bloated budgets and business-as-usual politics, please consider making a contribution today. Every dollar is greatly appreciated and makes a difference.
[**CHIP IN TODAY**](https://kcforjoco.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=d540da3225ec5b2797f3018d2&id=8b8c3e2cd6&e=7320325a61)
[ On July 29th, we are holding our campaign kick-off fundraiser. I’d love to see you there - here are the details:](https://kcforjoco.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=d540da3225ec5b2797f3018d2&id=70db3d943b&e=7320325a61)Tuesday, July 29th5:30 to 7:30 p.m.Nicklaus Golf Club at LionsGateOverland Park
Please RSVP to Allison Reed allielujah@yahoo.com

Thank you for standing with me. Let’s keep building this movement—and bring real leadership back to Johnson County.
With gratitude,
**Karen Crnkovich**
Candidate for Johnson County Commission Chair

---
| [View](https://kcforjoco.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=d540da3225ec5b2797f3018d2&id=10b192fa41&e=7320325a61)

subject: Not everyone will receive this invite.

body:
| | | Hi there, it's Senator Kirsten Gillibrand, your DSCC chair. I have a time-sensitive request for you -- **not everyone will receive this invite, but I need to be sure _you_ do.**

**[I'm conducting an _exclusive_ strategy survey with our strongest supporters to strengthen our plans to win big in the midterms -- and our records indicate that you're a TOP Democrat in Pennsylvania. Your direct input could make or break our path to victory, so please, will you take my Democratic Strategy Survey before midnight?](https://go.dscc.org/go/21904?ask=5&t=5&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=Pennsylvania&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&amounts=5%2C10%2C20%2C50&code=DPE-ND-20250718-KirstenGillibrand-ExclusiveStrategySurvey-Pros-sl1&refcode=DPE-ND-20250718-KirstenGillibrand-ExclusiveStrategySurvey-Pros-sl1&akid=20761%2E9716520%2EL0BEj1)**

Right now, Trump is gambling with our future -- our jobs, health care, fundamental freedoms, and more -- all to deliver massive tax breaks to the wealthiest Americans. Winning back the Senate is our best chance to stop his MAGA extremism, but to do it, we'll have to take on some of Trump's biggest GOP allies.

Those battles will be tough -- and flipping Republican-held seats will take bold and brilliant strategy. With so much on the line, we can't leave anything to chance. **This survey will only take a moment of your time, but your voice will make a real difference in how we fight back.

[I know that your input will be invaluable, so please -- will you take my survey before midnight to help Democrats win big in the midterms? We'll be standing by to read your response >>](https://go.dscc.org/go/21904?ask=5&t=7&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=Pennsylvania&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&amounts=5%2C10%2C20%2C50&code=DPE-ND-20250718-KirstenGillibrand-ExclusiveStrategySurvey-Pros-sl1&refcode=DPE-ND-20250718-KirstenGillibrand-ExclusiveStrategySurvey-Pros-sl1&akid=20761%2E9716520%2EL0BEj1)**

Kirsten

DSCC Chair
