This is an absolutely critical time, and we need to do two things: Continue hammering away at Trump every day, and start peeling off Trump’s supporters as they wake up.
#
Peter:
We know you can’t chip in to every email we send. Nobody can.
But we do need people to chip in when it really matters, and when they can afford to. Let us explain why today is one of those days.
**FIRST**
Trump ran on a platform that would empower regular people over the elites. He promised he’d never cut Medicaid, that he would always fight for Veterans, and that he’d finally drain the swamp. That was his promise.
**THEN**
He completely betrayed them. He’s gutting things that Veterans and their families depend on like Medicaid, the VA, the Department of Education and cutting taxes for the richest people in the country.
**NOW**
Even his own supporters are starting to see through the lies. This is an absolutely critical time, and we need to do two things: Continue hammering away at Trump every day, and start peeling off Trump’s supporters after they wake up.
It’s really important that we see a surge in donations right now, because we’ve got a huge opportunity to turn things around. Trump’s turned his back on Veterans and their families, and VoteVets is the only organization on this side of the aisle that is positioned to reach them.
This is a big moment. We can’t miss it.

**Winning in Arizona, Nevada, and New Hampshire is critical to the enduring success of President Trump 's America First agenda.**

These states have a number of important factors that make them the perfect targets for our grassroots ground game.
1. They are considered contested states that are important to both parties.
2. These states are growing.
3. **Republicans outnumber Democrats.**

As permitted by law, TTPAC, Inc. maintains a non-contribution account for the purpose of influencing federal, state, and local elections. Federal law requires us to use our best efforts to collect and report the name, address, occupation, and employer of individuals whose contribution exceeds $200 in an election cycle. For contributions from federal committees, corporations, and labor unions - regardless of contribution amount - federal law requires us to use our best efforts to collect and report the committee's name and address. State and local law may require us to collect and report additional information.

Contributions to this account may be used to influence federal elections by making independent expenditures in support of or in opposition to federal candidates. Additionally, in state and local jurisdictions that permit non-individual type (e.g., corporate) contributions directly to candidates and/or committees, contributions to this account may be used for both independent expenditures and direct contributions to candidates and/or committees, as permitted by state and local law.

Individuals, federal committees, corporations, and labor unions may contribute to TTPAC, Inc.'s non-contribution account without limit. Contributions by national banks, foreign nationals (without lawful permanent resident status), and other foreign entities (e.g., foreign corporations) are prohibited. Contributions to TTPAC, Inc.'s non-contribution account are not tax deductible for federal income tax purposes.

# BIG SKY REPORT:
An Update from Senator Daines
---
| Fellow American,

It’s been an extremely busy couple of weeks to kick off the month of July! We started the month of July with a BANG with the passage and signing of President Trump’s Big Beautiful Bill, just as we celebrated our nation’s 249th birthday! I am committed to delivering results that reflect our shared values and strengthen our communities. Thank you for your continued support and feedback—it drives me to fight for you every day.

I hope you enjoy this edition of the _**Big Sky Report**_.
---
|
### Policy Achievements: Advancing Montana’s Priorities
#### President Trump’s _Big Beautiful Bill_ signed into law
I joined President Trump at the White House for the signing of the _Big Beautiful Bill_ , which includes an enormous list of my legislative priorities. The legislation also prevents the largest tax increase in American history, makes the tax cuts permanent, and provides funding for border security and the military.

For a detailed list of what’s in the bill, you can visit my Senate website [**here.**](http://links.action.supportstevedaines.com/u/click?_t=71faa67ca88f4c4b899697b6a4621901&_m=427ffa0157124d5180ec9419705f3d3f&_e=k-n3SPlANL3iC8l45BhDL1oN4271MsLOr-PAXuCnbQDE9s3JjNxSnt3gM1JKi7Qck2eLAscvVh0FvkjGxqBqF81CCkRr3Z8Q6efWM8fa0Fkc0WkqQj9SPbcwXhCRiUaUVPocUdqIpxaHvIlcO5MQAeatwY_OnlxWKeR0AHrUAZkKtjO7D2GqEDQIks5pOzXi3gN25ic065yC03ZGfzj896V-bNtiUVwp7pQ5hFxeoIA2BdL-FI4XueBRC_dKFEuS655PeWyFeg5pWQ3PCjD5Mtfq-WyniwmlyQhJRhUkERnC13WBIT54dEayR8Ko5b5LVVdUFIdVPVhFBGP13BhVFPzXdJJZR2vJzXbCSaThHDeiIppl_oKU8AVIgs__fXYy66K3RFSSWBk9WIMZIrMvT4d_cP02HITS0Eg-PZsNRYdiI0VHTSFTpU7f9k5WCRO7Wuwuc4Dy6wqIkBeUTAo-5Dxcjh2T1lcaD4vEZx5mYTo%3D)

[](http://links.action.supportstevedaines.com/u/click?_t=71faa67ca88f4c4b899697b6a4621901&_m=427ffa0157124d5180ec9419705f3d3f&_e=k-n3SPlANL3iC8l45BhDL1oN4271MsLOr-PAXuCnbQDE9s3JjNxSnt3gM1JKi7Qck2eLAscvVh0FvkjGxqBqF81CCkRr3Z8Q6efWM8fa0Fkc0WkqQj9SPbcwXhCRiUaUVPocUdqIpxaHvIlcO5MQAb0ICk-lADqUEBcPX6upDeoWjKAgYmmjwX5sVThRpqHf9c4pjy1pFpkLRmT6c9YFR2UBsyCfTZPdA8YD4Jnlba4nUz-Jzx7L4JntE65RwRtWiAwjUIGZ7tqbV2JlDburknl0WufsfhQRG0g_XBEFIojp7Yr5SB0ECUWFHuHD4-w7yFXhF3n8k_wDeBAhC-RDf4U-hWenkr08qiuP758S69wvOzZNz71mWHzvSEwVOa995aF5c8EXipSS-FwnYzrrynFLc4lJVzfmR0CrQ_2F-m__g0iKBjctWHOwqqxOnEZ36L398tNpsWZom0ocagVf7d-LWZ3DAY2iFjzZ8iUjItY%3D)
#### Advancing the HALT Fentanyl Act
On July 10, 2025, my _Halt All Lethal Trafficking (HALT)_ of Fentanyl Act passed the Senate, a major step toward protecting Montana communities from the devastating impact of fentanyl. By permanently classifying fentanyl and its related substances as Schedule I under the Controlled Substances Act, this bill cracks down on the illegal trade fueling the opioid crisis. I’m proud to lead this effort to keep our families safe and hold those responsible for this deadly epidemic accountable.
#### Supporting Rural Communities with the Small County PILT Parity Act
I introduced the _Small County PILT Parity Act_ alongside Senator Catherine Cortez Masto to support Montana’s rural counties with populations under 5,000. This bipartisan bill ensures these counties receive fair Payment In Lieu of Taxes (PILT) funding, critical for essential services like emergency response and transportation. By creating parity with larger counties, we’re helping places like Petroleum and Garfield Counties thrive.
#### Protecting Montana’s Energy Jobs
On July 8, 2025, I urged the Senate Energy and Natural Resources Committee to pass my legislation, S.362, to keep the Bull Mountains Mine open. This mine is a vital economic driver for Musselshell County, supporting good-paying jobs and energy security. I also celebrated the approval of the federal mining plan for the Spring Creek Coal Mine in Decker, MT, after years of delays under the previous administration. These efforts underscore my commitment to Montana’s energy industry and the families who depend on it.
---
|
### Community Engagement: Listening to Montanans
#### Montana State University Ag Roundtable
On July 3, 2025, I hosted a roundtable discussion with Montana agriculture and forestry leaders at Montana State University’s Post Farm in Bozeman. We discussed priorities for the upcoming farm bill, ensuring USDA programs work for Montana’s farmers and ranchers. I also toured the Post Farm with Dr. Sreekala Bajwa, Vice President, Dean, and Director of the MSU College of Agriculture, to see the innovative work being done to train the next generation of ag leaders. Supporting Montana’s agricultural legacy is a top priority, and these conversations help shape policies that keep our farms and ranches strong.
#### Recognizing Montanan of the Week
Each week, I honor a Montanan who embodies our state’s spirit through my “Montanan of the Week” initiative. On July 7, 2025, I recognized Sarah Thompson, a Helena teacher who has dedicated 20 years to educating Montana’s youth. Her innovative STEM programs have inspired countless students to pursue careers in science and technology. Nominate a deserving Montanan by calling my office at 202-224-2651 or visiting [**www.daines.senate.gov/connect/email-steve.**](http://links.action.supportstevedaines.com/u/click?_t=71faa67ca88f4c4b899697b6a4621901&_m=427ffa0157124d5180ec9419705f3d3f&_e=BAL-QVS1WHSbNv8zMCngnfMWqlat3XB6RpW_TmZUaTpiYoj3_OjbicSWqD-S2L54uKk9Ah2kQCqvDVQjM6VLRRT9kk5ZBk4rRIwd2VjODrJKLTny9BDcsbmnVxrQM34vzlqQaXYq8XeyZvmntE6Ua4Z9jxdItGedbKbhmAMkYLWF5nxKfrUDTqwXHbx_OTgtSToRDJ8YMoGzot0DYuGOmnvHUq3v4cCfzN--MAvIC23QCAMDufsS1toj_prcDUnLlG2bcWEPAZlz_X9499toTcakSZf5xtrrJ2RDwCbw7jYgu7-eSBQpFyElMowVVTRD3DwHZDbjXNtx1fqDpZKoRgPTFcMiPo6quZo9QDRFJmlAFqqeqezqKMcm4JQjKeM4)
#### Meeting with Montana Tribal Leaders
On July 12, 2025, I met with leaders from the Crow Tribe to discuss public safety, tribal sovereignty, and economic opportunities. These conversations are critical to ensuring Montana’s tribes have the necessary resources and support. I remain committed to working with tribal communities to address their unique challenges and preserve their cultural heritage.
---
|
### Looking Ahead
This month showed what we can achieve when we’re united behind a common goal and a shared set of values. We delivered historic gains for the American people, but we still have a lot of work to do. clean_body: I’m proud to share what we’ve been working on to deliver for you—fighting for our shared values, strengthening our communities, and pushing forward common-sense policies that put Montana first. | | BIG SKY REPORT: An Update from Senator Daines | Fellow American, It’s been an extremely busy couple of weeks to kick off the month of July! We started the month of July with a BANG with the passage and signing of President Trump’s Big Beautiful Bill, just as we celebrated our nation’s 249th birthday! I am committed to delivering results that reflect our shared values and strengthen our communities. Thank you for your continued support and feedback—it drives me to fight for you every day. I hope you enjoy this edition of the Big Sky Report. | Policy Achievements: Advancing Montana’s Priorities President Trump’s Big Beautiful Bill signed into law I joined President Trump at the White House for the signing of the Big Beautiful Bill , which includes an enormous list of my legislative priorities. The legislation also prevents the largest tax increase in American history, makes the tax cuts permanent, and provides funding for border security and the military. For a detailed list of what’s in the bill, you can visit my Senate website Advancing the HALT Fentanyl Act On July 10, 2025, my Halt All Lethal Trafficking (HALT) of Fentanyl Act passed the Senate, a major step toward protecting Montana communities from the devastating impact of fentanyl. By permanently classifying fentanyl and its related substances as Schedule I under the Controlled Substances Act, this bill cracks down on the illegal trade fueling the opioid crisis. I’m proud to lead this effort to keep our families safe and hold those responsible for this deadly epidemic accountable. Supporting Rural Communities with the Small County PILT Parity Act I introduced the Small County PILT Parity Act alongside Senator Catherine Cortez Masto to support Montana’s rural counties with populations under 5,000. This bipartisan bill ensures these counties receive fair Payment In Lieu of Taxes (PILT) funding, critical for essential services like emergency response and transportation. By creating parity with larger counties, we’re helping places like Petroleum and Garfield Counties thrive. Protecting Montana’s Energy Jobs On July 8, 2025, I urged the Senate Energy and Natural Resources Committee to pass my legislation, S.362, to keep the Bull Mountains Mine open. This mine is a vital economic driver for Musselshell County, supporting good-paying jobs and energy security. I also celebrated the approval of the federal mining plan for the Spring Creek Coal Mine in Decker, MT, after years of delays under the previous administration. These efforts underscore my commitment to Montana’s energy industry and the families who depend on it. | Community Engagement: Listening to Montanans Montana State University Ag Roundtable On July 3, 2025, I hosted a roundtable discussion with Montana agriculture and forestry leaders at Montana State University’s Post Farm in Bozeman. We discussed priorities for the upcoming farm bill, ensuring USDA programs work for Montana’s farmers and ranchers. I also toured the Post Farm with Dr. Sreekala Bajwa, Vice President, Dean, and Director of the MSU College of Agriculture, to see the innovative work being done to train the next generation of ag leaders. Supporting Montana’s agricultural legacy is a top priority, and these conversations help shape policies that keep our farms and ranches strong. Recognizing Montanan of the Week Each week, I honor a Montanan who embodies our state’s spirit through my “Montanan of the Week” initiative. On July 7, 2025, I recognized Sarah Thompson, a Helena teacher who has dedicated 20 years to educating Montana’s youth. Her innovative STEM programs have inspired countless students to pursue careers in science and technology. Nominate a deserving Montanan by calling my office at 202-224-2651 or visiting Meeting with Montana Tribal Leaders On July 12, 2025, I met with leaders from the Crow Tribe to discuss public safety, tribal sovereignty, and economic opportunities. These conversations are critical to ensuring Montana’s tribes have the necessary resources and support. I remain committed to working with tribal communities to address their unique challenges and preserve their cultural heritage. | Looking Ahead This month showed what we can achieve when we’re united behind a common goal and a shared set of values. We delivered historic gains for the American people, but we still have a lot of work to do. For Montana, Senator Steve Daines | ABOUT SENATOR STEVE DAINES Steve Daines is a fifth-generation Montanan, life-long sportsman and father of four serving the people of Montana as their Senator. Steve is committed to always putting Montana first as he works to bring real change to Washington and fights for more jobs, less government and common sense solutions to get our country back on track. Paid for by Steve Daines for Montana

name: Heidi Ganahl
subject: The Mountain Minute: 7/18/2025
body:
Tracking the untracked: The dangerous fallout of Biden’s broken migrant child system
| | | | [](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
---
**PRESENTED BY[ROCKYMOUNTAINVOICE.COM](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
| | Hi friends,
Check out today's Mountain Minute podcast [here](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.), or watch on [YouTube](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) or [Rumble](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)! Here is a summary of today's top stories:
🏔️ **Here in Colorado...** Wildfire season is off to a fierce start, with[ over 35,000 acres burned](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) and Gov. Polis issuing a disaster declaration. The Turner Gulch fire has already consumed nearly 14,000 acres, expected to burn for weeks. Meanwhile, Lake Powell’s plunging levels [threaten hydropower production](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) by 2026, and a [Christian bookstore is suing the state](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) over laws it says would force it to affirm gender identities against its religious beliefs.
🇺🇸 **On the National Stage...** Vindication continues for President Trump as new evidence [confirms the Trump-Russia story was fabricated](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.), tied to Clinton-funded reports and politically motivated intel framing. A leaked memo also reveals D.C. [judges privately admitted bias against Trump](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)—raising serious questions about impartiality in ongoing legal cases.
**🗣️ Commentary Spotlight...** The “One Big Beautiful Bill” includes federal tax credits for [homeschool and private education](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)—if states opt in. Will Colorado? Also, a new conservation ballot measure could [sideline rural voices](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.), expand bureaucracy, and push global agendas at the expense of Colorado landowners.
This is the Mountain Minute for Friday, July 18, 2025.
– Team RMV
---
|
---
**TODAY'S TOP STORIES**
**[Tracking the untracked: The dangerous fallout of Biden’s broken migrant child system](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** BY SHAINA COLE, [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
Each year, children continue to arrive at the southern border without anyone to look after them. They’re officially called unaccompanied alien children—or UACs—but behind that label are thousands of kids trying to navigate an immigration system meant for adults.
Many are handed off to sponsors with little vetting, and some end up trafficked, working illegal jobs, or worse.
Back in the early 2000s, the Office of Refugee Resettlement (ORR) was handling fewer than 8,000 unaccompanied kids a year. But that’s changed dramatically.
By 2022, that figure had climbed to over 128,000. The following year saw more than 113,000 children placed with sponsors across the U.S.
**[READ MORE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
|
---
**[O’Donnell: Colorado’s new wildlife commission will conserve everything—except common sense](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
* A proposed 2026 ballot initiative would create a new Wildlife and Ecosystem Conservation Commission, overlapping with Colorado Parks and Wildlife and expanding state bureaucracy under the guise of conservation.
* The draft plan includes tax incentives for landowners to hand over up to 30% of their land but bars anyone with ties to agriculture, energy, or development from serving—effectively excluding those who work the land.
* Critics warn the initiative aligns with UN-driven “30x30” goals and RadicalxChange philosophy, threatening rural communities, agricultural viability, and private property rights through top-down regulation and potential land grabs.

**[READ MORE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
|
---
The Rocky Mountain Voice is a 501(c)(3) that exists to promote informed civic engagement and the principles of self-governance through enterprise reporting on public policy, politics, government, and culture throughout the state of Colorado. The only way we can continue to spread truth is with your support!
[DONATE TO ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
|
---
**[Enos: One Big Beautiful Bill? Encouraging news for homeschool freedom—and reason to stay engaged](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** [CHRISTIAN HOME EDUCATORS OF COLORADOO](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
* The "One Big Beautiful Bill," signed by President Trump, includes the Educational Choice for Children Act, providing private tax-credit-funded scholarships for students—including homeschoolers—without federal strings attached.
* Eligible donors can receive up to a $1,700 nonrefundable tax credit for contributions to approved Scholarship Granting Organizations (SGOs); however, states like Colorado must opt into the program for their residents to benefit.
* While the bill marks a major win for school choice, its true impact on homeschoolers will depend on state-level decisions and future implementation details, which won’t take effect until the 2027 tax year.

**[READ MORE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
|
---
** ONLY ON ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE ⤵️ **
**[Ganahl: What’s inside the ‘Big Beautiful Bill’—and why it’s a game-changer for families and freedom](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** **BY HEIDI GANAHL****| COMMENTARY | JULY 15** _They’re calling it the “Big Beautiful Bill”—and whether you love it or hate it, it’s the boldest shakeup to our tax and spending system since Trump’s 2017 tax cuts. Officially titled the Tax Relief for American Families and Workers Act, this legislation does a lot: extends the Trump tax cuts, rewrites how safety net programs work, beefs up border and defense spending, and trims down the green energy handouts._
**[Behind closed doors: Leaked memos reveal how Durango 9-R Schools avoided a DEI lawsuit—until now](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)****BY JEN SCHUMANN | LOCAL | JULY 15** _Durango School District 9-R changed its hiring policy this spring without a public announcement—but the move didn’t go unnoticed. According to a new federal civil rights complaint filed by Mountain States Legal Foundation (MSLF) and authored by its general counsel, William Trachman, the district had for years pushed a race-conscious hiring agenda, encouraged by internal equity committees and shielded by carefully worded legal guidance._ __
**[The COvid Chronicles June 16-23, 2020: Social justice got a platform—police got massive reform](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)****BY RMV EDITORIAL BOARD | COMMENTARY | JULY 14** _In this ninth chapter of The COvid Chronicles, summer arrived—but sanity didn’t. Looking back at our COVID-19 history is painful. But as Spanish-American philosopher put it, “Those who cannot remember the past are condemned to repeat it."_
__

**Feel like you have a unique story or commentary to share?** We'd love to talk with you about becoming an RMV Contributor. Reply to this email (or reach out to [tori@rockymountainvoice.com](mailto:tori@rockymountainvoice.com?subject=Contributor%20Interest%20\(via%20Mountain%20Minute\))) or send us a message on social media to chat!
|
---
|
---
**🍎 DID YOU KNOW?** **Johnny Appleseed wasn’t planting apples for snacking.** The real John Chapman filled the frontier with apple trees—but the fruit they bore was tart and bitter, not sweet. His apples were meant for fermentation, not the fruit bowl—perfect for making hard cider, not pie.
|
---
**CARTOON OF THE DAY**
|
---
**QUOTE OF THE DAY** "Remember the first rule of gunfighting... 'have a gun.'" – Jeff Cooper
|

Eligible donors can receive up to a $1,700 nonrefundable tax credit for contributions to approved Scholarship Granting Organizations (SGOs); however, states like Colorado must opt into the program for their residents to benefit. While the bill marks a major win for school choice, its true impact on homeschoolers will depend on state-level decisions and future implementation details, which won’t take effect until the 2027 tax year. | ONLY ON ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE ⤵️ BY HEIDI GANAHL| COMMENTARY | JULY 15 They’re calling it the “Big Beautiful Bill”—and whether you love it or hate it, it’s the boldest shakeup to our tax and spending system since Trump’s 2017 tax cuts. Officially titled the Tax Relief for American Families and Workers Act, this legislation does a lot: extends the Trump tax cuts, rewrites how safety net programs work, beefs up border and defense spending, and trims down the green energy handouts. JEN SCHUMANN | LOCAL | JULY 15 Durango School District 9-R changed its hiring policy this spring without a public announcement—but the move didn’t go unnoticed. According to a new federal civil rights complaint filed by Mountain States Legal Foundation (MSLF) and authored by its general counsel, William Trachman, the district had for years pushed a race-conscious hiring agenda, encouraged by internal equity committees and shielded by carefully worded legal guidance. RMV EDITORIAL BOARD | COMMENTARY | JULY 14 In this ninth chapter of The COvid Chronicles, summer arrived—but sanity didn’t. Looking back at our COVID-19 history is painful. But as Spanish-American philosopher put it, “Those who cannot remember the past are condemned to repeat it." Feel like you have a unique story or commentary to share? We'd love to talk with you about becoming an RMV Contributor. Reply to this email (or reach out to mailto:tori@rockymountainvoice.com?subject=Contributor%20Interest%20\(via%20Mountain%20Minute\)) or send us a message on social media to chat! | | 🍎 DID YOU KNOW? Johnny Appleseed wasn’t planting apples for snacking. The real John Chapman filled the frontier with apple trees—but the fruit they bore was tart and bitter, not sweet. His apples were meant for fermentation, not the fruit bowl—perfect for making hard cider, not pie. | CARTOON OF THE DAY | QUOTE OF THE DAY "Remember the first rule of gunfighting... 'have a gun.'" – Jeff Cooper | | | | ---|---|--- Did a friend forward you this email? | A product of Rocky Mountain Voice, Inc. 8200 S Quebec St, STE A3601 Centennial, CO 80112 This message was originally sent to: dpwillis67@gmail.com Be sure to put “RockyMountainVoice.com” on your safe-senders whitelist. If you ever stop receiving our emails, it might be because someone to whom you forwarded the email unintentionally removed you from the list. No worries; it is easy enough to reactivate your subscription immediately by visiting: Before you click the link below... If someone forwarded this email to you, please don’t! Clicking the link will end the subscription of dpwillis67@gmail.com []()

body:
[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/32783?t=1011&refcode=email-20250718_v&akid=9044%2E5885482%2EFzy3d1 ]Our Revolution logo

Peter Willis,

Progressive pressure to unite Democrats behind Zohran Mamdani’s bold
vision for NYC has reached a boiling point:

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/32783?source=email-20250718&t=1061&akid=9044%2E5885482%2EFzy3d1 ]Headline: 'It's time': Progressives pressure top Democrats to back
Mamdani

Even the mainstream media is highlighting how progressive powerhouses –
including Our Revolution – are OVERWHELMING Democratic leaders with
demands to back Mamdani as NYC’s next mayor.

The establishment can no longer ignore us in favor of its billionaire
donors who see Mamdani as a threat to their vast power – but we have not
won yet and we need your help to keep the momentum growing strong.

name: Weil Change Coming
subject: One Conversation is All It Takes
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Josh Weil, Democrat for US Senate
body:
[https://d15k2d11r6t6rl.cloudfront.net/public/users/Integrators/04eaa6b2-88f4-43b4-b978-7bb0708f5838/387153444E713877456653376A556574376F314841345859327A4D665362374C42586753794B64635252546F6551413647436E7356536243345157355952664A/editor_images/Screenshot%202025-06-18%20at%2011.41.25%E2%80%AFAM.png]
Friend, this moment right here... that’s what this U.S. Senate run is about.
[https://d15k2d11r6t6rl.cloudfront.net/public/users/Integrators/04eaa6b2-88f4-43b4-b978-7bb0708f5838/735079395736517559726A5141563568796C3668377A325863636A523866355552552B7478374C3262492B4436783137366F63374342376C5569706A55724C73/editor_images/Screenshot%202025-07-18%20at%2012.12.34%E2%80%AFPM.png]
Not the big stage or the cable news hits, but the quiet, honest conversations at folding tables under tents. The ones where folks share what’s really on their minds.
I’ve spent this campaign listening . Not lecturing . And what I’ve learned is this: people are ready for something better.
They’re tired of being talked down to, lied to, and forgotten. They want leadership that meets them where they are and walks with them forward. I believe one conversation can change everything. It starts with listening, and it builds with every person willing to believe in something better.
That’s why I’m running, because change needs a trailblazer to make the ripples. And it’s why I need you with me.
[DONATE TODAY to help me make that change EARLY, and make 2026 a WIN] [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/coreemail_jw_kl]
If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:
Express Donate: $10 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/coreemail_jw_kl?express_lane=true&amount=10&refcode=express_lane_10]
Express Donate: $25 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/coreemail_jw_kl?express_lane=true&amount=25&refcode=express_lane_25]
Express Donate: $50 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/coreemail_jw_kl?express_lane=true&amount=50&refcode=express_lane_50]
Express Donate: $100 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/coreemail_jw_kl?express_lane=true&amount=100&refcode=express_lane_100]
Or, donate another amount [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/coreemail_jw_kl?express_lane_other=true&refcode=express_lane_other]
Every contribution fuels more of these moments. More connection. More conversations. More hope.
Thank you Friend ,
[https://d15k2d11r6t6rl.cloudfront.net/public/users/Integrators/04eaa6b2-88f4-43b4-b978-7bb0708f5838/387153444E713877456653376A556574376F314841345859327A4D665362374C42586753794B64635252546F6551413647436E7356536243345157355952664A/editor_images/Screenshot%202025-06-18%20at%2011.05.04%E2%80%AFAM.png]
Paid for by Josh Weil, Democrat for US Senate
Josh Weil for US Senate
5800 Beach Blvd. STE 203-337
Jacksonville, FL 32207

name: Chilling
subject: The Death Sentence for NPR
disclaimer_text: Paid for by No Dem Left Behind PAC Not Authorized by Any Federal Candidate or Candidate's Committee
body:
Service may not end today but over the course of the next year we’ll see mass firings of key journalists, programs shut down, and offices shuttered, until nothing is left. | | [ ](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqvUWHo9tkdcudztgboR-91DsyN7JHTAxVJ1fElVIhvp5wHnAGI1xujxk1ZGzWkN3WBn2ThHW3VtgjUp6DBLnCem0kJRAIxBtlpdEs9VjHum6bwp442a2FBD15g_bhzkkHDwBHDZLSPFbMgMec_QrtKdZLkNYFZ4YqmucEp4Yzp1_zOnfZsjDSAlpioSb-6em_00EZ7Go0SQxHG2hKlh3_7wjvZl3Xp970F2dO-x4kTMX2u95dlgso8d7pRnL-9DWUplYuRR0TRPxJlfg2zWgyxUzYMR_JxM3Ru6xY8oMaAZlG4yxn72vzMh50PQXAoJmWQUH5qUm892kdiq_VTeSbUk/4ia/joCZBVRwShmUmsJLW8JqeA/h0/h001.L-nvs9fXb7qPFifDKNnc4GC0LN7urf1frdEajqArIGA)

Friend, Today Congress voted to cut nearly $1.1 billion in federal funding for the Corporation for Public Broadcasting. For NPR, who receives nearly all of their funding from the Corporation for Public Broadcasting **this is a death sentence.**
---
| [ ](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqvUWHo9tkdcudztgboR-91DsyN7JHTAxVJ1fElVIhvp55ou4p-NDhUaoPeASBmjSkjnhz3oncum5UnpIrDFjSjgQNpKOog8g0rjKUdKBzmhHGddUqjMQkJI6EEB6Msbf2bvhaQ8TPDx7qgLEbb3FOj35q1epLgfHx_PObhuowIWG7ItckgEa7AetHBZU1GbOckpX5hNciBHbysFxc3S0Urx2YzcFS79KS85JDBfFmR0OgRVHyCohhCDw6if_SNYEeVM5KYYSF3O1T5nCQmjCwg2ABjM_DzjmaCZI58kJoavnxZfBo4NFCKZfgvhCo705OWtyG3Xry7eLQdJ8dvxlTNl/4ia/joCZBVRwShmUmsJLW8JqeA/h1/h001.RgcMtzyqY42aQjc5Cztup02n1V6UyMXdzY1C4Sp-R9U)
---
Service may not end today but over the course of the next year we'll see mass firings of key journalists, programs shut down, and offices shuttered, until nothing is left. However, if NPR can hunker down for the next year and a half we have a fleeting opportunity to save it: **_Reclaiming the house._** **[No Dem Left Behind is hard at work supporting good democrats running in flippable rural house districts. For many of our candidates we are their only lifeline. Can we count on you to support our critical work by rushing to help us take back the house and save NPR?](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqvUWHo9tkdcudztgboR-91DsyN7JHTAxVJ1fElVIhvp58TZ_d5MMSUuDZc9Qs2PBTEQoEAGHdg_mCeZYXQYdNPrZXOHbuu7qtMCGO73x8PBkKlwAKM6jtFjt73tBH3hGVeFgOb9dw8jShSgp468MlCahTLjXyGCqx9lffg8_7TbAsy1jEWrLX3HTPz_QXyQ7BsgpB0SuNny3OJSwRfEHOEkux6CgHYAgtWc56NtcYjjDXssr4TMadJNTxV4flsxWUW3UH-keUY5mMLwRhEMWUDvmVE7Au2rsukIRHum879K7l10CdfQZmqSeyIHaiL_YjWZz1ruSFRV5hwK_ZOakNxE/4ia/joCZBVRwShmUmsJLW8JqeA/h2/h001._YgYQSeH6zop78dE5DoQMjIPBUeLc0Mpqg0TxKSrAOY)**

clean_body: Service may not end today but over the course of the next year we’ll see mass firings of key journalists, programs shut down, and offices shuttered, until nothing is left. | | Friend, Today Congress voted to cut nearly $1.1 billion in federal funding for the Corporation for Public Broadcasting. For NPR, who receives nearly all of their funding from the Corporation for Public Broadcasting this is a death sentence. | Service may not end today but over the course of the next year we'll see mass firings of key journalists, programs shut down, and offices shuttered, until nothing is left. However, if NPR can hunker down for the next year and a half we have a fleeting opportunity to save it: Reclaiming the house. Just like we strive to leave no Dem behind, we cannot leave NPR behind. Team No Dem Left Behind | | Mail checks to: No Dem Left Behind P.O. name: Tobias Read
subject: I want you to hold me accountable
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/read-em-24?refcode=250718em [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/read-em-24?refcode=250718em]
Hi, it's Oregon Secretary of State, Tobias Read, reaching out with an update on how your tax dollars are being spent here in Oregon.
My team at the Audits Division just released a new report that examines how Oregon handles state contracts and purchasing, and here are the three things you need to know:
1. Oregon isn’t doing everything it can to manage contracts, oversee spending, and maximize the value that we get from every tax dollar spent. By shedding light on this, my hope is that Oregon will improve moving forward.

2. We know Oregonians are stretching every dollar they have, and the state should be doing the same. We’ve recommended a slate of strategies to identify and prevent risky contracting decisions before they’re made and before tax dollars are wasted, and to ensure we’re getting more value for our tax dollars in Oregon. You can review those strategies for yourself here [https://sos.oregon.gov/audits/Pages/audit-2025-18-DAS.aspx?fbclid=IwY2xjawLfzeVleHRuA2FlbQIxMABicmlkETFqOExjbm1UV3l0dWhJMWZRAR7KHCyj2j6jP4B1vRp9EpUfSpbBI7s4_50iqIXeHhz_5_X1PLHLO5-A5d3U_A_aem_K0ZdOkZnsWw182Bkzo7loQ] .

3. It’s on all of us —elected leaders, state employees, and the general public—to hold the Oregon government accountable and ensure we’re making progress on our goals. Oregonians and generous and kind, but we need to do much more to build confidence with voters that we are using existing resources prudently.
My team formulated these recommendations because we’re committed to the values I campaigned on — transparency, efficacy, and progress.
That’s why I’m reaching out today with a simple request: Would you take a moment to complete my one-question survey and let me know if you support our efforts to reduce wasteful government spending, improve efficiency, and better manage your tax dollars? [https://secure.ngpvan.com/H94WsVwh8Ua4AIiR33To3g2]
TAKE THE SURVEY [https://secure.ngpvan.com/H94WsVwh8Ua4AIiR33To3g2]
Thank you,
Tobias Read
P.S. Don’t forget! The Oregon Political Tax Credit allows you to donate $50 (if you file single) or $100 (if you file joint) to a candidate, PAC, or party, which will be fully credited back as a rebate — so it’s free to donate! For more information, click here. [https://www.oregon.gov/omd/ONG/Documents/SMFS/Personal%20Financial%20Counselor/Political-Tax-Credit.pdf?]
CONTRIBUTE [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/read-em-24?refcode=250718EM]
https://www.facebook.com/TobiasReadOR [https://www.facebook.com/TobiasReadOR] https://twitter.com/TobiasRead [https://twitter.com/TobiasRead] https://instagram.com/tobiasreador [https://instagram.com/tobiasreador]

Friends of Tobias Read (5208)
PO Box 42307
Portland, OR 97242
United States
If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please unsubscribe: https://secure.ngpvan.com/p/IMahE5FQZEyEjGesjn_XSA2?unsubscribedata=CkIGnVJm6iIs%2FkMcD9kDp1BNITIaA4UowVE1UVxN1Xqu40HCxyHQQrrOwFvM5SB%2BzlNgrCSTNB4tsKFhVpXclXH%2FTk%2BT5I5KT7jLqRVgr%2BVUZMqMiOfL8lw7cp1q3ZCxCR3NivNZQxjCOVhM3LpBeCu6Giu3h1Xs7EbQ4NYmfHAqXrPTDIp7gF1udHT9QQj7n6hK%2Bcc7Mr2NbOpZUN3sdQt%2F0MyqWq5QVPr2xXnOZ2RUwVcOkq842kJ7GxT2MaqFavCMYvK%2B7%2BIPUTH5SHusnJ78fhZnh%2Bh%2BVE7D4ZtPkpVxoHn6nKql3QxT%2F36E4FVtd3%2BAihaLxC3Q8Bf2f6DLQxTVhvQXeNHDmLXRTMowhcciYRzuTjeI8QOEgvp7o3%2FjRgtT2k2%2FqP3ObDx%2FZ2pKRMeUpV%2BpCSqzEw5SrjpFwLCCGZzs1tgi95ki0hGq%2Fv1F .

name: With Honor Action
subject: 💪 Stronger Support for Troops, Smarter Security for America
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY WITH HONOR ACTION, INC
body:
| | | | | [View this email in your browser](https://us16.campaign-archive.com/?e=33f53a5b1a&u=80940e6d1b1e08928d721cf8d&id=2f8a53f26b)
---
| | | |
---
| | | | [ ](https://withhonor.us16.list-manage.com/track/click?u=80940e6d1b1e08928d721cf8d&id=4acd39a9e9&e=33f53a5b1a)
---
| Friends,

This week, the House Armed Services Committee passed the annual defense bill with strong bipartisan support—a big step toward making sure we take care of our service members and their families while continuing to modernize the Department of Defense.

For the first time, biotechnology is being recognized as just as important as artificial intelligence and robotics in shaping the future of our national security. Thanks to the [recommendations](https://withhonor.us16.list-manage.com/track/click?u=80940e6d1b1e08928d721cf8d&id=132294d3e9&e=33f53a5b1a) of the National Security Commission on Emerging Biotechnology—like [this one ](https://withhonor.us16.list-manage.com/track/click?u=80940e6d1b1e08928d721cf8d&id=199cc53375&e=33f53a5b1a)championed by For Country Caucus Co-Chair Rep. Don Davis—the Department of Defense will be able to safely and quickly advance new biotech innovations. These include creating stronger, more reliable supply chains, improving protection against biological threats, and finding new ways to make essential materials right here at home.

With Honor Action's other major priorities, which are in the bill, include:

📈 A 3.8% pay raise for all service members.
💵 The expansion of eligibility for the Basic Needs Allowance for low-income service members.
🪖 Provisions to modernize the DoD's acquisition and procurement processes.
🇺🇦 Continued support for our Ukrainian allies.

It’s worth noting that 18 of the 37 veteran members of the For Country Caucus serve on this critical defense committee, demonstrating their commitment to thoughtful, bipartisan policymaking. It’s a powerful reminder of how important it is to have veterans’ leadership and experience in Congress.
---
|
---
|
# **With Honor Co-Founder & CEO Rye Barcott reflects on David Gergen's life and advice on SiriusXM**
---
| [ ](https://withhonor.us16.list-manage.com/track/click?u=80940e6d1b1e08928d721cf8d&id=228e7ab3de&e=33f53a5b1a)
---
| With Honor's co-founder, David Gergen, was "a cut above." An advisor to four Presidents of different parties, a mentor to hundreds of changemakers across the world, and a dear friend and father, David embodied the principled leadership that With Honor asks of all our public officials.

Yesterday, With Honor Co-Founder & CEO Rye Barcott joined Michael Smerconish on SiriusXM to talk about David's mentorship. A piece of advice? Leadership at its best is service to others.

🔗 You can listen to the full interview [here](https://withhonor.us16.list-manage.com/track/click?u=80940e6d1b1e08928d721cf8d&id=18777e3a45&e=33f53a5b1a).
---
|
---
|
# **With Honor Action joins 16 other national bipartisan organizations for a conversation in Washington, D.C.**
---
|
---
| On Wednesday, With Honor Action joined the leadership of the Problem Solvers Caucus, Representatives Brian Fitzpatrick (R-PA) and Tom Suozzi (D-NY), to discuss making bipartisanship a national priority.

We highlighted the vital role that veteran leaders play in bridging divides and advancing principled solutions that put country over party. The conversation focused on building consensus around critical areas such as national security, supporting veterans and their families, and strengthening democratic institutions, underscoring a shared commitment to restoring trust and effectiveness in government through cooperation rather than division.
---
|
---

and consider forwarding this to a friend who may be interested in learning -- and doing -- more for our country.

name: Mazie Hirono
subject: The Supreme Court gave Republicans the green light
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Mazie Hirono For Hawaii**
body:
|
#
---
Peter, If you’re a billionaire, Republicans will cater to your every need. But if you’re an average American like the other 99.99 percent of us, then they don’t give a rip about you. Donald Trump came into office promising to give billionaires a massive tax break, and he set his sights on eliminating the Department of Education to pay for it. Not only did he try to fire thousands of employees and slash spending, but he also directed his Secretary of Education to completely abolish the agency. While his plans were temporarily halted due to a court order, **the Supreme Court just gave him the green light to continue his assault on public education.** Let’s be clear about this, Peter — it won’t be Trump or any of his allies who suffer the consequences. It will be our children. I will not sit quietly by while Republicans try to rob our children of their ability to receive a quality education. That’s why I’m fighting every day in the Senate to protect the American people from their dangerous ideas. **[But I can’t do it without you. clean_body: | Peter, If you’re a billionaire, Republicans will cater to your every need. But if you’re an average American like the other 99.99 percent of us, then they don’t give a rip about you. Donald Trump came into office promising to give billionaires a massive tax break, and he set his sights on eliminating the Department of Education to pay for it. Not only did he try to fire thousands of employees and slash spending, but he also directed his Secretary of Education to completely abolish the agency. While his plans were temporarily halted due to a court order, the Supreme Court just gave him the green light to continue his assault on public education. Let’s be clear about this, Peter — it won’t be Trump or any of his allies who suffer the consequences. It will be our children. I will not sit quietly by while Republicans try to rob our children of their ability to receive a quality education. That’s why I’m fighting every day in the Senate to protect the American people from their dangerous ideas. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | = = | = = | = = Thanks for standing with me, Mazie Friends of Mazie Hirono 335 Merchant Street #677 Honolulu, HI 96809 United States mailto:info@mazieforhawaii.com | Paid for by Mazie Hirono For Hawaii

subject: ✏️ Petition: Call on Congress to save FEMA now!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jane Fonda Climate PAC, not authorized by any candidate or candidate’s committee
body:
Trump is dismantling the agency that saves lives during disasters.

| [](https://click.everyaction.com/k/111721155/558176120/1328794144?refcode=ea.754296cb-0c64-f011-8dc9-6045bdfe8e9c&ms=ea.754296cb-0c64-f011-8dc9-6045bdfe8e9c&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzk2NzgyIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjc1NDI5NmNiLTBjNjQtZjAxMS04ZGM5LTYwNDViZGZlOGU5YyIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=TCRDozTUwpn4Fa_27OKfBU01m98AD4l6zr5efJM81cY=&emci=225f81c5-0c64-f011-8dc9-6045bdfe8e9c&emdi=754296cb-0c64-f011-8dc9-6045bdfe8e9c&ceid=4823105)
---
**_CNN:_ Trump says he plans to phase out FEMA after 2025 hurricane season** **[Abolishing FEMA would be devastating for millions of Americans who rely on this life-saving agency for climate disasters. We’re counting on 1,500 JanePAC supporters to sign our petition to demand Congress fully fund FEMA! >>>](https://click.everyaction.com/k/111721156/558176121/-1288763417?source=direct_link&referrer=group-jfcp-woa&redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fsecure.actblue.com%2Fdonate%2Fjane-bax-email-ja%3Frefcode%3Dea.754296cb-0c64-f011-8dc9-6045bdfe8e9c%26ms%3Dea.754296cb-0c64-f011-8dc9-6045bdfe8e9c&refcode=ea.754296cb-0c64-f011-8dc9-6045bdfe8e9c&ms=ea.754296cb-0c64-f011-8dc9-6045bdfe8e9c&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzk2NzgyIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjc1NDI5NmNiLTBjNjQtZjAxMS04ZGM5LTYwNDViZGZlOGU5YyIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=TCRDozTUwpn4Fa_27OKfBU01m98AD4l6zr5efJM81cY=&emci=225f81c5-0c64-f011-8dc9-6045bdfe8e9c&emdi=754296cb-0c64-f011-8dc9-6045bdfe8e9c&ceid=4823105)** | [**SIGN ON →**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/111721157/558176122/-1288763417?source=direct_link&referrer=group-jfcp-woa&redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fsecure.actblue.com%2Fdonate%2Fjane-bax-email-ja%3Frefcode%3Dea.754296cb-0c64-f011-8dc9-6045bdfe8e9c%26ms%3Dea.754296cb-0c64-f011-8dc9-6045bdfe8e9c&refcode=ea.754296cb-0c64-f011-8dc9-6045bdfe8e9c&ms=ea.754296cb-0c64-f011-8dc9-6045bdfe8e9c&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzk2NzgyIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjc1NDI5NmNiLTBjNjQtZjAxMS04ZGM5LTYwNDViZGZlOGU5YyIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=TCRDozTUwpn4Fa_27OKfBU01m98AD4l6zr5efJM81cY=&emci=225f81c5-0c64-f011-8dc9-6045bdfe8e9c&emdi=754296cb-0c64-f011-8dc9-6045bdfe8e9c&ceid=4823105)
---
* * *
While Texas communities reel from flash floods and search for missing loved ones, Trump is actively working to destroy the very agency that delivers billions of dollars in assistance to communities devastated by natural disasters.
Trump's plan to eliminate FEMA is following a predictable playbook: First, sabotage the agency, then argue it's beyond repair. Here's the damage Trump and his DHS Secretary Kristi Noem have already done to gut FEMA:
**❌ They fired nearly a quarter of FEMA's full-time staff

❌ They made it impossible for FEMA to act quickly by requiring Noem's personal sign-off on spending over $100,000, contributing to slower response times during the Texas disaster

❌ They allowed thousands of call center workers to be laid off after the flood, ensuring desperate calls for help went unanswered

❌ And they installed an acting director who has been notably absent from disaster response efforts**
This couldn't be more reckless: Trump wants to eliminate FEMA just as climate disasters are becoming deadlier and more frequent. Despite the fact that 8 in 10 Americans support federal disaster relief, Trump would rather leave families to fend for themselves than provide the assistance that saves lives.
**[We can't let them get away with this. Only Congress has the power to stop Trump's reckless plan to shutter FEMA, and that's why we need to demand action. clean_body: Trump is dismantling the agency that saves lives during disasters. | CNN: Trump says he plans to phase out FEMA after 2025 hurricane season | While Texas communities reel from flash floods and search for missing loved ones, Trump is actively working to destroy the very agency that delivers billions of dollars in assistance to communities devastated by natural disasters. Trump's plan to eliminate FEMA is following a predictable playbook: First, sabotage the agency, then argue it's beyond repair. Here's the damage Trump and his DHS Secretary Kristi Noem have already done to gut FEMA: ❌ They fired nearly a quarter of FEMA's full-time staff ❌ They made it impossible for FEMA to act quickly by requiring Noem's personal sign-off on spending over $100,000, contributing to slower response times during the Texas disaster ❌ They allowed thousands of call center workers to be laid off after the flood, ensuring desperate calls for help went unanswered ❌ And they installed an acting director who has been notably absent from disaster response efforts This couldn't be more reckless: Trump wants to eliminate FEMA just as climate disasters are becoming deadlier and more frequent. Despite the fact that 8 in 10 Americans support federal disaster relief, Trump would rather leave families to fend for themselves than provide the assistance that saves lives. In solidarity, Team JanePAC | = | = = Follow along: | | | | | ---|---|---|---|---|--- Paid for by Jane Fonda Climate PAC, not authorized by any candidate or candidate’s committee. Jane Fonda Climate PAC 600 Pennsylvania Ave SE #15180 Washington, DC 20003 United States

name: Christina Bohannan
subject: The science behind our national parks
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY CHRISTINA BOHANNAN FOR CONGRESS
body:
| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111721207/558176288/-975735282?refcode=em_logoheader&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjIyOCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5MjMxYmFlYi0wYzY0LWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=4-5sR545x8CTfHM5ha6OPByCxi9wTm1ZFpUTvTP0JbE=)
Peter, before I was a law professor, I was an environmental engineer.

I chose that path because I believed deeply — and still do — that every generation deserves to inherit a planet that is healthy, thriving, and full of wonder. **That’s why I want to talk to you today about something close to my heart: our national parks.**

This month, we celebrate National Parks and Recreation Month. But while families are enjoying these public treasures, Mariannette Miller-Meeks and her buddies are quietly working to dismantle the very systems that protect them.

Our national parks and public lands are more than hiking trails and scenic views. Scientifically, they’re _essential infrastructure_ in the fight against climate change. They protect carbon-rich forests that absorb greenhouse gases while also preserving biodiversity and endangered species. The plant life within is critical to filtering the air we breathe and the water we drink. At the same time, the parks service also protects important landmarks and monuments – including four monuments and heritage sites here in Iowa.

Mariannatte Miller-Meeks has supported a more than 24% cut to the Park Service’s workforce. She voted for a tax bill that will _take back over $200 million_ in funds already set aside for the National Parks Service. And some of her friends want to go even further by privatizing or selling off public lands to the highest bidder. _I will not stand for that._

We cannot afford to stand by while corporate interests sell off our future. We need leaders in Congress who will protect the environment and our national parks. I'm ready to be that leader, but I need your support.
**[Will you make a donation to help me defeat Mariannette Miller-Meeks and fight for our parks, our planet, and our future?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111721208/558176289/-1612730695?refcode=em25_250718_FRNpak_fnd_gq_act___CB_ettx&utm_campaign=em25_250718_FRNpak_fnd_gq_act___CB_ettx&utm_medium=email&refcode2=9231baeb-0c64-f011-8dc9-6045bdfe8e9c&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjIyOCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5MjMxYmFlYi0wYzY0LWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=4-5sR545x8CTfHM5ha6OPByCxi9wTm1ZFpUTvTP0JbE=)** **[Chip in now →](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111721209/558176290/-1612730695?refcode=em25_250718_FRNpak_fnd_gq_act___CB_ettx&utm_campaign=em25_250718_FRNpak_fnd_gq_act___CB_ettx&utm_medium=email&refcode2=9231baeb-0c64-f011-8dc9-6045bdfe8e9c&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjIyOCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5MjMxYmFlYi0wYzY0LWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=4-5sR545x8CTfHM5ha6OPByCxi9wTm1ZFpUTvTP0JbE=)**

clean_body: | = Peter, before I was a law professor, I was an environmental engineer. I chose that path because I believed deeply — and still do — that every generation deserves to inherit a planet that is healthy, thriving, and full of wonder. That’s why I want to talk to you today about something close to my heart: our national parks. This month, we celebrate National Parks and Recreation Month. But while families are enjoying these public treasures, Mariannette Miller-Meeks and her buddies are quietly working to dismantle the very systems that protect them. Our national parks and public lands are more than hiking trails and scenic views. Scientifically, they’re essential infrastructure in the fight against climate change. They protect carbon-rich forests that absorb greenhouse gases while also preserving biodiversity and endangered species. The plant life within is critical to filtering the air we breathe and the water we drink. At the same time, the parks service also protects important landmarks and monuments – including four monuments and heritage sites here in Iowa. Mariannatte Miller-Meeks has supported a more than 24% cut to the Park Service’s workforce. She voted for a tax bill that will take back over $200 million in funds already set aside for the National Parks Service. And some of her friends want to go even further by privatizing or selling off public lands to the highest bidder. I will not stand for that. We cannot afford to stand by while corporate interests sell off our future. We need leaders in Congress who will protect the environment and our national parks. I'm ready to be that leader, but I need your support. = = Thank you, Christina Christina Bohannan is a mom, law professor, former engineer and state representative. She's running for Congress to defeat Mariannette Miller-Meeks and flip IA-01 blue. Chip in to support her campaign today>> | = = | PAID FOR BY CHRISTINA BOHANNAN FOR CONGRESS P.O. Box 722 Iowa City, IA 52240 If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please .

In the middle of the night, Republicans in the House voted to cut more than $1 BILLION from public media networks like PBS + NPR
#
**In the middle of the night, Republicans in the House voted to cut more than $1 BILLION from public media networks like PBS + NPR.**
This move is expected to shutter public radio + TV stations across the country — especially in rural areas, where these networks are often the only widely-available source of local news.
**Peter, we can’t let this attack on the First Amendment go unanswered.**
We need to spend every day holding the GOP accountable for it. And we need to elect Democrats who will stand up for everyday Americans by fiercely protecting public media.

name: Jena Griswold
subject: I won’t flinch
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jena for Colorado
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/2507-q3-jg?refcode=250717-email [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/2507-q3-jg?refcode=250717-email]
Peter –
I want to share something personal with you.
I grew up in a working-class family in Estes Park. There were times we relied on food stamps to put food on the table. I was the first in my family to attend a four-year college, and later, I became a lawyer because I wanted to fight for people, like my family, who worked hard and deserved a fair chance.
That’s why I’ve spent my career in public service—fighting for voting rights, standing up to corruption, and defending our democracy.
Now, I’m running to be Colorado’s next Attorney General because I believe we need someone who won’t back down.
Whether it’s protecting abortion access, keeping our communities safe, or taking on powerful special interests—I’ll never stop doing what’s right for Coloradans.
If you believe in that vision, I’m asking for your support. A donation today—no matter the size—helps us build a campaign rooted in justice, fairness, and courage. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/2507-q3-jg?refcode=250717-email]
Thank you for being in this fight with me.
Jena

name: Rudy Giuliani
subject: The hardest (& most personal) letter I’ve ever written
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Rudy Giuliani Freedom Fund Legal Defense Trust
body:
Waiting for your signature.
| [](http://links.conservativesdefendingfreedom.com/u/click?_t=91d01aa68bbe4105984439520ca0f0af&_m=7a6e711635b5431d9b40565211059e92&_e=d2PZde_2FsDGmDmyxuoFOUWv07mIoB-V90l5vxjf_iGtoGcy64pLsHkL9_nNkBcKUr_6vYJgIvMnMTTKmVSrH-GTz-R-FSId3scK_FQslQViuepjiDaazvOiSyjksd07HFM8s0duwL7nDeMzeYG_Ntw3K_b1cyhAKpWel5QZ2F36jbHstxZ2-gUQsWq7BSD1IyE4_EYWDUrIsVOvHTCeXn6VjmymfmjO2OmHzBPYYmx3qwPa11gq2JFrk9yLbMS4ziYbeOdRj2tB-KXyjZP02njpaJ9KBWfPD4-rnNW9-p4fpAEgpBdzY1P21STlR26JaI87pQq-Dnwgo32v34IPL-XWO4SvzH3f7MhRh1mUNrnmD4bOLL649lesW-XvH8PtAG2sCT3hgX0vNzzfixXnylkVvB00p0AapMC3tizW_WCjkd0ZGtmNE1EG-FnKRx8pq0XD_fIyiiAXgWOfeFab5RnVWW8e3bv0d5s4rY4kJ1X9fKx1p4Bq30znDnE2EzHCMW91JH8QKc0HpGQO2CrA-um5XWUOy6dyo4V17-OWaITWykj1V0fq2HXSyrelMtF9Yf3n50D_0fzjBYVZXani26ZJ3sb6Y2T8rCM4n2XTwQFrCQLWMHtNgRGYnskCTJIfBTPZydJj73kpRuvoByuHNg%3D%3D)
---
Dear Friend,
---
The Radical Democrats think they’ve finally got me.
---
**You see, a Democrat Attorney General is making me stand in a rigged trial for bogus state-level crimes, because she knows that not even the most powerful man on the face of the planet – President Trump – can save me with a _federal_ pardon in _state_ court.**
---
_I’m left with no escape and no lifeline, especially after they’ve already STRIPPED me of my law licenses!_
---
But even though the most powerful man in the country legally cannot step in to save me from being **JAILED** for the rest of my life, the most powerful force absolutely can: _THE AMERICAN PEOPLE_.
---
With my back against the wall, I’m turning to the _ONE_ _THING_ that can save me: _**the collective voices of MILLIONS of patriots like YOU.**_
---

clean_body: Waiting for your signature. | Dear Friend, The Radical Democrats think they’ve finally got me. You see, a Democrat Attorney General is making me stand in a rigged trial for bogus state-level crimes, because she knows that not even the most powerful man on the face of the planet – President Trump – can save me with a federal pardon in state court. I’m left with no escape and no lifeline, especially after they’ve already STRIPPED me of my law licenses! But even though the most powerful man in the country legally cannot step in to save me from being JAILED for the rest of my life, the most powerful force absolutely can: THE AMERICAN PEOPLE. With my back against the wall, I’m turning to the ONE THING that can save me: the collective voices of MILLIONS of patriots like YOU. I’ve taken on mobsters. I’ve faced terrorists. I’ve battled corruption. I’ve been through it all. Or so I thought... Never did I imagine that I would be the centerpiece of one of the Democrats’ most vindictive plots in history. This entire plan by the Deep State to destroy a close friend of the President’s is truly the type of sinister scheme you’d think would only exist in the movies. But believe me, it exists; I’m living it! And if I fail to win this trial and defeat these sham charges, the Democrats will be able to jail me for the rest of my years on God’s green Earth. These Deep State thugs know I have no pardon coming. No protection. No cavalry coming over the hill... ...But what they continue to underestimate time and again is the collective strength of millions of faithful patriots like you who also REFUSE to surrender to the Left’s tyranny. They entered my mugshot under booking #2313757, so my goal is for 2,313,757 patriots to sign my official petition — ” — calling for my freedom and to DROP ALL CHARGES. name: Team Hall for Congress
subject: A lot of eyes are on this race
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY HEIDI HALL FOR CONGRESS
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/hall-email?refcode=20250718_em_fr_b1_THH_BIG [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/hall-email?refcode=20250718_em_fr_b1_THH_BIG]
Friend, it's Heidi Hall's team.
The stakes of this race have not been lost on anyone.
With Democrats needing to regain only three seats to take back control of the House, we're seeing more people get involved nationally— from both sides.
Take it from Benjamin Ray, who runs the federal campaigns team for EMILYs List:
“This district (CA-03) is trending Democratic quickly, and the right candidate with a strong message focused on Californians will have the opportunity to replace Congressman Kiley.”
And the Democratic Congressional Campaign Committee (DCCC) has been looking to support qualified candidates who are running to flip key seats throughout the country, our race VERY MUCH included.
But the GOP is taking notice of how important this seat is as well.
House Speaker Mike Johnson and his Grow the Majority PAC have already given Heidi's opponent hundreds of thousands of dollars in campaign contributions, and the closer we get to flipping this seat, the more he will get.
Friend, it's important that we're able to show national groups like EMILYs List and the DCCC that we have the grassroots support that's needed to win this election and that we can keep up with Kiley's fundraising numbers.
Our communities are tired of toxic D.C. politics that only serve to benefit the wealthy elite and leave working people like us behind. That's why Heidi is running to represent you, not special interests. Can you please make a grassroots donation to her campaign for Congress today? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/hall-email?refcode=20250718_em_fr_b1_THH_BIG]
MAKE A GRASSROOTS DONATION [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/hall-email?refcode=20250718_em_fr_b1_THH_BIG]
In solidarity,
Team Hall for Congress
Heidi Hall has spent her three-decade public service career working with others to solve problems, protect our land and water, improve health, and build opportunities for others, all while raising two boys primarily as a single mom.
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/hall-email?refcode=20250718_em_fr_b1_THH_BIG [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/hall-email?refcode=20250718_em_fr_b1_THH_BIG]
Raised by parents who had persisted to make a middle-class life for their kids in Contra Costa County while lifting up others, Heidi was taught to push through and overcome challenges rather than complain about them. She learned to stick up for the vulnerable, to respect working people, and to value equal opportunity and justice.
After graduating from Pomona College and earning graduate degrees from Columbia and Rutgers, Heidi went to work for the Rockefeller Foundation promoting reproductive care and then began her career at the Environmental Protection Agency, stopping the dumping of toxic waste and holding corporate polluters accountable.
Today, Heidi chairs the Nevada County Board of Supervisors, where she’s taken on homelessness, expanded broadband access, and worked to reduce crime and illegal cannabis growth. Heidi flipped her seat from red to blue – and now she’s ready to do it again in Congress to take on toxic politics in Washington and put people first.
Prefer to donate via mail? Address a check to:
Heidi Hall for Congress
PO Box 729
Grass Valley, CA 95945
SUPPORT HEIDI [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/hall-email?refcode=20250718_em_fr_b1_THH_BIG]
PAID FOR BY HEIDI HALL FOR CONGRESS

Heidi Hall for Congress
PO Box 729
Grass Valley, CA 95945
United States

subject: Everyone deserves access to health care
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jimmy Panetta for Congress 2024, FEC ID# C00592154
body:
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111722403/558180255/-2113420158?source=EM_NBI_JP_20250718_FR_BBB_0_6MD_TeamPanetta_NA_NA_Hospitals&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MTkwMSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyMTUyNzgxZS0xMDY0LWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=V6r2d1PM1-Wt8z6ez3pn7IgWbnQdYzB0apOQEz3YyEI=&emci=23ded68e-d160-f011-8dc9-6045bdfe8e9c&emdi=2152781e-1064-f011-8dc9-6045bdfe8e9c&ceid=6108787) | Peter,

When Speaker Mike Johnson’s Majority passed the President's reconciliation tax bill that made huge cuts to Medicaid, they threatened access to healthcare for many families across the country.

**Rural hospitals are most at risk of closure — and California has 53 of them!**

This means California families will lose emergency rooms and inpatient care. When hospitals close, communities suffer and healthcare deserts get bigger.

**_[That’s why Jimmy voted NO. name: IlhanOmar.com
subject: A historic event
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY ILHAN FOR CONGRESS
body:
For the first time, all four Muslim members of Congress are coming together
# [](https://act.ilhanomar.com/go/35?t=1&akid=1722%2E791427%2EHaVhOJ)

Friend — This month Ilhan will be taking part in a historic event:
For the first time, all four Muslim members of Congress are coming together for **A Night of Muslim Power,** a celebration of Muslim representation across the country and the Muslim community’s role in building power for the progressive movement.
We are reaching out to this team today to ask for your help in raising funds to support our great Muslim members of Congress and their work to fight the Trump administration:
**[Make a contribution to the Empowerment Fund today to support Ilhan Omar, Rashida Tlaib, Andre Carson, and Lateefah Simon as they work each and every day to bring our progressive values and ideas to Congress.**](https://act.ilhanomar.com/go/7?c0=io-em-july25-empowerment-fund.K5flbX&refcode=em250718-1-90&t=2&akid=1722%2E791427%2EHaVhOJ)
**[CONTRIBUTE NOW](https://act.ilhanomar.com/go/7?c0=io-em-july25-empowerment-fund.K5flbX&refcode=em250718-1-90&t=3&akid=1722%2E791427%2EHaVhOJ)**
Keith Ellison became the first Muslim member of Congress in 2006, at a time when Muslims in America faced intensified discrimination and many challenges. Keith was followed shortly thereafter by Andre Carson in 2008.
Ilhan Omar and Rashida Tlaib then made history in 2018 as the first Muslim women to represent their communities in Congress — defying the odds and sending a message to the far-right, the media, and the entire establishment.
Now, as we face down constant threats to our communities from the Trump administration, it’s more important than ever that we organize to protect the progress that has been made.
Especially as Muslim leaders and voices are leading the way on the pressing issues of our time, from immigration, to the cost of living, to US involvement in the genocide in Gaza and in needless wars around the world.
So please: **[Show your support for Ilhan Omar and her fellow progressive Muslim leaders ahead of this historic event by making a contribution to the Empowerment Fund. Every dollar makes a difference when everyone does their part.**](https://act.ilhanomar.com/go/7?c0=io-em-july25-empowerment-fund.K5flbX&refcode=em250718-1-90&t=4&akid=1722%2E791427%2EHaVhOJ)

name: Rob Bonta
subject: Add your name: ICE should not have access to private health data
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY ROB BONTA FOR CALIFORNIA ATTORNEY GENERAL 2026 FPPC ID #145642]
body:
[ https://act.robbonta.com/go/4?refcode=em250718&t=1001&akid=1886%2E1088799%2EL0B7_7 ]Rob Bonta

The Associated Press is now reporting that the
Trump administration is illegally providing the personal data of Medicaid
recipients to Immigration and Customs Enforcement.

I’m deeply disturbed by this.

When we received early reports of illegal data sharing earlier in the
month, I took immediate action and filed a lawsuit against the
administration to stop them. Despite that, it appears that the Trump
administration has entered a new illegal data sharing agreement with ICE.
Trump is trying to use personal, private, and sensitive data to build a
mass deportation machine — we can’t allow it to continue.

This is horrifying, illegal, and a violation of our privacy. [ https://act.robbonta.com/survey/em-250718-pet/?t=1002&akid=1886%2E1088799%2EL0B7_7 ]If you
agree we need to stop Trump from breaking the law to fuel his
anti-immigrant agenda, please add your name to my petition now.

ADD YOUR NAME

I just went on MSNBC to talk about this issue and make sure everyone knows
about this unprecedented move from the federal government — you can watch
the interview [ https://act.robbonta.com/go/771?t=1003&akid=1886%2E1088799%2EL0B7_7 ]here.

[ https://act.robbonta.com/survey/em-250718-pet/?t=1004&akid=1886%2E1088799%2EL0B7_7 ]AG Rob Bonta on MSNBC talking about the illegal data sharing agreement
between HHS and ICE

It’s devastating to think that people across California and the country
may be too afraid to seek the medical care they need out of fear that they
will be targeted by Trump and ICE.

We cannot allow this to continue. I’m moving as quickly as possible to
secure a court order blocking this unlawful and unprecedented data sharing
agreement with ICE. Updates to come.

Thanks for standing up and speaking out with me.

-Rob

Rob Bonta
Attorney General of California

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with grassroots supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:

Receive Fewer Emails: https://act.robbonta.com/signup/receive-fewer-emails/?t=1005&akid=1886%2E1088799%2EL0B7_7
Update Your Information: https://act.robbonta.com/signup/update-your-information/
Unsubscribe: https://act.robbonta.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=1006&akid=1886%2E1088799%2EL0B7_7

Rob Bonta for Attorney General 2026
c/o Deane & Company
1700 Tribute Road, Suite 201
Sacramento, CA 95815
info@robbonta.com

Fellow Patriot,

For the American left, if you burn down whole city blocks and riot, you'll probably get a financial settlement.

If you invade the country, NGOs will pay your legal bills as you enjoy free housing and debit cards filled with taxpayer cash.

Non-Contribution Account (For TTPAC, Inc.'s Expenditures Other Than Direct Contributions to Federal Committees)

As permitted by law, TTPAC, Inc. maintains a non-contribution account for the purpose of influencing federal, state, and local elections. Federal law requires us to use our best efforts to collect and report the name, address, occupation, and employer of individuals whose contribution exceeds $200 in an election cycle. For contributions from federal committees, corporations, and labor unions - regardless of contribution amount - federal law requires us to use our best efforts to collect and report the committee's name and address. State and local law may require us to collect and report additional information.

Contributions to this account may be used to influence federal elections by making independent expenditures in support of or in opposition to federal candidates. Additionally, in state and local jurisdictions that permit non-individual type (e.g., corporate) contributions directly to candidates and/or committees, contributions to this account may be used for both independent expenditures and direct contributions to candidates and/or committees, as permitted by state and local law.

Individuals, federal committees, corporations, and labor unions may contribute to TTPAC, Inc.'s non-contribution account without limit. Contributions by national banks, foreign nationals (without lawful permanent resident status), and other foreign entities (e.g., foreign corporations) are prohibited. Contributions to TTPAC, Inc.'s non-contribution account are not tax deductible for federal income tax purposes.

clean_body: Fellow Patriot, For the American left, if you burn down whole city blocks and riot, you'll probably get a financial settlement. If you invade the country, NGOs will pay your legal bills as you enjoy free housing and debit cards filled with taxpayer cash. If you wave the Mexican flag while assaulting law enforcement, the news will call it a " potent sign of protest." If you burn the American flag, they'll call it free speech. But if you dare tear down a Pride flag, you face years in prison. This demonstrates the lunacy of the radical left. It's also what's at stake if we rest on our laurels instead of taking action to win in 2026, 2028, and beyond... Turning Point PAC has a plan to hold the line! We have identified 3 battleground states where we can have the most impact: Arizona, Nevada, and New Hampshire. By establishing field offices and ramping up field operations in those states, to protect our America First victories. To do so, we'll need to raise $75,000 before the end of the month to fund this mission. for a copy of my new book, Right Wing Revolution. for a copy of my new book and a President Trump lithograph. for a President Trump lithograph and a SIGNED copy of Right Wing Revolution. I know this is no small ask, but this is no small operation. Winning in Arizona, Nevada, and New Hampshire is critical to the enduring success of President Trump 's America First agenda. These states have a number of important factors that make them the perfect targets for our grassroots ground game. 1. They are considered contested states that are important to both parties. 2. These states are growing. 3. Republicans outnumber Democrats. Plus, if we win these states, we don't have to worry about winning in other states like Michigan, Pennsylvania, or Wisconsin. That's the truth. Only three states stand between us and a series of America First victories in 2026, 2028, and beyond. Please help us and secure an America First future by donating today. For America, Charlie Kirk Turning Point PAC P.S. Now that you've heard our plan, I want you to understand what is at stake if we fail: our Republic. Everything President Trump has achieved... undone. Our America First movement... finished. Our freedom... surrendered. The future of America... in peril! We CANNOT let that happen. Peter, it's official: we're a month into the general election.
I'm ready to flip the Governor's office blue this November, defeat Trump's agenda at the ballot box, and get to work creating a more affordable Commonwealth for all.
But one lesson I learned during my years in Congress and at CIA is that a strong team beats going at it alone every time, so I hope you will join my campaign today.
[_**Will you support my campaign for Governor by making our suggested $25 contribution? In a few short months, we can start Democrats on a winning streak by flipping Virginia blue.
_It's not going to be easy._
Four years ago, we lost this same Governor's race by _two points_ and saw years of progress erased in an instant. And my opponent Winsome Earle-Sears has shown she will indulge in fearmongering and disinformation to keep Virginia under Republican control.
Your contribution today, whatever the amount, will help us rise above the attacks and build a winning campaign that reflects our shared values.
If you're fed up with the chaos coming out of Washington, if you're outraged watching GOP officials bend their knee rather than uphold their oath, I am counting on your immediate and full support.
[_**Click this link to complete your donation to my campaign for Governor.**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111722680/558181147/830632682?refcode=BLU_EM_ASFG_2025-07-18_2&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjQvMS8xMDM4NTgiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiNzgzOWQzM2YtMTE2NC1mMDExLThkYzktNjA0NWJkZmU4ZTljIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=rm4mh4kFl4KGIL7VsMyTv7jbbTmRVELfm07iQGYYMK4=) Together, we will flip Virginia blue and send a resounding message to this White House and beyond that voters will not stand for the corruption, the attacks on fundamental rights, and the brazen disregard for the needs of working families.
