clean_body: I hope you will join my campaign today! = Peter, it's official: we're a month into the general election. I'm ready to flip the Governor's office blue this November, defeat Trump's agenda at the ballot box, and get to work creating a more affordable Commonwealth for all. But one lesson I learned during my years in Congress and at CIA is that a strong team beats going at it alone every time, so I hope you will join my campaign today. = = = = = = = In a few short months, we can start Democrats on a winning streak by flipping Virginia blue. It's not going to be easy. Four years ago, we lost this same Governor's race by two points and saw years of progress erased in an instant. And my opponent Winsome Earle-Sears has shown she will indulge in fearmongering and disinformation to keep Virginia under Republican control. Your contribution today, whatever the amount, will help us rise above the attacks and build a winning campaign that reflects our shared values. If you're fed up with the chaos coming out of Washington, if you're outraged watching GOP officials bend their knee rather than uphold their oath, I am counting on your immediate and full support. = Together, we will flip Virginia blue and send a resounding message to this White House and beyond that voters will not stand for the corruption, the attacks on fundamental rights, and the brazen disregard for the needs of working families. = I'm honored by the trust you are placing in me. Virginia is where I grew up and where I'm raising my three girls, and I refuse to let far-right extremism win the day. With immense gratitude, Abigail Spanberger By submitting your phone number you're agreeing to receive periodic text messages from the Spanberger for Governor campaign. Message & data rates may apply. Text HELP for more info. Text STOP to stop receiving messages. . Paid for by Spanberger for Governor Spanberger for Governor P.O. Box 3121 Glen Allen, VA 23058 United States

name: Rep. Jeremy Shaffer
subject: Staying in the fight and Seizing the Moment
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Jeremy Shaffer
body:
July 18 @ 4pm

| | | | |
---
| [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/83cdf012721f/im-running-and-i-need-your-support-12929307?e=8c80cba7e7)
---
[|
---](https://voteshaffer.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=5a4998b6ce4b01517c474232e&id=17ac6310c5&e=8c80cba7e7)
| | | | | Hello Friends,
I hope this note finds you well. I wanted to share some important developments out of Harrisburg. Earlier this week, House Democrats passed their proposed 2025–2026 spending plan. I hesitate to call it a true "budget"—because it lacks long-term responsibility and simply outlines how they intend to spend taxpayer dollars. Once again, we're seeing how easy some in government find it to spend other people’s money without considering the consequences.
Here’s the bottom line: this plan increases state spending far beyond what our revenues can support. If enacted, it would create a staggering $5.3 billion deficit—putting us on a path toward draining our reserves and triggering major tax hikes in just a few years. Fortunately, this isn’t the final word. The State Senate now has a chance to fix it, and I’m hopeful they'll push for a more fiscally responsible solution.
As your representative, I remain committed to commonsense budgeting and protecting Pennsylvania families from reckless tax-and-spend policies. We need to grow our economy—not government. Just this week, I attended the AI and Energy Summit at Carnegie Mellon, where it was clear: Pennsylvania has everything it needs to lead nationally. What we lack is a government that knows how to get out of the way and partner in that growth.
This fight couldn’t be more urgent. Right now, Democrats hold the State House by just one seat—102 to 101. As we saw with the passage of this spending plan, a single vote can reshape the future of our Commonwealth. The stakes in 2025 couldn’t be higher, and I’m counting on your support to hold the line and continue pushing for reform.
I’d love for you to join me next week at my reception and fundraiser at Wildwood Country Club on July 23rd. I’ll be sharing an inside look at what we’ve accomplished over the past six months—and what’s coming next. Everyone’s welcome, and there’s no set cost to attend—just come, and contribute what you’re able.
---
[|
---](https://voteshaffer.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=5a4998b6ce4b01517c474232e&id=40025c77e7&e=8c80cba7e7)
| Thank you again for your continued support. I look forward to seeing you there.

Respectfully,
---
[|
---](https://voteshaffer.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=5a4998b6ce4b01517c474232e&id=120f8542a9&e=8c80cba7e7)
| Jeremy Shaffer
State Representative (HD-28)

name: Kristen McDonald Rivet
subject: Three things for you...
body:
|
#
Friend, Our frontline race just shifted into new territory, and I need folks like you with me in this fight. Here’s why: **One:** Because I voted NO on the GOP’s plan to gut Medicaid and SNAP, Republican dark money flooded my district – **$800,000** worth of lies about me going straight to our voters, all funded by the special interests who benefit from the bill. **Two:** We just got our first GOP opponent. He’s already on the record saying he’d vote YES on Trump’s disastrous budget bill. **Three:** Our race is one of the most competitive in the country – and control of the House could come down to what happens right here in Michigan’s 8th. While Republicans are being propped up by dark money and special interests, our campaign is powered by grassroots supporters who believe in putting people first. Friend, While D.C. politicians talk, Governor Ron DeSantis is taking action.

He built _Alligator Alcatraz_ — Florida’s newest detention center to deport illegal immigrants — surrounded by swamp, guarded by gators, and backed by real deportations.

name: Rev. Raphael Warnock
subject: Help me send a message to the GOP
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Warnock for Georgia
body:
| | |
---
Republicans in Congress should be ashamed of themselves, friend.
The budget bill they just passed raises costs on virtually every American. It sells out the working folks they were elected to serve. It strips millions of Americans of their health care. And it takes food away from hungry children.
Meanwhile, they were sure to include a tax cut for the special interests and billionaires who fund their campaigns.
I know that folks are sick and tired of politicians in Washington putting billionaires ahead of the middle class. This GOP budget bill was historically unpopular, and yet Republicans passed it anyway – because they think they won’t pay any political consequences.
**That’s why I’m asking this team to help me make it clear to the GOP that the American people are outraged by this bill. My team created a new poll so you can share where you stand, and I’m counting on 10,000 responses by midnight tonight. Please, tell me now:**
**Do you approve of the GOP’s extreme budget bill?**

name: Marina Jenkins
subject: July 18, 1946
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the National Democratic Redistricting Committee, John Bisognano, President
body:
79 years ago today, a voting rights pioneer was murdered
| [](https://click.everyaction.com/k/111723253/558183191/-3547583?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxMy8xLzg5Mjk0IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjQxY2YxNGNhLTExNjQtZjAxMS04ZGM5LTYwNDViZGZlOGU5YyIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=WStbl-dBpv4jPJvgwvTZxByWwHrPLXNyoJlF2XmdYV8=&emci=1eca7f0f-f363-f011-8dc9-6045bdfe8e9c&emdi=41cf14ca-1164-f011-8dc9-6045bdfe8e9c&ceid=8431045)
---

Peter — Seventy-nine years ago on this date, July 18, Maceo Snipes was lynched for casting a vote. The day before was a Primary Election Day, and he’d been the only Black person who voted in Taylor County, Georgia — after a federal court had outlawed the state’s rule barring nonwhite voters from participating in primaries. One day later, Snipes was shot and mortally wounded by a group of white men alleged to be part of the Ku Klux Klan. The shooter claimed self-defense, and the FBI ultimately closed the case — determining the killing was not related to Snipes exercising his right to vote. **Maceo Snipes never got the justice he deserved, but his legacy carries through the work you and I do every day to bolster the right to vote and advance fair representation in states across the country.** As anti-democracy forces seek to eliminate Black opportunity districts and dilute the voting power of communities of color, we draw inspiration from voting rights pioneers like Maceo Snipes. We honor their courage and sacrifice with our own commitment to advance fair representation. We challenge manipulated maps. We fight for pro-democracy leaders and judges. We mobilize wherever threats to democracy arise — and we never stop fighting. I hope you’ll keep Maceo Snipes in your heart today, and continue to summon the courage needed to stop the ceaseless attacks on our fundamental voting rights. In solidarity, Marina Jenkins
Executive Director
National Democratic Redistricting Committee | [DONATE](https://click.everyaction.com/k/111723255/558183195/-246980373?refcode=don_ndrc_tier1_email_Snipes_email_202050718-NDRC-Tier1-Snipes_&utm_source=email&utm_medium=email&utm_term=don&utm_content=202050718-NDRC-Tier1-Snipes_&utm_campaign=Snipes&amount=30&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxMy8xLzg5Mjk0IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjQxY2YxNGNhLTExNjQtZjAxMS04ZGM5LTYwNDViZGZlOGU5YyIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=WStbl-dBpv4jPJvgwvTZxByWwHrPLXNyoJlF2XmdYV8=)

clean_body: 79 years ago today, a voting rights pioneer was murdered | Peter — Seventy-nine years ago on this date, July 18, Maceo Snipes was lynched for casting a vote. The day before was a Primary Election Day, and he’d been the only Black person who voted in Taylor County, Georgia — after a federal court had outlawed the state’s rule barring nonwhite voters from participating in primaries. One day later, Snipes was shot and mortally wounded by a group of white men alleged to be part of the Ku Klux Klan. The shooter claimed self-defense, and the FBI ultimately closed the case — determining the killing was not related to Snipes exercising his right to vote. Maceo Snipes never got the justice he deserved, but his legacy carries through the work you and I do every day to bolster the right to vote and advance fair representation in states across the country. As anti-democracy forces seek to eliminate Black opportunity districts and dilute the voting power of communities of color, we draw inspiration from voting rights pioneers like Maceo Snipes. We honor their courage and sacrifice with our own commitment to advance fair representation. We challenge manipulated maps. We fight for pro-democracy leaders and judges. We mobilize wherever threats to democracy arise — and we never stop fighting. I hope you’ll keep Maceo Snipes in your heart today, and continue to summon the courage needed to stop the ceaseless attacks on our fundamental voting rights. In solidarity, Marina Jenkins Executive Director National Democratic Redistricting Committee | = = Since 2017, the NDRC has executed a comprehensive redistricting strategy that shifts the redistricting power, creating fair districts where Democrats can compete. Our victories have been made possible by our strong community of grassroots supporters. = If you want to receive periodic updates from the NDRC on our fight for fair maps, text MAPS to 36787. Text HELP for help, STOP to end. Msg & Data rates may apply. We know we send a lot of emails, but we think it's important to keep you updated with the latest information on Republican gerrymandering and voter suppression and how we're fighting back -- regardless of whether you choose to donate. = to make a donation. Every dollar you give will go toward our fight to end gerrymandering and voter suppression. | | Paid for by the National Democratic Redistricting Committee, John Bisognano, President. | | | | | ---|---|---|---|--- National Democratic Redistricting Committee 712 H St NE, #25 Washington, DC 20002 United States if you'd like to receive fewer emails from the NDRC.

subject: I’ll be real with you, Peter

body:
Hey Peter, it’s Maxwell. I’m sending this from my phone, so I’ll be quick:

Our organizing teams are working year-round to bring more people into our fight against facism. But this time of year, campaigns tend to see “summer slump” in fundraising.

So we’re setting daily fundraising goals to stay on track and avoid the summer slump – and my team just told me that we’re still $792 short of today’s goal.

Every dollar counts in this fight. DeSantis is raising millions to crush our movement, so we can’t miss any goals if we want to out-organize them.

But if every person reading this pitches in just $3, we’ll pass our goal and have the resources to expand our organizing teams!

So Peter, can I count on you to donate $3 to fund my campaign before midnight? Your donation has the power to fuel our youth organizing teams and ensure Florida has a future free of fascism.

subject: "I love you, Kate," 1,500 Bikers, and the Oldest Ultramarathoner
body:

**Good Afternoon!** This week, we report on an [unexpected friendship](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0DGOpDAQheHTmMzILsAFgYNNyHrTDZHBZdqLGyNT04jbj3quMPEnPT39_x7Tw8UU9_Wve5ENa5g45Gmn60zETGWKKb_pzl-bY9KdUnPcqJxu9xMo6KRCqfvJu5juqa28DURATUVWYwe9GTSa6mmXucUZm2YwvWn90uFMje89BNQqaAhVtJ81hboHpYw2NeFi_DDjMDcIBEq0inzkXOr_Oe78pJDu_KZSL_lVJftkPk7R_BEwChiv66o5e3d_VMAYcvYCxoPykUjAyHHjvFGR7DY65YDyJldkTl6eXNy-fijLi3Z_n7IsuwON0GBVrD-umFI8DYpWrS8X088D_nW6t4XvAAAA__8XooPD) between two women, one aged 29 and the other 97, that started with a ride and a burger. Their bond proves that kindness can bridge more than just age gaps. Do you have friends who don't necessarily fall within your generation? Let us know by replying to this email. ## Kentucky Priest Shouts Love at Wimbledon
A [Kentucky priest stole the spotlight at Wimbledon](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLGOgzAQRL8GOkf2EmwoXFyTLtdeiQxeYC_GRvYmEX9_Ir9w9YxGb97Pfbg7ChSXb7ehnZd54DkNEd8lIDPmgUJ64ZGeD8eoWilHemAuLvoBJLRCGqG6wTsKx3CtvZ0RAZsarTItdLpXRterbbVrm9FAY0YAdOjc2INpwetuakwPNdlzTRrVgZRa6QuaSft-NP3YGECQ1VWiJ0758pso8opzONIL82VKWx3syryXqvmq4FbBbce0BzyjCm4nuNjI-4CcosjoJi5idlGUNT2Z4iJInCfFkZ7i037TNgb0KQrOaV8PsWcsGNkxpSiUMkapFups_f6mEKhoU13lsjkKHx7-t8iXhb8AAAD__wcSic4) when he shouted, _" I love you, Kate!"_ as Princess Kate Middleton stepped onto Centre Court to present trophies after the men's final last Sunday. The voice came from Father Jim Sichko of Lexington, Kentucky, who was in the stands with a group from St. Mary School on a benefit trip. [Watch a video of the moment here](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkcGOnDoQRb_G7NwyBdhmweI9RSizmCiRJprskMHldjUGt8CN038f0fmEbG8dnarS_Xwf3g0FWq_fzIKdu7ohuTismPeAKeE2UIgHPuNjNgnLRoiRZtx2s9oBBDRcKF7qwRoKz6EubOcQAasCu1I1oGVbKln4rqpgUqN21lrdaAdt4yYtZdtKO6IpTUHdaROq1CCELOUF1SRtO6p2rBQgCFYLtJTidrlFWpNHF57xwO0yxaUInU_pvrPqPwY9gz7nfHFmwjHG-QQY9LedJj9HBv1BFuPOoCfujeUpchs5JU6OP-ODZ7OmM5zXmHn2JnHiluzfGYXAvTnwBLJJk-fJI38ZOTHooQRRNbpsm7pu61qc51T9QiP-TmRZ9SXnX29uYyDNcmfV_5t9pd9lfEs_5_6mPz8-Hl9_FFtn7-cy2qVitbguhsLr0_TPFR0d_AkAAP__q62k-g). Kate laughed at the unexpected outburst, drawing smiles across the stadium and headlines online. Father Jim has served as a papal missionary of mercy and once gifted Pope Francis a bottle of Pappy Van Winkle bourbon. His good-natured shout brought a dose of Southern warmth to Centre Court. ## Couple Wraps National Parks Quest by Age 70
Deborah Ann Cathey and Mel Richards wrapped up a coast-to-coast bucket list this summer, [visiting all 63 U.S. national parks](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLGuozAQAL8GOkeLAzYULq6hy7VXWg5ew142NrINEX9_yv3Cq0eakebPwz4cMcX1t3ujCWuwNSQb8VMYa8VsidOJVzpermI3ADzphbm46K0EOQjQohutd8SX7VtvAqLEe4um04Mc1dRp1W5m7IYgdQih1wpGBeO0uGWctFZ9j9APLZmvDXQ3SgDVqRvqRfnpqafnXUuU0PSAnmrKt7-JYt0w8JVOzLclvVs2W617ae6_Gjk3ck5H9Snl8oWNnDWIC10Wib1Y0rEzikCRyoZFnFSoUlyFYxaHKCK6Sik6FrvLr9LIuc3G7x9ipqJ008P6dsT_s_XHv04j_wUAAP__LiCADg), just in time for their 70th birthdays. This week, we report on an between two women, one aged 29 and the other 97, that started with a ride and a burger. Their bond proves that kindness can bridge more than just age gaps. Do you have friends who don't necessarily fall within your generation? Let us know by replying to this email. Love what you're reading? Don't miss the video podcast Feel Good Flyover on , , or only), where host Amy Hess brings you even more behind-the-scenes stories, visuals, and conversations. Today, is sponsoring us. This is your chance to invest in technology that will disrupt the real estate market. | Kentucky Priest Shouts Love at Wimbledon A when he shouted, " I love you, Kate!" as Princess Kate Middleton stepped onto Centre Court to present trophies after the men's final last Sunday. The voice came from Father Jim Sichko of Lexington, Kentucky, who was in the stands with a group from St. Mary School on a benefit trip. . Kate laughed at the unexpected outburst, drawing smiles across the stadium and headlines online. Father Jim has served as a papal missionary of mercy and once gifted Pope Francis a bottle of Pappy Van Winkle bourbon. His good-natured shout brought a dose of Southern warmth to Centre Court. . 📣 Feel Good Flyover Podcast host Amy Hess sits down with Father Jim for a deeply personal and. Couple Wraps National Parks Quest by Age 70 Deborah Ann Cathey and Mel Richards wrapped up a coast-to-coast bucket list this summer, , just in time for their 70th birthdays. The Texas couple completed their final stop at Isle Royale, a remote island in Lake Superior, in early July, marking the end of a nine-year journey that took them from the Virgin Islands to Alaska. Cathey shared the news--along with some photos-- for park travelers, where the post drew more than 50,000 likes. She says they got serious about the quest in 2018 and completed their last eight parks this year alone. Their journey started with tents and road trips and ended with a deep appreciation for places they say they never imagined visiting. 📣 Amy talks with the couple about the biggest surprises and best advice for first-timers on . Miami Pair Forge Bond Across Generations A chance encounter in Miami led to between 29-year-old Estrella Quiroz and 97-year-old Lilian, who was spotted walking with a walker on a hot June day. Estrella circled back, offered her a ride, and then lunch at Wendy's--a simple gesture that sparked a deep, cross-generational connection. As they chatted, the two discovered shared roots in Nicaragua and similar life values. Lilian, a former accountant living alone on a fixed income, opened up about her life over burgers and fries. . The moment went viral, drawing millions of views. Estrella later launched a to help Lilian with bills and to buy her a new walker and raised over $21,000! | Flying together with our sponsor His First Venture Sold for $120 million. This Time is Different. Austin Allison is familiar with using tech to disrupt real estate—his first venture, dotloop, was acquired for $120 million. But despite bringing industry fame, one nagging regret remained. "I always wished we gave retail investors the chance to invest in dotloop and share that success," Allison later said. Now he’s doing just that. Austin built upon that experience to launch , the co-ownership marketplace disrupting the $1.3 trillion vacation home industry. And it’s working. They’ve surpassed $110 million in gross profit to date, including 41% YoY growth last year. They have also earned venture backing from firms like Maveron and Greycroft. They even reserved the Nasdaq ticker PCSO. name: Team Crow
subject: Trump broke his promise — again.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jason Crow for Congress
body:
Jason Crow For Congress [https://nvlupin.blob.core.windows.net/images/van/NGP/NGP21/1/88523/images/logoJC%20(1).png]
Team,

Donald Trump ran on the promise of making life better for working Americans.

But this month, he and his Republican loyalists in Congress broke that promise in one of the cruelest ways possible by voting to take health care away from 17 million people across the country.

That’s almost triple the population of Colorado.

This vote wasn’t just an attack on health care. It showed where Trump’s priorities really lie: in securing more tax breaks for billionaires, stripping more rights away from you and your loved ones, and leaving even more Americans struggling.

Trump and his ultra-wealthy allies don’t know what life is like for working people or how hard they have to work just to get by.

But Jason does. And in Congress, he’s fighting every single day to protect affordable health care, lower costs, and make sure no family has to choose between paying for medicine or putting food on the table.

But this fight is going to take all of us. If you’re ready to push back against Trump’s broken promises and protect health care for millions of families, will you pitch in to support Jason’s work today? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jc-em-july2025?amount=]

If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:

DONATE $10 NOW [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jc-em-july2025?amount=10&express_lane=true]

ANOTHER AMOUNT [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jc-em-july2025]

Jason’s not in Congress to serve the rich and powerful. He’s there to fight for hardworking Americans like you.

Thanks for always standing with him.

— Team Crow

Greenwood Village, CO 80112

name: The Real Texas PAC
subject: Trump just signed an executive order targeting you
body:
Trump has launched a direct attack on the grassroots. And if we don’t fight back now, there's no telling how far he'll take this.
Rush $7 to defend ActBlue and protect the future of small-dollar democracy: [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-abeo?refcode=071825_3]
CHIP IN NOW TO DEFEND ACTBLUE AGAINST TRUMP >>> [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-abeo?refcode=071825_3]
Imagine this:
You chip in $5 to support a pro-choice woman running for Congress. Or a first-time statehouse challenger. Or a school board candidate fighting book bans.
Now imagine the President of the United States signs an executive order to investigate — and dismantle — the platform you used to make that donation.
Not a right-wing conspiracy theory. Not a hypothetical.
That just happened.
Donald Trump is abusing executive power to go after ActBlue — the secure, trusted platform that powers nearly every grassroots Democratic campaign in the country.
He’s not targeting dark money. He’s not going after billionaire-funded super PACs. He’s coming after you — the small-dollar donors who fuel this movement.

But what I do know is folks are sick and tired of [Big Brother](http://links.burchettforcongress.com/s/c/A8OInNpiAbX4_zb6JWj_xq6Zm-UItiOMN904LkIa6mKmBewnRlOC1mH082kCfi-TlokLBvYBlOcy_pkKZU1tjunSW0PSJuKOe9ozUql-vNZoSFSRjBfn8wgXtesSey1CYfVeATuRk-DkuQ-TwhniiXHlbi8ONNslave-RqjFFEuosuPHXe4IHZefEeJJr0nKlyklJ8F-81qHg3cEQYxKDkRdVX_bSQN4MHVf3mRB_V_XUH6LkjuNMTM_Q0VXIKP4Phde43AszKiLMefJxrAkR6pfBkZHpF6pVgz_75_gxV0xPW1wzy9Dmlx6jcwtkyPzwthD9utTR_WAXgisfy095DTiQNd_oIzFTE1GMDksgFqdC_lV1rdbIkxR78GwtIih04PX0ANLBMD0IZPs_06-T7WQpZ9PDUGZRtd7oDRMT1l0WBqu5Q/m1rSnB4uD4RDLCH4sX1mcnWMPUZs6jCl/11) spying on their every move, so this week [the House](http://links.burchettforcongress.com/s/c/PE-PBLsZmB8cf_uNWfPuH25Lkp--LBt0HAN7rfpB_p5bkFKHmrmL_6wNYaWaK-jgPg39jdRrVAe8KbIXZ2koxnSWlPLdXyhpjoByOZyLLJY2j0O29y5Uqaz6-wBwQAKqaXbtDCeveFyYU5-6WYs7Ngl3ZfFN6kZlD2FIVxU1FIYqag0kOEJOv8BYYIy0ragc292JsZGJSzTOtWf0uDf7S-dlbpXJOAfrM31I4_jOcDwQQq-y9Uw0wevvX88Wplw5hgdS87VoZercO58-JQOb2cX5vqHxoV_4XXu0XGADkug6RhXV2zNqEsqmE-24SRaEC_xEwIFs1Ecy6KbMxDbxsrmGASsb8deBAC8dlzd6Z6VxnxnrD2JjUS2JXN_8tYi2nAeyPejU1Ol26GPHnSJ7ctYcfdwY5n0a2VH6nhnDdggetQ8piQ/R6ePR8ZucsqKMpdgBuQdGV5Lq5r095CT/11) did something about it. It took a bit of time, including a late-night agreement with President Trump, but we passed legislation that will keep [crypto transactions](http://links.burchettforcongress.com/s/c/DTpBNbhJzoBMM05_UFABIMrqtPxJTUl5w0bKbmaaGMAD0NpmzKbV9JRZAV3X_SKxNTzeOotPUWYRsnO1MII-dWA-QSHtpu4TshaZ66jAPYXd2XoyZxZyK7g3QW6jubHEKaAonDWH8iamxxxftLVeTUodbjDX_5rfYxY5ICvDk98KOvXqjXpyyCBCFbgg-RCd7cUALISwr6sqFQsr22BFoYyINABYSCEz7F-CAKFFjUUD-G3yGOaJ3lRKY-t7VE-fuwLZL7QxCCIm6ZWNHYsY8XF08Z0YpcRrzSH0hjFyLfsLIQc8smtr0e3OaTGpzTA2aKSwBUWPaWoKAKlNhFtyYjTmgDyPr1aEX3uxDkbIh3uqIXELurtAcUD0i1W38SLvfaioGiKhHtcdpiKVwX3iHijpDtq2hNzcG0Th7ZvW6Vg2VBisdKWYRB4g15iQzbRpegG612a9WDY8Fh6if1FU1JYCbL8KjpQQZ4IL5tVQLzf9RFFmWLgYub-LYdhjhKJfHpsOXH7blKF8IIyi9ca1r1dPjsj0HkniA3A67xxCyyKkbT_pqRnRP-RPYqPQtJE3JfCH/EAvxC7bEg8BN7CybmvsLlthD7D5PBigS/11) private like cash. Don’t get me wrong, I know our leaders want to double down on our strength in this area—but we’ve got to be [smart](http://links.burchettforcongress.com/s/c/SaVRYS8NdrwiAwRA6ezRrkmtZY9weOOeihejkb6ltgGDuQzW_BhoBgxK6VdSh_Gn2l3skjARRbO7MUcnavJsMUW-SsKeajb17XRUhmmuQ1ui14kzRsT9lKwGRIkFOYkQRjCA5sdVfvdClcfWMeDTJq8fkcGmSaRI_0MdJGrtvltTFv8Eu0nRO4caVHIHng3w86xiapLIm2_QxsVyX4HfQb6WruV3RPkasjkGeUG8NmqF58i3zI7VIx7DmO40hfv7-qy3E_ibO48cqbqn9S7lpuCypXwi5ZD35kRkg8ScBL5O0wHZ1CmdkDUJ_7BtxHiCAJuAsa9M0BVb-y1I-fKZYgZat_qp8ggDW_8UozG1zMoVz1WvANLbEKmJ76aPvbVWZDBEP6QQJ4uGd__ixWroAAKkv61PVBOVzrM2g3zm0Y3-9Ed-Cw/9keBAOxbU7ymdnKd1db41I9CQtGjTvcY/11) about it and promote the freedom this nation was founded on. Whether you’re paying your nephew 20 bucks to mow the lawn or buying a vintage car, it’s none of the federal government’s business how you use your money, and that was my biggest concern in all this. Tracking every single crypto transaction has nothing to do with national security—it’s plain old overreach. The Left wants to know your every move and track every dadgum dollar you make, just to turn around and take it from you on April 15. But maybe it goes even deeper than that. Sometimes I think bureaucrats want so much red tape so the little guy doesn’t even have a chance to dabble in something like crypto. It’s just another way to hold everyday Americans down—and I won’t allow it. We need more capitalism, not more control. We need more innovation, not more interference. And above all, we need more leaders like President Trump who trust the American people to make their own decisions without Washington breathing down their necks. Your hard-earned dollars shouldn’t have a tracking number on them, no matter the tax bracket you fall into. That’s the kind of country I’m fighting for here in Congress.

clean_body: TIMtalk will keep you updated on the important issues and goings-on in our campaign, in Tennessee, and in our country.| | | | | Trump: 1️⃣ Big Brother: 0️⃣ | | | | I’m just a humble boy from Knoxville. I don’t play the stock market, and I don’t know a lot about this fancy crypto stuff. Heck, most of my money’s in . But what I do know is folks are sick and tired of spying on their every move, so this week did something about it. It took a bit of time, including a late-night agreement with President Trump, but we passed legislation that will keep private like cash. Don’t get me wrong, I know our leaders want to double down on our strength in this area—but we’ve got to be about it and promote the freedom this nation was founded on. Whether you’re paying your nephew 20 bucks to mow the lawn or buying a vintage car, it’s none of the federal government’s business how you use your money, and that was my biggest concern in all this. Tracking every single crypto transaction has nothing to do with national security—it’s plain old overreach. The Left wants to know your every move and track every dadgum dollar you make, just to turn around and take it from you on April 15. But maybe it goes even deeper than that. Sometimes I think bureaucrats want so much red tape so the little guy doesn’t even have a chance to dabble in something like crypto. It’s just another way to hold everyday Americans down—and I won’t allow it. We need more capitalism, not more control. We need more innovation, not more interference. And above all, we need more leaders like President Trump who trust the American people to make their own decisions without Washington breathing down their necks. Your hard-earned dollars shouldn’t have a tracking number on them, no matter the tax bracket you fall into. That’s the kind of country I’m fighting for here in Congress. | | Made In East Tennessee 🧡 | | | | Well folks, I think I went this week—but I’m happy to say it was for something real cool. If you follow me on , you’ve probably seen my homemade skateboards. They’re crafted with a lot of love (and recycled materials) right in my garage back in Tennessee. Like I told one of my followers, it’s a fun hobby and it’s a whole lot cheaper than therapy when I need to decompress after being in the sewer all week. The one in this video is actually a gift for Tulsi Gabbard. Made with bamboo, oak, and banana plant fiber, even my dog approves. Stay tuned for the big reveal when I get it over to Ms. Gabbard’s office. I think it’s my best one yet, and I have a feeling we’ll see her rolling around D.C. often. | | Just For Fun 🥳 | | → Down on the farm 🚜 () → Not the only one catching waves 👀 ()→ Block party hero 🤘 () | | Product Of The Month 🛍 | | | | | Join the conservative movement! Chip in to help us pave the way for a solid Republican agenda. | | Stay in Touch 👇 | | | | ---|---|---|--- Paid for by Burchett for Congress

subject: July 26: Families First mobilizations near you
body:
Find a Families First Mobilization on July 26 to make sure every family and care worker impacted by cruel Medicaid cuts has a chance to make their voices heard
[](https://click.everyaction.com/k/111724213/558186531/664783965?utm_campaign=cia_pp&utm_medium=email&utm_source=partner_ndwa&ms=c4-find-event-families-first-wide-july-2025&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNV08vMS83NjczNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjYTU1ZDFmZi0xNTY0LWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=tDGenqVbioW2FSFoiJ17Lcv764NOuV0S9gpmG1rEjSA=&emci=de7c813c-7962-f011-8dc9-6045bdfe8e9c&emdi=ca55d1ff-1564-f011-8dc9-6045bdfe8e9c&ceid=864787)
**On July 26th, we’ll be in all 50 states with coalition partners, domestic workers, and allies to stand together with the millions of families who will be impacted by Republicans’ cruel reconciliation bill that guts Medicaid.**
Earlier this month, **Republicans in Congress betrayed families across the country to show loyalty to Trump’s agenda** – forcing us ALL to pay the price for giant tax breaks to billionaires and a massive funding increase for ICE.
Now, this cruel bill puts the livelihoods of home care workers and many other domestic workers across the country on the line – which is why **it’s vital we make sure every family and care worker impacted by these cuts has a chance to make their voices heard by creating a massive multi-racial, multi-generational movement**. And it starts when we come together in massive numbers next week for family friendly events across the country!
[**Will you join us? Find a Families First mobilization near you and sign up for this nationwide day of action on July 26th.**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/111724215/558186533/-792613724?utm_campaign=cia_pp&utm_medium=email&utm_source=partner_ndwa&ms=c4-find-event-families-first-wide-july-2025&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNV08vMS83NjczNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjYTU1ZDFmZi0xNTY0LWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=tDGenqVbioW2FSFoiJ17Lcv764NOuV0S9gpmG1rEjSA=&emci=de7c813c-7962-f011-8dc9-6045bdfe8e9c&emdi=ca55d1ff-1564-f011-8dc9-6045bdfe8e9c&ceid=864787)

[ ](https://click.everyaction.com/k/111724219/558186537/-792613724?utm_campaign=cia_pp&utm_medium=email&utm_source=partner_ndwa&ms=c4-find-event-families-first-wide-july-2025&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNV08vMS83NjczNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjYTU1ZDFmZi0xNTY0LWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=tDGenqVbioW2FSFoiJ17Lcv764NOuV0S9gpmG1rEjSA=&emci=de7c813c-7962-f011-8dc9-6045bdfe8e9c&emdi=ca55d1ff-1564-f011-8dc9-6045bdfe8e9c&ceid=864787)
**Donald Trump and Republicans in Congress traded our health, safety, and basic needs for billionaire tax giveaways and ICE raids**. 17 million people will lose their healthcare, domestic workers will lose their jobs, rural hospitals will close, families will be left without food and care, communities will suffer, and people will die.
While the bill is now law, **it is NOT too late to show that we won’t stay silent about the pain and suffering this bill will unleash on our communities**. Which is why we’ll be out on July 26th at peaceful, family friendly events with thousands and thousands of people in our coalition to show that these heartless cuts will have real consequences on millions of families across the country.
[**Join us on July 26th to show that the people are on the side of healthcare and domestic workers, not billionaires.**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/111724221/558186539/-792613724?utm_campaign=cia_pp&utm_medium=email&utm_source=partner_ndwa&ms=c4-find-event-families-first-wide-july-2025&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNV08vMS83NjczNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjYTU1ZDFmZi0xNTY0LWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=tDGenqVbioW2FSFoiJ17Lcv764NOuV0S9gpmG1rEjSA=&emci=de7c813c-7962-f011-8dc9-6045bdfe8e9c&emdi=ca55d1ff-1564-f011-8dc9-6045bdfe8e9c&ceid=864787)

clean_body: Find a Families First Mobilization on July 26 to make sure every family and care worker impacted by cruel Medicaid cuts has a chance to make their voices heard On July 26th, we’ll be in all 50 states with coalition partners, domestic workers, and allies to stand together with the millions of families who will be impacted by Republicans’ cruel reconciliation bill that guts Medicaid. Earlier this month, Republicans in Congress betrayed families across the country to show loyalty to Trump’s agenda – forcing us ALL to pay the price for giant tax breaks to billionaires and a massive funding increase for ICE. Now, this cruel bill puts the livelihoods of home care workers and many other domestic workers across the country on the line – which is why it’s vital we make sure every family and care worker impacted by these cuts has a chance to make their voices heard by creating a massive multi-racial, multi-generational movement. And it starts when we come together in massive numbers next week for family friendly events across the country! Donald Trump and Republicans in Congress traded our health, safety, and basic needs for billionaire tax giveaways and ICE raids. 17 million people will lose their healthcare, domestic workers will lose their jobs, rural hospitals will close, families will be left without food and care, communities will suffer, and people will die. While the bill is now law, it is NOT too late to show that we won’t stay silent about the pain and suffering this bill will unleash on our communities. Which is why we’ll be out on July 26th at peaceful, family friendly events with thousands and thousands of people in our coalition to show that these heartless cuts will have real consequences on millions of families across the country. As always, thanks for all you do, The Care in Action Team

name: John Fetterman
subject: Trump's holding $200+ million hostage from PA schools
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Fetterman for PA
body:
| | |
---
[](https://act.johnfetterman.com/go/51456?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&%3Bzip=18326&%3Bcode=jtk192-su-fl&t=1&akid=10208%2E434096%2EQlzrCE)
Derek, it's John.

I'm pissed – the Trump administration is now ILLEGALLY withholding almost **$7 BILLION** in federal education funding.
These are funds Congress ALREADY approved – **at least $230 MILLION of which belongs to PA schools.**
_This isn’t oversight. It’s sabotage._ Our schools were counting on this money – now they’re scrambling. Summer school is happening _now_. After-school programs are supposed to start in _just weeks_.
**Trump is throwing grenades into school budgets across PA, ESPECIALLY in our rural communities, and it's our kids who will pay the price.**
When after-school tutoring is canceled, when teachers can’t get the training they need, when reading and writing test scores begin to drop, we will know exactly who to blame.
We need to show there is undeniable support for our kids and their right to a quality public education, so my team and I are compiling a petition, and we need at least 20,000 signatures by midnight tonight.
**👉 [If you’re sick of the Trump administration running roughshod over our schools and communities, I need you to sign the petition now to tell Trump to quit playing political games and RELEASE this funding NOW. >>](https://act.johnfetterman.com/go/51457?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&code=jtk192-su-fl&t=2&akid=10208%2E434096%2EQlzrCE)**

John ❤️

Trump is throwing grenades into school budgets across PA, ESPECIALLY in our rural communities, and it's our kids who will pay the price. When after-school tutoring is canceled, when teachers can’t get the training they need, when reading and writing test scores begin to drop, we will know exactly who to blame. We need to show there is undeniable support for our kids and their right to a quality public education, so my team and I are compiling a petition, and we need at least 20,000 signatures by midnight tonight. 👉 | | Thanks for signing, Derek, John ❤️ | | | | Email us: info@johnfetterman.com

name: Justice Democrats
subject: BREAKING: 100+ Dems helped pass massive crypto giveaways
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY JUSTICE DEMOCRATS
body:
[1]Justice Democrats

Peter,

To conclude “Crypto Week” in Congress, more than 100 Democrats joined
Republicans in passing two massive giveaways to the corporate-backed
Crypto lobby yesterday.

With pressure from Democratic sell-outs like Josh Gottheimer, Ritchie
Torres, and Don Davis — whose campaigns have collectively received
millions in donations from the crypto lobby — 78 Democrats joined every
Republican to vote for the CLARITY Act, with even more (102 Democrats)
voting for the GENIUS Act.

Justice Democrats like Reps. Ayanna Presley and Delia Ramirez loudly
opposed these bills and exposed them for what they really are: a green
light to self-enriching crypto schemes.

[ https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-em-20250718?refcode=em20250718-36855&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&refcode2=36855_1203867_tn6UJb&akid=36855%2E1203867%2Etn6UJb ]Tweet from Congresswoman Delia C. Ramirez: Republicans want to force 3
bills on crypto through the House that reduce regulations, weaken
enforcement, erode consumer protections, empower corporate giants & big
banks, threaten our national security, and allow Trump to enrich himself.
I am a HELL NO.

Instead of stopping Trump’s personal crypto grift and properly regulating
the industry, these bills endanger consumers, open the floodgates for even
more fraud and even less oversight to boost corporate profits, and make
crypto billionaires even richer — all while allowing a taxpayer-funded
bailout when crypto inevitably fails.

In summary: They profit, we pay.

The passage of these bills comes after pro-crypto super PACs spent more
than $170 million to influence elections last cycle. The crypto lobby
effectively bought this legislation,
Peter.

By becoming the biggest corporate spender in the 2024 election cycle, Big
Crypto secured enough votes on both sides of the aisle to give Silicon
Valley and Wall Street new ways to get richer by endangering your life
savings and our economy.

More than 100 corporate Democrats threw working-class people under the bus
and legitimized Trump’s crypto scheme by supporting these bills. They
don’t deserve their seats in Congress, and they need to be primaried in
2026.

[ https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-em-20250718?refcode=em20250718-36855&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&refcode2=36855_1203867_tn6UJb&akid=36855%2E1203867%2Etn6UJb ]We’re actively recruiting progressive, working-class candidates to
challenge out-of-touch, corporate Democrats who put crypto profits before
the people. Fuel our fight with $5, $10, or whatever works for you today →

[ https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-em-20250718?refcode=em20250718-36855&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&refcode2=36855_1203867_tn6UJb&akid=36855%2E1203867%2Etn6UJb ]Contribute $5

The Crypto lobby poses a grave threat not only to our economy but our
democracy as we know it. That’s why we need to rally and fight back NOW
before it’s too late.

In solidarity,

Justice Democrats

Contribute to Justice Democrats: https://secure.actblue.com/donate/justicedemocrats?refcode=em20250718-36855-footer&t=1002&refcode2=36855_1203867_tn6UJb&akid=36855%2E1203867%2Etn6UJb

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way for us to reach you about opportunities to get involved. If you need to remove yourself from our email list, click here to unsubscribe: https://go.justicedemocrats.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=1003&akid=36855%2E1203867%2Etn6UJb

Lancaster PA 17603

name: AOC
subject: We can make a new world
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress
body:
#
I grew up in a working-class family.
My mom cleaned houses and my dad worked to build a small business. My parents worked themselves to the bone so that my brother and I could just have a shot.
And for a moment there, it looked like we were going to make it: we had a home, bills were a little less stressful to pay, and I was doing well in school. What may have looked like a modest life to others was a dream come true for us.
But suddenly, when I was a teenager, my dad was diagnosed with a rare form of cancer. He passed away in September of 2008, just as the economy crashed in the Great Recession and millions of jobs across this country vanished.
It felt like the world of our family fell apart as the larger world was falling apart too.
Overnight, my mom was left to figure out how to provide for two kids by herself, put them through school, keep our house, pay off my dad's medical bills — all on a house cleaner salary.
That's how I became a waitress. Because it was millions of innocent working families who paid the price, while the billionaires and thieves who defrauded our financial system got off richer than ever before. **And not a single one of them went to jail**.
And you know what?
**_That_ is the story that Republicans mock — not just mine, but all of ours — when they say a waitress or working person is unqualified to serve in Congress**.
I tell this story not because it is special, but because of how _common_ it is.
While the details may differ, so many of us know what it feels like for life to be one bad day, one bad piece of news, one major setback from everything feeling like it's going to fall apart.
**We don't have to live like this anymore**.
We can make a new world, a better country, where we can fight for the dignity of all people.
And it looks like **living wages**.
It looks like **stable housing**.
It looks like **guaranteed health care**.
And it looks like **respect for all of our differences** , no matter who we are or where we come from.
That means that we must choose to out-organize the oligarchy. We will and must do away with the power of big money.
And that is why, even as a waitress with a long shot campaign, I decided that **I would never take money from lobbyists or corporations — and I never will**.
[Will you make a $5 contribution right now to help us host more town halls on the road? These town halls take major resources to host safely.](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/go/14659?c0=aoc-email-20250718.WKpJMw&refcode=em20250718-9347-P_FND_EGR_GRM&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1&akid=9347%2E2526796%2EcK8gSv)
Every donation helps us mobilize, organize, and fight for the working class in the places that need it most.
**[Contribute $5](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/go/14659?c0=aoc-email-20250718.WKpJMw&refcode=em20250718-9347-P_FND_EGR_GRM&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=20&akid=9347%2E2526796%2EcK8gSv)**
In solidarity,
Alexandria

**[Donate](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/aoc-email-20250718?refcode=em20250718-9347-P_FND_EGR_GRM&t=21&refcode2=9347_2526796_cK8gSv&akid=9347%2E2526796%2EcK8gSv)** | **[Merch](https://shop.ocasiocortez.com/?utm_source=em-footer-20250718-9347-P_FND_EGR_GRM&t=22&akid=9347%2E2526796%2EcK8gSv)**
---|---
[](https://www.facebook.com/OcasioCortez/?t=23&akid=9347%2E2526796%2EcK8gSv) | [](https://twitter.com/AOC?t=24&akid=9347%2E2526796%2EcK8gSv) | [](https://www.instagram.com/aoc/?t=25&akid=9347%2E2526796%2EcK8gSv) | [](https://bsky.app/profile/aoc.bsky.social?t=26&akid=9347%2E2526796%2EcK8gSv)
---|---|---|---
Email us: [speak@ocasiocortez.com](mailto: speak@ocasiocortez.com)
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way we keep in touch with people like you, so thank you for reading to the end. Small donors like you keep us going. To contribute via check, please address to Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress, PO Box 680080, Corona, NY 11368.
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/signup/receive-fewer-emails/)
* [Update My Personal Information](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/signup/did-your-information-change/?t=27&akid=9347%2E2526796%2EcK8gSv)
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=28&akid=9347%2E2526796%2EcK8gSv)

name: John Hickenlooper
subject: I never thought I’d run for Senate
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY HICKENLOOPER FOR COLORADO
body:
I was a laid-off geologist turned brewer, and I first ran for office because the concerns of everyday Coloradans simply weren’t being addressed.

[ ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111725755/558191670/1435897075?refcode=email_071825_bio-rs&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83MjY1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxOWQxOWFiNi0xYjY0LWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=37CjA_jQ5scS3QjfV8iKqSeGZRcgspMZPMHZA5lIbQs=&emci=9fb08c8f-4c63-f011-8dc9-6045bdfe8e9c&emdi=19d19ab6-1b64-f011-8dc9-6045bdfe8e9c&ceid=7280715) Folks, when I first arrived in Washington to serve Colorado in the US Senate, I’ll be honest: it was a lot to take in. See, I had served in public office before, but I was still *far* from being a typical politician. My path to serving in the US Senate looked a little different from the typical politician you’ll find in Washington. I’m a laid-off geologist who became a local brewer, and I first ran for Mayor of Denver because the concerns of everyday Coloradans simply weren’t being addressed. And since then, no matter what office I’ve held, I’ve worked to put Colorado families and communities first.
Despite the dysfunction we’ve seen from Washington lately, I’m proud to say that grit, integrity, and hard work has paid off in the time I’ve served Colorado in the Senate.
I got my hands dirty working hard in the Senate to make real change for Coloradans. We invested billions of dollars to confront the climate crisis, lowered prescription drug costs, made investments to rebuild our infrastructure, and brought thousands of good-paying manufacturing jobs back to America.
And in this unprecedented and critical moment, I’m fighting tooth and nail to blunt the worst of Trump’s extreme agenda, protect Colorado’s public lands, and preserve all of the progress we’ve made over the past few years.
**Coloradans don't want to see more chaos and dysfunction in Washington — they’re counting on us to deliver real relief and progress on the issues that matter most, and that’s what we’ll always do.**
**[If you believe our country deserves more results and fewer political games, will you chip in to support my campaign? body:
| It’s going to take all of us to stop Trump’s dangerous agenda.
# [](https://act.adamschiff.com/go/61?t=1&akid=8792%2E5257348%2E7dMW4w)
Derek: Trump’s Big Ugly Bill is the most unpopular piece of legislation in American history, and it passed the Senate by the narrowest margin possible.
It’s one of the most selfish acts I’ve seen in my career representing Californians, **but the fight isn’t over.**
**[Flipping the Senate and securing a Democratic majority in Congress is the first step to putting a stop to this shameful agenda and standing up for working families. Will you pitch in to help us strengthen our coalition and put a stop to this extreme agenda?](https://act.adamschiff.com/go/1014?c0=q32025_abs_em.sQXYU5&refcode=AU_ABS_FR_GEN_GEN_AC90_20250718_PM1_V1_S1&email=&t=4&akid=8792%2E5257348%2E7dMW4w)**

This bill is a tragedy — it will kick millions of people off their health insurance, rip away SNAP benefits across the country, and hand billions of dollars to the wealthy and well-connected to buy more planes and yachts for themselves.
And I won’t let the Republicans who passed it forget their shameful act. Not today, not tomorrow, not for one minute.
All this does is take help away from the people that need it the most, Derek. And at a time when costs are rising, not falling, Americans deserve better than that.
**[Pitch in right now to help us flip the Senate blue and get back to working for working people.](https://act.adamschiff.com/go/1014?c0=q32025_abs_em.sQXYU5&refcode=AU_ABS_FR_GEN_GEN_AC90_20250718_PM1_V1_S1&email=&t=11&akid=8792%2E5257348%2E7dMW4w)**

President Obama spoke out.
President Biden spoke out.
Vice President Harris spoke out.
Secretary Clinton spoke out.

name: Marie Gluesenkamp Perez
subject: Bring bipartisanship & common sense back to Congress
body:
| | Peter – The big bill passed by the Majority earlier this month shows us what happens when hyperpartisanship and special interests hold the reins in Congress:
| **_OPB:_**
_**Small Washington hospitals brace for ‘severe’ effects of Medicaid cuts**
“As many as 250,000 residents could lose Medicaid coverage in Washington. That reduction would mean fewer patients can pay at hospitals that are already struggling.”_
---

Instead of middle-class tax cuts, Medicaid protections, or cost-saving measures – you know, things that actually help working families – this bill saddles our kids with $4 trillion in debt to pay for tax giveaways for the top 0.1%.

Frankly, it’s ridiculous. And if we don’t protect seats like ours and flip the House next year, we’re in store for more special favors for special interests while everyday Americans are left carrying the bag.

**Extremists have started launching attacks against me earlier than ever, in hopes of flipping WA-03 – we need to fight back.** [Can I count on you to help us prepare for anything they throw at us on the road ahead? We need to raise another $20,000 by midnight tomorrow to stay on track for the month:](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111726168/558192788/-618465495?amount=10&refcode=MGP_EM_FR_90DO_250718_1_1&refcode2=b30148ad-1d64-f011-8dc9-6045bdfe8e9c&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS85NTkyOCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiMzAxNDhhZC0xZDY0LWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=kERtOyFTUFNWaIEjuPU-6Nf9Nft8OFjOZN-pBjTdNdw=)

I’m already facing an ultra-competitive re-election fight. But in the meantime, rest assured that I’ll keep working across the aisle to uphold our shared values and deliver results on the issues that really matter to Southwest Washingtonians, whether it’s bringing down everyday costs, uplifting the middle class, or supporting rural communities.

We have a lot of work to do – and I’m grateful to have you on our team as we continue forging ahead.

– Marie

body:
|
---
Welcome to the Indiana Democratic Party's weekly newsletter, The Kernel of Truth. The State Party will provide the most important facts - a kernel of truth - from the week. _[If you were forwarded this newsletter, you can sign up to receive the Kernel every week by clicking here.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111726338/558193108/1416943042?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzZTMxOGNkOS0xZDY0LWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=3K3h7OZJqrJHKHbqVjEHAeWe19QundsO6zXG5PV3j1M=&emci=1814db5b-1b64-f011-8dc9-6045bdfe8e9c&emdi=3e318cd9-1d64-f011-8dc9-6045bdfe8e9c&ceid=4925535)_ [_**If you'd like to donate to the INDems' Reorganization Fund, you can do so by clicking here.**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111726341/558193120/1764064610?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzZTMxOGNkOS0xZDY0LWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=3K3h7OZJqrJHKHbqVjEHAeWe19QundsO6zXG5PV3j1M=) ****
_Rep. Houchin, Republicans Block Release of Epstein Files_
**[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111726344/558193123/477167595?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzZTMxOGNkOS0xZDY0LWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=3K3h7OZJqrJHKHbqVjEHAeWe19QundsO6zXG5PV3j1M=&emci=1814db5b-1b64-f011-8dc9-6045bdfe8e9c&emdi=3e318cd9-1d64-f011-8dc9-6045bdfe8e9c&ceid=4925535)**[ Share INDems](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111726348/558193126/477167595?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzZTMxOGNkOS0xZDY0LWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=3K3h7OZJqrJHKHbqVjEHAeWe19QundsO6zXG5PV3j1M=&emci=1814db5b-1b64-f011-8dc9-6045bdfe8e9c&emdi=3e318cd9-1d64-f011-8dc9-6045bdfe8e9c&ceid=4925535) This week, Democrats have filed multiple amendments to force a vote on releasing the Epstein Files. Four votes have taken place this week on releasing the files, and Republicans have blocked all of them. Rep. Erin Houchin in particular has voted against the release of the files multiple times. As a member of the House Rules Committee, she has voted against forcing a vote on the files. On the House floor, the motion failed by just ONE vote, 211-210. That means that ANY Indiana Republican could have voted the other way and forced the release of these files. Instead, Republicans have chosen to protect the powerful people on the lists instead of the victims. We will keep pushing for accountability. Here is the statement shortly after the first set of votes from Chair Karen Tallian: “I served with many of these Republicans in the Indiana Senate. They had no trouble explaining their votes then — why are they silent now? Every Indiana Republican in Congress voted to protect those who participated in this heinous child trafficking ring with Epstein. Hoosiers deserve to know why,” said Tallian. “Every American, including Republican voters, wants to know the truth. What possible reason do they have to bury it? Is it because they know Donald Trump is on Epstein’s list? Please explain your vote.” “Congressional Democrats will keep fighting to bring these files to light because Democrats actually care about children and believe in the rule of law. clean_body: | Welcome to the Indiana Democratic Party's weekly newsletter, The Kernel of Truth. The State Party will provide the most important facts - a kernel of truth - from the week. = ** Rep. Houchin, Republicans Block Release of Epstein Files This week, Democrats have filed multiple amendments to force a vote on releasing the Epstein Files. Four votes have taken place this week on releasing the files, and Republicans have blocked all of them. Rep. Erin Houchin in particular has voted against the release of the files multiple times. As a member of the House Rules Committee, she has voted against forcing a vote on the files. On the House floor, the motion failed by just ONE vote, 211-210. That means that ANY Indiana Republican could have voted the other way and forced the release of these files. Instead, Republicans have chosen to protect the powerful people on the lists instead of the victims. We will keep pushing for accountability. Here is the statement shortly after the first set of votes from Chair Karen Tallian: “I served with many of these Republicans in the Indiana Senate. They had no trouble explaining their votes then — why are they silent now? Every Indiana Republican in Congress voted to protect those who participated in this heinous child trafficking ring with Epstein. Hoosiers deserve to know why,” said Tallian. “Every American, including Republican voters, wants to know the truth. What possible reason do they have to bury it? Is it because they know Donald Trump is on Epstein’s list? Please explain your vote.” “Congressional Democrats will keep fighting to bring these files to light because Democrats actually care about children and believe in the rule of law. Hoosiers demand to know who Congressional Republicans are working so hard to protect.” CNBC Names Indiana as Third Worst Quality of Life in Nation CNBC has its new state rankings for 2025, and Indiana is at the bottom of the list in the most important categories once again. Most notably, CNBC named Indiana as having the third-worst quality of life in the entire country. What is bringing down our state? It’s the lack of focus by this Republican legislature on the issues that matter. CNBC highlights our state’s long-term struggle with affordable childcare and healthcare. In some parts of Indiana, childcare can cost as much as 46% of an average household’s income. Moving forward, Democrats are focused on reducing healthcare costs, and investing in childcare with universal Pre-K and a childcare tax credit for working Hoosier families. Each week, the State Party will highlight key policy from Democrats at the Statehouse or across the state. Democrats deliver a better future for Hoosier families, and we urge you to share this information with your networks. This week, Senator Fady Qaddoura and Indiana Democrats responded to the state fiscal report, which showed a $2B surplus that was gained on the backs of working families. Senator Qaddoura pointed out that seniors and children are still waiting on Medicaid waitlists, and thousands of children were denied spots in On My Way Pre-K. Instead, Republicans cut these programs to ensure billionaires can get tax breaks. In response, Senator Qaddoura is calling for the state to prepare a report within 30 days showing the negative impacts of the Republican Big Ugly Bill on our state. People’s Town Hall Series We have more upcoming People’s Town Hall events! Richmond – Thursday, July 24, 2025 at 6:00-7:30 PM ET Leland Legacy Ballroom, 900 South A St, Richmond, IN 47374 Information on County Party events, Town Halls, protests, volunteer opportunities, and more can be found on our Make Your Voice Heard! Submit Public Input on Upcoming Indiana Projects INDIANAPOLIS PUBLIC SCHOOLS – IPS is taking comments about the recent legislative session, and the creation of the Indianapolis Local Education Alliance by lawmakers. IN DEPT OF ENVIRONMENTAL MANAGEMENT — IDEM is seeking public comment on current environment regulations. This is to implement Braun’s executive order this spring to weaken regulations. ** Upcoming Candidate Trainings the National Democratic Training Committee on Monday, July 21st from 1-2 pm ET for a free virtual live training on Phone Banking for Political Campaigns. name: Sarah McBride
subject: Donald Trump made it easier for billionaires to buy a private jet than for working families to put food on the table
disclaimer_text: Paid for by McBride for Delaware
body:
The more people learn about MAGA’s cruelty, the harder it’ll be for them to get away with it.
#
Hi,
After Donald Trump signed his “Big Ugly Bill” earlier this month, I’ve been hearing from people up and down the First State worried about how they’ll feed their children.
This billionaire budget guts food assistance for tens of millions of people, including nearly _40,000_ of our neighbors in Delaware, to pay for tax breaks for the ultra-rich.
**Simply put, it makes it easier for a billionaire to buy a private jet than for working families to put food on the table.**
We can’t let this slide.
Republican politicians are counting on people forgetting. They’re hoping this fades into the background — that no one remembers who owns this harm.
That’s why I’ve been busy speaking out everywhere I can — on social media, on TV, and in person — to make sure voters know Republican politicians took food out of the mouths of children to reward billionaires. But I need your help, too.
[**Will you make a grassroots donation to help us expand our reach, get the truth out, and power my work to prevent families from going hungry? The more people learn about MAGA’s cruelty, the harder it’ll be for them to get away with it.**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mcb-em-20250718-1?refcode=em20250718-2191&refcode2=2191_21716_cPPD-o&akid=2191%2E21716%2EcPPD-o)
**[Donate »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mcb-em-20250718-1?refcode=em20250718-2191&refcode2=2191_21716_cPPD-o&akid=2191%2E21716%2EcPPD-o)**
Thank you for doing your part.
—Sarah
* * *
**[Donate »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mcb-em-footer?refcode=em20250718-1&t=1&refcode2=2191_21716_cPPD-o&akid=2191%2E21716%2EcPPD-o)**

body:
We’re one step away from a serious national security crisis.

[](https://trk.emails.troydowning.)Peter,
Go to China and try to buy land.
__See how that works out for you.__
Now, try buying land near their military bases.
**__You’d vanish without a trace.__**
But here in America?

Whoever allowed this has betrayed our country.
It’s time to **SEIZE that land** and shut this down, permanently.

subject: This is what we’re up against.

body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_mfb_email-25q3?refcode=X_250718_gopopponents_act [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_mfb_email-25q3?refcode=X_250718_gopopponents_act]
Hi team,
A new Republican opponent recently entered this race—not just any opponent, either, but an ultra-wealthy opponent who plans to spend millions of his own dollars to fund his campaign.
And he’s not alone...
Right now, there are five Republicans running to be the next Governor of Colorado... One of which claimed that Planned Parenthood sells body parts and believes that federal agents helped ‘lure’ January 6 rioters to the Capitol.
These Republicans are jumping into this race because they believe they can win a statewide race in Colorado. And we, for one, aren’t taking any votes for granted or making any assumptions about how competitive this race might be.
If there is anything Michael knows about politics, it’s to expect the unexpected. That’s why we’re working day in and day out, laying the groundwork for a long campaign, and getting Michael’s message out to as many Coloradans as possible.
Friend, we’re going to need your support to get all this work done. Can you help us out by chipping in a few bucks today? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_mfb_email-25q3?refcode=X_250718_gopopponents_act]
DONATE [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_mfb_email-25q3?refcode=X_250718_gopopponents_act]
Michael is reaching out, engaging with, and listening to Coloradans' concerns. He’s traveling to every corner and community of our state, talking with voters, and sharing his record of fighting for us—first as the Superintendent of Denver Public Schools and then as our U.S. Senator.
We can’t afford to let our guard down in an election that will be so consequential for the future of our state.
We’re counting on small-dollar donors like yourself to power our campaign forward. Can you donate $25, $10, or $5? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_mfb_email-25q3?refcode=X_250718_gopopponents_act&amount=25]

name: Thank you, Stephen Colbert! (via VPP)
subject: Re: Stephen Colbert’s show ENDING >>
disclaimer_text: Paid for by VPP
body:
This marks the end of an incredible and honorable era.
| **This is __exactly__ what Trump had in mind when he went after CBS:** [](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/92334?t=6&akid=20686%2E878179%2E4MALkE) _Trump is attacking any voice that holds him accountable and speaks truth to his MAGA lies!_

That’s why he’s absolutely thrilled that Stephen Colbert’s late night program has been cancelled. **He said he “absolutely loves” that Colbert got fired — _we’re APPALLED!_**

[**To thank Stephen Colbert for his decade of hard work in the face of escalating MAGA attacks, we’re sending him a letter tomorrow at 9am promptly. Rush $3 to add your name before we hit send »**](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/92334?t=7&akid=20686%2E878179%2E4MALkE) | [**CHIP IN $3 TO SIGN NOW »**](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/92334?t=8&akid=20686%2E878179%2E4MALkE)
---
TO: Stephen Colbert
FROM: A grateful Democrat
Subject: Thank you. Dear Stephen Colbert,

**Thank you.** Your commitment to journalistic integrity and the truth over the last 10 years has been truly inspirational and has kept us all laughing through the MAGA madness.

While Fox News and other far-right outlets spew misinformation and conspiracy theories, you have been a constant, outstanding voice for our democracy.

**We know this is exactly the outcome that Trump and his MAGA cronies wished for — _and we are committed to fighting back tirelessly to protect the free press._**

While I’m going to miss seeing you on TV every night, I’m glad you’ll finally be getting the much-needed rest and recuperation you deserve.

[**So I’m signing this letter today by chipping in $3 to VPP to thank you for your years of service and to join VPP in the fight against Trump’s attacks on the free press. It is _absolutely critical_ that we elect good Democrats who will stand with your progressive values and honor your legacy, and we are so sad to see you go. »**](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/92334?t=9&akid=20686%2E878179%2E4MALkE) [](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/92334?t=10&akid=20686%2E878179%2E4MALkE) | [**CHIP IN $3 TO SIGN NOW »**](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/92334?t=11&akid=20686%2E878179%2E4MALkE)

**_X A proud Democrat_**
\- The Voter Protection Project
| | Paid for by VPP
(www.protectvoting.org)
Not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. 401 2nd Ave. S, Ste. 303, Seattle, WA 98104

The Voter Protection Project is on an important mission: To end partisan gerrymandering and voter suppression by electing candidates at the state and federal levels that are forceful advocates for voting rights. Our enfranchisement agenda includes working to ensure that every state has automatic voter registration and online voter registration, convenient early voting, and independent redistricting commissions to ensure politicians are not able to draw their own maps.

Please, consider making a [one-time contribution by clicking here](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/121?t=1&akid=20686%2E878179%2E4MALkE). **Or, to make the most powerful lasting impact and help us oust Republicans up and down the ballot who attack our right to vote, make a[monthly sustaining contribution by clicking here](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/122?t=2&akid=20686%2E878179%2E4MALkE).**

That’s why he’s absolutely thrilled that Stephen Colbert’s late night program has been cancelled. He said he “absolutely loves” that Colbert got fired — we’re APPALLED! | TO: Stephen Colbert FROM: A grateful Democrat Subject: Thank you. Dear Stephen Colbert, Thank you. Your commitment to journalistic integrity and the truth over the last 10 years has been truly inspirational and has kept us all laughing through the MAGA madness. While Fox News and other far-right outlets spew misinformation and conspiracy theories, you have been a constant, outstanding voice for our democracy. We know this is exactly the outcome that Trump and his MAGA cronies wished for — and we are committed to fighting back tirelessly to protect the free press. While I’m going to miss seeing you on TV every night, I’m glad you’ll finally be getting the much-needed rest and recuperation you deserve. | X A proud Democrat \- The Voter Protection Project | | Paid for by VPP (www.protectvoting.org) Not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. 401 2nd Ave. S, Ste. 303, Seattle, WA 98104 The Voter Protection Project is on an important mission: To end partisan gerrymandering and voter suppression by electing candidates at the state and federal levels that are forceful advocates for voting rights. Our enfranchisement agenda includes working to ensure that every state has automatic voter registration and online voter registration, convenient early voting, and independent redistricting commissions to ensure politicians are not able to draw their own maps. # FROM THE DESK OF NEWT GINGRICH
---
| | Reader,I'm pleased to share with you that The New York Sun is now carrying my exclusive weekly column. In it, I'll weigh in on the most pressing matters facing our country - **and there are many.** Our nation has lost its way. Many politicians and major institutions are corrupt. Leading corporations are following their lead.
---
| | [ JOIN THE SUN FOR 1¢ → ](http://links.makingamericathegreatest.com/u/click?_t=79fa4a3daf024a0a886c5b498ff32e32&_m=8fcde8d8f2964403be2bcd3912902de2&_e=tGkrjO7YH3XrP_eRua_BmxtfOk7k3mq9cgXaSX9ABw0_Bdw8sqznmDyWY0zSLYvuAzSauz7XPeNCkX9wdNPxk7zAyNRxiuseB1T5QNqE3tEXj2mFyzJVrC6yCY0rgD6rkwPfkBNu4wzQZ8601YGGSt2yiJAPFr5Da96nG7vG0SjSPywu0i2A2E8USe8STP9MQ4oXBgt3fs-_u_RHAIHEs_QbbIqiPAb-BhOUlvjUCGmWRuy1yaECTQTZn_87fhH6gPTLWBS2LYONOO8_hHfLq0kBjNt16yRpqNiYs2c6p5eisoUs06n2jmIwKyWy-H3jGyt6TYXSl9Gk2W-WCfBE7dbcdHvFIQlbbwo8Y19y35twv_IH0qQxxrzNb3Kspqei6s8IpmTACZDBUYjMsQmFV8TBzKfyJS5IuA1WbCTbnQrU0LwAcig9_M2OWBhQY_mGPPbZRBw7VmfSKX-5MSSSKmmX2iaSFtkpnWpTnYTvGv5LI7_K68SzEzp7Ek1Wyiy9EV-UcTRjWO1qCJhoo_PdeVZimAfdsdYnWT6YryRdLpglVyQ7UdTh02__Vf3c-9NW8FppDzGY8LOCOSDxdyeVK7Sa9EeQxulmv1rk-l_BnMQ%3D)
---
| | But it's the corruption of the media that has me most concerned. In my 70-year career in American politics, I've never seen it so bad. For this reason, **I 've chosen The New York Sun as a partner.**It's a paper that shines like a beacon amid a sea of lies and misinformation. It has done so for nearly 200 years. It's a patriotic paper that lives and breathes American values. It places our Constitution front and center and has long been among its most valiant defenders.It remains our nation's most effective counterweight to The New York Times, which has bizarrely claimed the title of "paper of record" but speaks for so few of our fellow citizens. Now more than ever, the battle for the future of our nation starts with the restoration of real American journalism, and The New York Sun is devoted to that mission. It truly "shines for all."But I know what the Sun is up against. All the cards are stacked against us.**To win, we need you to join us.** To join The New York Sun is to join the movement to save America. Membership starts at just 1 cent per day.
---

body:
Our Slate of Candidates is almost here.

| | [ ](https://home.forwardparty.com/r?u=JgyyvCfQwldlSeD7KJl_Ih7269o_4hwqOhPs1_PHvgI&e=61868e7acdd36ae0ddf3e39aa8429171&utm_source=forwardparty&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=2025_july_statewide_call&n=1)

| [](https://home.forwardparty.com/r?u=MtBLw57MCBnd28lIl741u6P9-Hx0zs-QPm6VAaSuDh6Op_49yAgxC17idOtsnHUaaiaadxpr1aFNoDHVPxFBzw&e=61868e7acdd36ae0ddf3e39aa8429171&utm_source=forwardparty&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=2025_july_statewide_call&n=2) Join us July 21st at 8pm for our Monthly Statewide call. We've had a busy month. We'll talking to candidates and finalizing endorsements which we look forward to sharing with everyone soon. There are a lot of great people stepping up to serve their communities this year, if you know someone we should be working with, please suggest they reach out for an endorsement. There's still a very short amount of time to get onto the ballot as an Forward Party Candidate. If you are interested in running for Judge of Elections or another hyper-local office, we'd love to talk to you. These races are fundamental to what we're doing. Helping get great local public servants elected helps everyone. Aligning with them helps our party make the relationships we'll rely on when we're building on this foundation. This is a great time to get involved and make a difference. clean_body: Our Slate of Candidates is almost here. | | | Join us July 21st at 8pm for our Monthly Statewide call. We've had a busy month. We'll talking to candidates and finalizing endorsements which we look forward to sharing with everyone soon. There are a lot of great people stepping up to serve their communities this year, if you know someone we should be working with, please suggest they reach out for an endorsement. There's still a very short amount of time to get onto the ballot as an Forward Party Candidate. If you are interested in running for Judge of Elections or another hyper-local office, we'd love to talk to you. These races are fundamental to what we're doing. Helping get great local public servants elected helps everyone. Aligning with them helps our party make the relationships we'll rely on when we're building on this foundation. This is a great time to get involved and make a difference. Click below to see more** And Join in a conversation with other PA Forward Supporters on Slack! Pennsylvania Forward Party Copyright © 2025 Forward Party®, all rights reserved. name: Adam Schiff
subject: Trump just called for my prosecution — here’s why I won’t back down
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Torres For Congress PO Box 580303, Bronx, NY 10458
body:
| Hi Peter — this is Adam Schiff. Ritchie said I could reach out with this urgent message:

**Donald Trump just went on Truth Social and called for me to be prosecuted.**

Why? Because I led his first impeachment. Because I’ve held him accountable. Because I haven’t stopped. And I never will. This is just Trump’s latest attempt at political retaliation against his perceived enemies. name: Katie Porter
subject: This is just cruel
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Katie Porter for Governor 2026
body:
# [](https://act.katieporter.com/go/21486?t=1&akid=39906%2E3422442%2EYxCjPy)
Dear Peter,
Yesterday, the Trump Administration officially shuttered the dedicated LGBTQ+ youth suicide hotline.
**This cruel policy change will lead to young people losing their lives.**
We know that LGBTQ+ youth are at significantly higher risk for suicide attempts compared to their peers, and we know that the LGBTQ+ youth hotline actually worked to help kids in crisis.
Our leaders should be empowering every American child to have happy, healthy lives. Once again, Donald Trump is doing the opposite.
**As Governor, I will always stand with the LGBTQ+ community and defend LGBTQ+ Californians against extremists’ attempts to attack or scapegoat them.**
—Katie

Katie is running to be California’s next governor because California and our country need a leader who will stand up to Trump to lower costs, protect our rights, and combat climate change. **[Donate ›››](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kp4g-em-footer?refcode=em20250718-39906&amounts=&t=3&refcode2=39906_3422442_YxCjPy&akid=39906%2E3422442%2EYxCjPy)**

Peter — I hope this clears things up: Republicans and their billionaire donors are using DEI in place of _much more offensive_ terms to disparage people of color, women, and queer people. **This is beyond wrong, and it's harming people from all walks of life.** When the most powerful people in our country talk down to marginalized groups, the most extreme among their supporters are emboldened to do the same — if not much worse. Their dog whistling is a complete and utter distraction from the fact that Trump has _no plans_ to provide any sensible leadership as our nation's president. [**I'm urging Republicans to drop their obsession with DEI, stop scapegoating and attacking helpful programs that increase accessibility for everyone, and focus on pressing issues like disaster relief and lowering costs.
